PRIME Rural › Overview
PR-OV-001

Welcome to the PRIME Rural Living BRD

This is the single source of truth for the PRIME Rural Fellowship Platform. It covers every user journey, every form field, every validation rule, and every test case needed to build and verify the portal. Use the sidebar to navigate, the role filter to see what each user sees, and the review panels at the bottom of each section to track approvals.
11
Modules
27
Frameworks
6
User Roles
5
Dashboards
How to review this document: Each section has a review panel at the bottom. Set the status to "In Review" when reading, add comments for feedback, and mark "Approved" when satisfied. Use "Export Comments" to download all feedback as CSV for review meetings.
What changed: This BRD reflects the latest requirement update from the client. Financial Bookkeeping, Shelving, Cataloguing, Design Experiment, and Design Thinking frameworks have been removed. Tasks workspace is replaced by Fellow Activity Logger. Infrastructure is now a single combined framework (BE + EE + CE).
Section Review:
PR-OV-002

System Architecture

The platform has two delivery channels powered by a shared Frappe v16 backend.

Flutter Mobile App

  • Offline-first data capture
  • Field user (Fessociate) focused
  • Auto-sync when online
  • Google SSO authentication (single sign-on, no password / OTP)
⇆

Frappe v16 Backend

  • 194 DocTypes across 4 modules
  • REST API + whitelisted endpoints
  • Role-based permissions
  • Scheduled jobs & webhooks
  • Apoorva AI chatbot integration
⇆

Vue.js Web CRM

  • Single-page application
  • Monsoon Court design system
  • 45+ Vue components
  • Dashboards, forms, reports
  • Email ID + password or Google SSO login
Technology Stack
LayerTechnologyVersionNotes
BackendFrappe Frameworkv15/v16Custom app, NOT ERPNext
FrontendVue.js 33.xSPA with Monsoon Court design system
StylingTailwind CSS + Monsoon Court tokens--Georgia serif, teal/terracotta/cream palette
MobileFlutter--Offline-first with background sync
Auth — MobileGoogle SSO--Single sign-on only; no password / no OTP on Flutter app
Auth — Web CRMEmail ID + password or Google SSO--Two parallel options; both land on the same Frappe session
AI ChatbotApoorva AI--Client's proprietary LLM
DatabaseMariaDB--Via Frappe ORM
Section Review:
PR-OV-002b

Network Architecture

Every external service, third-party platform, and internal node that the PRIME Rural CRM and mobile app depend on at runtime. This is the end-of-day deployment surface that must all be reachable, monitored and provisioned for the product to be functional in production.

Service Inventory

Layer Service / Component Provider / Tech Purpose Failure Mode if Unreachable
AuthenticationGoogle SSO (OAuth 2.0)Google IdentitySign-in for mobile (only) and web (optional)Mobile app cannot log in at all; web CRM falls back to email + password
AuthenticationFrappe Auth (email + password)FrappeWeb CRM password loginWeb CRM login broken (Google SSO still works)
ApplicationWeb CRM (Vue 3 SPA + Frappe v15/v16)Dhwani-hosted (staging: stgprime-rural.dhwaniris.in)Primary application for core team, designer, SELCO, DCFull system outage
ApplicationFlutter Mobile AppDhwani build, Android (Play Store / sideload)Fessociate field data capture, offline-firstNo new field capture; existing offline buffer holds until reconnect
DatabaseMariaDBVia Frappe ORMAll transactional + master dataTotal outage
File storageFrappe File doctype on application volumeLocal FS / object storePhoto uploads, attachments, exportsUploads fail; existing files cached client-side
SyncFrappe REST API + whitelisted endpointsFrappeMobile ↔ backend sync, web data fetchesMobile cannot sync; web cannot load lists
AI ChatbotApoorva AIClient (MBMA) proprietary LLMIn-app chatbot helper for usersChatbot disabled; rest of system unaffected
Map tilesOpenStreetMap / Leaflet tilesOSM / chosen tile providerEntrepreneurial Landscape map, village geo-pinsMap renders blank; pin overlays still work
EmailSMTP relayProvider TBD (e.g. Postmark, SES, Google Workspace SMTP)Welcome email on user creation; notification emailsNo outbound email; in-app notifications still work
Notifications — in-appFrappe Notification Log + websocketFrappeReal-time bell-icon alertsNotifications delayed until next poll
Notifications — mobile push (if used)Firebase Cloud MessagingGoogle FCMMobile push for assigned tasks / mentionsNo push delivery; in-app notifications still work
HostingApplication serverCloud VM (provider TBD — AWS / GCP / Azure)Frappe app + MariaDB + RedisTotal outage
Cache / QueueRedisFrappe stackBackground jobs, real-time, cacheSlow responses; background jobs (emails, syncs) queued
CDN / StaticStatic asset hostSame origin or external CDNVue bundle, fonts, iconsUI loads slowly; degrades to cached version
MCP server (internal tooling)Prime Rural MCPGoogle Cloud RunAI tool surface for Claude / internal teamAI tooling unavailable; product unaffected for end users
Source controlGitHubgithub.com/dhwani-ris/frappe_prime_ruralCode, deployments, CINo new deploys; runtime unaffected
ObservabilityApplication logs + uptime monitorProvider TBD (e.g. CloudWatch, Grafana, Better Uptime)Operational visibilityBlind to outages until users report
Provisioning checklist: Before go-live, each row above must have a confirmed provider, credentials in a secret manager, monitoring/alerts wired, and a runbook entry for failure recovery. Items marked TBD need a decision from the delivery team.
Notably not in scope: MSG91 OTP (previously planned) has been removed — mobile auth is Google SSO only; web auth is email/password or Google SSO. Third-party payment gateways are not in scope; all disbursements are recorded post-facto via the Infrastructure Disbursement doctype.
Section Review:
PR-OV-003

Module & Framework Map

The portal is organized into 13 modules containing 27 diagnostic frameworks, plus the standalone Entrepreneur Progress Report. Each framework is a form that field users fill for entrepreneurs. Source of truth: Module Master Google Sheet.
Entrepreneur Progress Report
Entrepreneur Progress Report (standalone, not under a module)
Tabs: Entrepreneur Progress Report
Product
Product Profile
Tabs: Product Details, Process Flow, Product Variants, Production Capacity, Resources

Funding

Schemes & Funding
Legal Compliance
Product Compliance
Business Registration
Standard Framework Tab Pattern: Summary › Data Point tabs (Existing / Required, or framework-specific tabs) › Log Book › Resources. Exceptions: Entrepreneur Progress Report, Product Profile and Financials do not have a Summary tab; Business Plan Canvas uses a single Canvas tab; Infrastructure consolidates BE / EE / CE as three tabs inside one framework. The Task tab has been removed across all frameworks — activity logging is handled via the Fellow Activity Logger.
Renames from previous BRD: Upskilling → Skilling · Marketing → Market · Financial Bookkeeping → Financials (under new Accounts module) · Registration & Licensing → Business Registration (moved to new Legal Compliance module). New module added: Legal Compliance (Product Compliance + Business Registration). New module added: Product (Product Profile separated as its own module).

PR-OV-004

Section Review:
PR-OV-005

Entrepreneur Lifecycle

Every entrepreneur in the system follows a 3-state status model. There is no "Rejected" or "Pending" state. The final status (Incubated / Supported) is set only by Core Team IT via a dropdown on the entrepreneur record, after Selection & Evaluation is complete.
MET Any saved profile
→
Incubated Set by Core Team IT post-selection
or
Supported Set by Core Team IT post-selection
Lifecycle Flow
Fellow meets entrepreneur
On ground
→
Profile created on CRM
Status = MET · Fellow fills 10Q + Rural E-Champ score during profiling
→
Selection & Evaluation
Pitching + scoring review by Core Team
→
Core Team IT sets final status
Incubated or Supported (dropdown on EP record)
→
Framework Selection & Prioritisation
In EP Profile form · by Core Team IT or Fellow
→
Diagnostic Assessment
Framework forms filled in priority order
Products can be added to the profile at any point from Profile created onward (independent of selection), since several frameworks reference the product catalogue (Golden Thread).
Prioritisation logic: Once an entrepreneur is marked Incubated / Supported, the EP Profile form exposes a Framework Selection & Prioritisation section. All 27 frameworks are listed with priority = 0 by default. Core Team IT or the Fellow can flip a framework to "Applicable" and assign a priority number. Dashboard cards reorder automatically by priority (lower number = higher priority). Diagnostic assessment proceeds in this order.
Section Review:
PR-OV-006

General Rules (All Forms)

These rules apply to every diagnostic framework form across all modules. Developers must implement these consistently.
Rule 1 — Calculated Fields
Calculated fields must be visually distinct from input fields (italic text, cream-deep background). Each must show its formula in a clean, user-friendly tooltip so field users understand how the value is derived.
Rule 2 — Fetched Fields
Fields auto-fetched from another form must display "Fetched from [X] form" with a clickable redirect link to the source field. The user should be able to see and edit the source if needed.
Rule 3 — Intervention Banner
In the Existing tab, rows with intervention need show the intervention flag in the row banner. In the Required tab, corresponding rows show implementation status in the row banner.
Rule 4 — Product Filters
For all product-level frameworks, show product button filters so users can toggle between products of the entrepreneur. New products added to the portfolio auto-appear in this filter.
Rule 5 — Summary Tab
Summary tab content is generated only after the form is saved for the first time. Before first save, the summary tab shows an empty state with a prompt to save.
Rule 6 — Intervention Details Tab
Intervention Details is its own tab in every framework form — not a section inside the Required tab. It contains Pre-Intervention Comments and Post-Intervention Comments text boxes plus any other intervention-level metadata. Sitting at the framework level (not the row level) means it captures the overall intervention narrative for that entrepreneur in that framework.
Rule 7 — Task Tab Removed
The Task tab shown in some HTML mockups has been removed. Activity logging is handled via the Fellow Activity Logger workspace instead. Framework forms only have the Log Book tab for logging.
Rule 8 — Log Book Structure (per-framework scope)
Every framework has its own Log Book tab. Columns: Date, Fessociate (autofill), Entrepreneur (autofill), Task (dropdown from Framework Task Master, filtered to that framework only), Upload / View (photo), Comments. Scope: the Log Book for framework X shows only activities logged against framework X — whether the activity was added directly inside that framework's Log Book (with framework/entrepreneur prefilled) or created via the Fellow Activity Logger workspace. Activities from other frameworks never appear here. Activity Logger entries also carry through automatically.
Rule 9 — Framework Completion
Profile dashboard shows "Not Started" by default for each framework task. Shows "Completed" once a single activity log exists. If logged again, shows repetition count. Summary popup shows not-started vs completed with percentage.
Rule 10 — Block Restriction
Field users see data from all blocks but can only create/edit for their own assigned block. District and block fields auto-fill from logged-in user's profile when creating new records.
Rule 11 — Cohort-Scoped Monthly Trackers
Any monthly tracker shown anywhere in any framework (any tab) must render month-rows for every year spanning the entrepreneur's cohort start date to cohort end date. The cohort is resolved from the entrepreneur's cohort link on the EP Profile; cohort dates are read from the Cohort master. Example: if the cohort runs Apr 2026 → Mar 2029, the tracker shows 36 month-rows grouped by year. Trackers must never be hard-coded to a single year.

Standard Form Design Template

Every framework's field specification follows this 6-column table format. All subsequent module sections use this structure.
Field Name Field Type Input Values Input / Autofill Logic Validation & Error Messages
e.g. Entrepreneur Name e.g. Data (read-only) e.g. Free text / Dropdown options e.g. Autofill from EP Profile e.g. Auto-populated on form load e.g. "Entrepreneur name is required"
e.g. Cost per Batch e.g. Currency (calculated) e.g. Auto: Price/Unit x Qty/Batch e.g. Calculated e.g. Recalculates on Price or Qty change e.g. Must be ≥ 0
Section Review:
PR-OV-007

Cross-Module Data Flow — "The Golden Thread"

Data flows between frameworks. Understanding these connections is critical for development and testing. If a source field changes, all downstream consumers must update.
Primary Data Flow Chains
Product Profile → Raw Materials Ingredients list auto-populates as rows in Existing tab
Product Profile → Process Flow → Raw Materials → Existing Machines marked in process flow under Existing Machinery are added as pre-created rows in the Raw Materials Existing tab (editable, removable)
Product Profile → Process Flow → Machinery → Existing Process flow machines populate Existing tab as pre-created rows (editable)
Raw Materials → Unit Pricing Raw material cost per batch feeds cost-based pricing
Machinery (Required) → Infrastructure → EE Required machines exposed as a dropdown in EE tab — fellow selects machines to add; SELCO pricing is fetched on selection (not auto-pushed)
Infrastructure → BE → Unit Pricing Rent per unit, electricity per unit feed overhead costs
Market Research → Unit Pricing Competitor average prices feed competitor-based pricing
Product Profile → Variants → Labels Variants exposed as a dropdown in label variant content table — fellow selects which variants to add (not auto-prepopulated)
Product Profile → Variants → Packaging Variants exposed as a dropdown in packaging detail table — fellow selects which variants to add (not auto-prepopulated)
Unit Pricing → Product Profile → Variants Retail price auto-fetched back into variant table
Infrastructure → Financial Model → Infrastructure → Summary SELCO/MBMA/LIFCOM/User funding split and EMI
Existing (any framework) → Required (same framework) Intervention bridge: flagged items auto-create linked Required entries
Fellow Activity Logger → Framework Log Books Entrepreneur+framework activities sync to respective log books
Fellow Activity Logger → EP Profile → Intervention Logs All activities reflect as read-only intervention logs on profile
Village Survey → Geo Location → Entrepreneurial Landscape Map Village coordinates render on the map
TDD Implication: Every data flow arrow above generates at least 3 test cases: (1) source data correctly populates target on load, (2) source data change propagates to target on refresh/save, (3) deleting source gracefully handles target (no orphan data, no crash).
Section Review:
PR-UJ-001

Journey: Core Team IT

The Core Team IT has full administrative access to every workspace, every form, and every master. They are the system administrators who configure the portal, manage users, create milestones, and maintain master data.

What Core Team IT Does

Primary Actions
User creation, role assignment, master data management
Secondary Actions
Create milestones, create activities inside milestones, then manage both milestones and activities; data exports
Framework Prioritisations
For each Incubated / Supported entrepreneur, mark applicable frameworks and assign priority numbers in the EP Profile form. Can also be done by Fellow.
Unique Capability
Set the final Incubated / Supported status (only role that can); only role with full User Management + Masters access

Journey 1: User Management

Login as Admin
Email + password or Google SSO
→
Open User Management
Left menu → User Management
→
Click "Add User"
Top right button
→
Fill user form
Name, email, role, block assignment
→
Assign Role
Select from 6 roles
→
Save & Trigger Email
Welcome email with set-password link
Welcome Email: On user creation, a custom-template email fires automatically with: set-password link, role-based quick pointers explaining expected actions, and a warm welcome message. The user then sets their password and logs in via (a) email + password, or (b) Google SSO if the email is a Gmail address.

Journey 2: Milestone & Activity Management

Open Milestone Tracker
Left menu
→
Add Milestone
Create Milestone Master record
→
Add Activities
Milestone Activity records inside milestone
→
Track Progress
Overview, Activities, Timeline, RACI tabs

Journey 3: Entrepreneur Selection & Prioritisation

Open EP Profile
Selection tab
→
Review Fellow's inputs
10-question rating + Rural E-Champ score — both filled by Fellow during profiling, not by Core Team IT
→
Pitching review
Review pitching attachment / round outcome
→
Final Selection (CTIT only)
Set status: Incubated or Supported via dropdown
→
Framework Prioritisation
Mark applicable frameworks & set priority numbers · can be done by CTIT or Fellow
Who does what:
· Fellow — fills the 10-question rating and the Rural E-Champ score during profiling. Both inputs land on the EP Profile and are visible to Core Team.
· Core Team IT — reviews Fellow's inputs + pitching outcome, then marks Incubated or Supported via the final status dropdown. Only CTIT can set this final status.
· Framework Prioritisation — happens inside the EP Profile form after final status is set. All 27 frameworks listed with priority = 0 by default. Either CTIT or the Fellow can flip a framework to Applicable and assign a priority number. Lower number = higher priority. Dashboard cards reorder automatically.

PR-UJ-002

Core Team Program has near-identical access to Core Team IT except for User Management (create + edit only, no delete) and Masters (view + edit, no create). Their primary focus is programme delivery: selection, evaluation, monitoring, and approvals.

CapabilityCore Team ITCore Team Program

MastersFull CRUDView + Edit (no create)

Approval capability: All "Approved by Core Team" fields across all frameworks can be filled by Core Team Program or Core Team IT users. This includes intervention approvals, design draft approvals, testing consent, etc.
Section Review:
PR-UJ-003

Journey: Fessociate (Field User)

The Fessociate is the primary data entry user — a block-level field fellow who meets entrepreneurs on ground, captures their profiles, adds products, fills diagnostic frameworks, and logs activities. This is the longest and most critical user journey.
Block restriction: A Fessociate sees data from all blocks (read) but can only create and edit entrepreneurs, products, and frameworks for their own assigned block. District and block auto-fill from the logged-in user's profile.

The Complete Fessociate Journey (7 phases)

Phase 1: Login & Landing ▼
Set password
From welcome email link
→
Login
Email + password or Google SSO
→
Entrepreneurial Landscape
Landing page — map with all entrepreneurs
Landscape Map Filters: District, Block, Cohort, Sector, Business Basket, Product Name. Map shows pins for each entrepreneur. Click a pin to see summary and navigate to profile.
Phase 2: Add Entrepreneur ▼
Left menu → Entrepreneur
Opens sub-workspaces
→
Entrepreneur List
See all entrepreneurs in CRM
→
Click "Add Entrepreneur"
Top right button
→
Fill EP Profile form
See PR-EP-001 for full spec
→
Save
Status = MET. Dashboard populates.

Entrepreneur Profile Tabs (as filled by Fessociate)

TabWhat the Fessociate fillsKey Fields
ProfileBasic info + 10 field evaluation ratingsName, Gender, Phone, Email, DOB, Education, Family Size, Marital Status, Income, Position, Sector, Enterprise Stage, Business Progress, Market Accessibility + 10 rating fields (1-10)
Location & ContactAddress + geolocationDistrict (auto), Block (auto), Village, Residence Address, Work Address, Geolocation pin
DocumentsUpload supporting docsDocument table (child), Profile Picture, Uploads
SelectionField evaluation is filled here (or by Core Team)10 rating parameters, avg rating (calc), Rural E-Champ Score, Attachment, Final Selection Status (Incubated/Supported)
PrioritisationSet framework prioritiesPriority Table (child) — all frameworks listed, priority 0 by default
Networking & MentoringAdd introductions, log meetingsIntroductions (child table), Meeting logs inline
Intervention LogsRead-only — auto-populated from Fellow Activity LoggerActivity logs synced from logger
Profile DashboardView only — auto-generatedProduct photo cards, framework completion %, intervention summary
Phase 3: Add Products ▼
Left menu → Products
Product workspace
→
Add Product for entrepreneur
See PR-PRD-001 for full spec
→
Fill 4 tabs
Details, Process Flow, Variants, Capacity
→
Save
Product card appears on EP Profile Dashboard
Data cascades on save: Raw materials from Product Profile auto-fill the Raw Materials framework existing tab. Machines from Process Flow auto-fill the Machinery framework existing tab. All products show as clickable photo cards on the Entrepreneur Profile Dashboard.
Phase 4: Fill Diagnostic Frameworks ▼
Left menu → Diagnostics
Opens module dropdown
→
Select Module
e.g. Manufacturing
→
Select Framework
e.g. Raw Materials
→
List view
All entered forms for entrepreneurs
→
Add new / Open existing
Fill framework form
Navigation hierarchy: Diagnostics → Module (Manufacturing, Marketing Tools, Pricing, Market, Product Development, Skilling, Logistics, Infrastructure, Funding) → Framework (e.g. Raw Materials, Machinery, HR). Each module is clickable to reveal its frameworks. Each framework opens in list view showing entries per entrepreneur.
Alternative entry: The Fessociate can also open a framework form directly from the Entrepreneur Profile Dashboard by clicking a framework card. This takes them to the specific framework form for that entrepreneur.

Diagnostics Left Menu Structure

Source of truth: Module Master Google Sheet. 13 modules · 27 frameworks (in addition to the standalone Entrepreneur Progress Report).
Module (Level 1)Frameworks (Level 2)
ProductProduct Profile
AccountsFinancials
ManufacturingRaw Materials, Machinery, Human Resource
InfrastructureInfrastructure (single framework with Summary · BE · EE · CE · Log Book · Resources tabs — dashboard view first, then form)

Phase 5: Log Activities (Fellow Activity Logger) ▼
Left menu → Fellow Activity Logger
Default: Calendar view
→
Toggle to Board view
Top right toggle
→
Click "Add Activity"
Top right button
→
Fill activity form
See branching logic below

View Behaviour

Calendar View (default)
All activities the fellow has logged render as tiles on the date they were logged for. Multi-activity days show stacked chips. Click a date to expand its activities inline.
Board View (block-wise card view)
Toggled from the top-right view switch. Activities are grouped block-wise as cards (per the HTML wireframe). Within each block group, cards are sorted in descending order of logging date — latest activities at the top. Each card shows entrepreneur, framework / non-framework, task, comments preview, photo thumb.

Activity Form Branching Logic

Step 1: Date (default = today, editable)
Step 2: Activity Type → Entrepreneur or Non-Entrepreneur

If Entrepreneur:
  → Sub-type: Framework Activity or Non-Framework Activity
  → If Framework: Select Entrepreneur (single) → Select Framework (single) → Select Framework Task (single, from master)
  → Comments + Photo upload/view
  → On submit: Activity reflects in calendar tile + syncs to framework Log Book + syncs to EP Profile Intervention Logs (read-only)

If Non-Entrepreneur:
  → Activity Details (text) + Comments + Photo upload/view
  → On submit: Activity reflects in calendar tile only

Activity Logger Filters

FilterTypeBehaviour
DistrictMulti-selectCascading — filters block options
BlockMulti-selectFiltered by selected district(s)
EntrepreneurSingle selectShows entrepreneurs in selected blocks
ModuleSingle selectFilters framework dropdown
FrameworkSingle selectFiltered by selected module
Framework TasksSingle selectFiltered by selected framework (from master)
Framework completion tracking: On the EP Profile Dashboard, each framework task shows "Not Started" by default. Once a single activity log exists for that task, it shows "Completed". If logged again, it shows a repetition count (e.g. "Completed x2"). The summary popup shows not-started vs completed count with a percentage bar.
Phase 6: Research Notes & Village Survey ▼
Research Notes: Left menu → Research Notes workspace. Fessociate fills the form following the standard form design template (6-column field spec). Notes capture observations, insights, and qualitative data from field visits.

Village Survey: Left menu → Village Survey workspace. Fessociate fills the village survey form with geo-location tagging. Geo-tagged locations are used to plot villages on the Entrepreneurial Landscape map.
Phase 7: Networking & Mentoring ▼
Open EP Profile
Networking & Mentoring tab
→
Add Introduction
Inline form opens
→
Select Mentor
From Mentor Master (read-only for Fessociate)
→
Fill details
Introduced by, date, objective
→
Log Meetings
Inline under each introduction card
See PR-EP-004 Networking & Mentoring for full field spec.
Section Review:
PR-UJ-004

Journey: Brand Designer

The Designer has the most restricted view. They only see Marketing Tools frameworks (Branding Identity, Labels, Brochure) and are responsible for uploading design drafts for review and approval.
Login
Designer credentials
→
Left menu: Diagnostics → Marketing Tools
Only module visible
→
Select framework
Labels, Brand Identity, or Brochure
→
Upload drafts
Draft 1, Draft 2, Draft 3
→
Await approval
Entrepreneur + Core Team dual approval

Designer's Specific Actions per Framework

FrameworkWhat Designer DoesApproval Flow
Branding Identity → LogoUpload up to 3 logo drafts (HD files)Each draft: Pending → Under Review → Approved/Redo. Requires both Entrepreneur Approval + Core Team Approval
Branding Identity → Business CardUpload up to 3 business card draftsSame dual approval flow
LabelsUpload up to 3 label drafts per variantPer-variant status: Pending/Under Review/Redo/Approved/Not Approved + Entrepreneur checkbox
BrochureUpload up to 3 brochure draftsSame per-brochure dual approval
Designer assignment: In each framework's Required tab, there is an "Assign Designer" dropdown (Designer A, Designer B, AI Generated). The designer sees only forms where they are assigned.
Section Review:
PR-UJ-005

Journey: SELCO Executive

SELCO Executive has a single-purpose role: updating energy-efficient machinery (EE) pricing and implementation status in the Infrastructure module.
Login
SELCO credentials
→
Infrastructure → EE Machinery
Only visible workspace
→
View required machines
Auto-populated from Machinery module
→
Update SELCO Price
Per machine pricing
→
Approve for solarisation
Checkbox: "Approved by SELCO"
→
Upload quotations
BOM and quotation log
Data flow: Machines in EE tab are auto-populated from the Machinery module's Required tab. SELCO pricing updates feed into the Infrastructure Financial Model for funding slab calculations.

PR-UJ-006

District Coordinators are view-only observers. They can see all data across all workspaces but have zero edit access. Their role is monitoring and oversight.

Section Review:
PR-EP-001

Entrepreneur Profile Form

The Entrepreneur Profile (DocType: Enterprenur Profile, autoname EP-.{enterprenur_name}-.#####) is the root record of the entire system. Every product, framework form, activity log, introduction and selection decision hangs off one EP record. It is the first thing a Fessociate creates on ground and the object Core Team IT acts on at selection time. 139 fields across 8 tabs.
Status anchor: Saving any profile sets status = MET. The Final Selection dropdown (Incubated / Supported) on the Selection tab is editable only by Core Team IT. The 10-question rating and Rural E-Champ score are filled by the Fellow during profiling — not Core Team. See Entrepreneur Lifecycle.

On-Screen UI Structure

Top bar: Entrepreneur name + EP code (left), status chip (MET / Incubated / Supported) + Active/Inactive toggle (right), Save / New buttons.
Left: sticky vertical tab rail with the 8 tabs.
Body: the active tab. Form tabs use the standard two-column Frappe section/column-break layout; calculated fields (e.g. Average Rating) render italic on cream-deep background with a formula tooltip (General Rule 1).
Profile Dashboard tab is a read-only visual canvas (KPI cards + product photo cards + framework-completion grid), not a form.
Profile
→
Location & Contact
→
Documents
→
Prioritisation
→
Selection
→
Connections
→
Profile Dashboard
→
Intervention Logs

User Journey

Fellow: Add Entrepreneur
Entrepreneur list → top-right button
→
Fill Profile + Location + Docs
District/Block auto from user
→
Fellow rates 10 parameters + Rural E-Champ
During profiling (Selection tab)
→
Save → Status MET
Dashboard generates
→
Core Team IT: set Final Selection
Incubated / Supported (CTIT only)
→
Framework Selection & Prioritisation
CTIT or Fellow
Tab 1: Profile ▼

Section I: About Entrepreneur

FieldTypeInput ValuesLogicValidation

18 yes/no support flags. Each ticked checkbox reveals a dependent Note text box (depends_on). These feed the Prioritisation tab's applicable-framework list.
Support Flag (Check)Reveals on tick

Tab 6: Connections (Networking & Mentoring) ▼
Full spec at PR-EP-004 Networking & Mentoring. Inline Add-Introduction form + per-introduction meeting logs.
FieldTypeLogicValidation
IntroductionsTable (Entrepreneur Introduction)Child rows: mentor (from Mentor Master, read-only for Fellow), introduced by, date, objective, meeting statusMentor + objective required per row
Networking ComponentHTMLInline meeting-log panel per introduction; auto-syncs meeting_count, last/next meeting date, latest outcome to parent--
Tab 7: Profile Dashboard (read-only) ▼
Auto-generated after first save (General Rule 5). Rendered from HTML fields (information_header, cards, basic_business, diagnostic_framework).
Information Header
Name, photo, status chip, block, sector, cohort, contact quick-glance.
Product Photo Cards + Add New
All products as clickable photo cards → open Product Profile. Trailing "+ Add New Product" tile opens a fresh Product doctype prefilled with this entrepreneur. (See PR-DB-005.)
Basic Business
Enterprise snapshot — stage, market access, progress, avg rating, Rural E-Champ score.
Diagnostic Framework Grid
Per applicable framework (priority order): Not Started → Completed → Completed xN. Summary popup shows % completion (General Rule 9).
Tab 8: Intervention Logs (read-only) ▼
FieldTypeLogicValidation
LogsTable (Task Logs)Read-only mirror of all Fellow Activity Logger entries for this entrepreneur (framework + non-framework). Synced automatically. See Activity LoggerSystem-populated
Visit Date / Intervention DateDateMost recent visit / intervention stamps≤ today
Key validations (whole form): Entrepreneur Name + Phone + District + Block required to save (sets MET). District/Block auto-fill and gate edit rights to the user's own block (Rule 10). Final Selection writable by CTIT only. Cohort required for monthly trackers to render across cohort years (Rule 11). Phone must be a valid 10-digit number; Email valid format; DOB & business start date ≤ today.

PR-PRD-001

Product Profile

The Product Profile is the anchor form of the system. It defines a product's identity, variants, production process, and capacity. Data from this form cascades to Raw Materials, Machinery, Unit Pricing, Labels, Packaging, and the Monthly Production Tracker.
Golden Thread origin: This form is where the data cascade begins. Ingredients feed Raw Materials. Process Flow machines feed Machinery. Variants feed Labels + Packaging. Available Months + Variants feed the Production Tracker. Retail Price is fetched BACK from Unit Pricing.

Tab Structure

Product Details
→
Product Variants
→
Process Flow
→
Production Capacity
Tab 1: Product Details ▼

Section I: Product Identity

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Product StatusSelectNew, ExistingInput--Required. "Please select product status"
SectorSelectAgro & Allied, Food Processing, Handloom & Handicraft, Wellness, Personal Care, OthersInput--Required. "Please select sector"
Business BasketSelectPickles & Preserves, Teas & Beverages, Spices & Herbs, Bakery & Snacks, Earrings & Jewellery, Home Decor, Mufflers & Stoles, OthersInput--Required. "Please select business basket"
Product TypeSelectProcessed Food, Beverage, Handicraft, Handloom, Spices & Herbs, Personal Care, OthersInput--Required
Product NameLink (Product Master)Search & selectFetched from Product MasterAutocomplete search on typeRequired. Must exist in Product Master
Entrepreneur NameLink (EP Profile)Search & selectInputAuto-populates entrepreneur contextRequired
Product SnapshotTextareaFree textInput----
Product ImageAttach ImageFile upload (.jpg, .png, .pdf)InputAttach + View buttons--
IngredientsRead-only pills--Autofill from Raw Material FrameworkDisplays as pill chips. To add new, edit Product form → add raw material thereRead-only. "Fetched from Raw Material Framework"
USPTextareaFree textInputUnique selling proposition--
Shelf Life TestedSelectYes, NoInput----
Shelf Life DurationDatae.g. "12 months"InputIndependent of Available Months--

Section II: Available Months

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Jan through Dec (12 checkboxes)Checkbox tableTRUE / FALSE per monthInput (click to toggle)Checked months appear in Monthly Production Tracker (Tab 4). State label shows TRUE/FALSE dynamically.At least 1 month must be selected before Production Tracker can generate
Tab 2: Product Variants (Child Table) ▼
ColumnTypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation
NoInt (auto)SequentialAuto-incrementRow number--
SKUData (auto)--Calculated: VARIANT-SUBVARIANT-WEIGHT-UNITAuto-generates on variant name/sub-variant/weight/unit changeRead-only
Variant StatusSelectExisting, NewInput--Required
Variant NameDataFree textInputFeeds SKU generationRequired
Sub VariantDataFree textInputFeeds SKU generation--
Net WeightFloatNumericInputFeeds SKU generationMust be > 0
UnitSelectg, kg, ml, L, pcs, bundleInputFeeds SKU generationRequired
Dimensions L x B x H3x Float + SelectNumeric + unit (mm, cm, m, in)InputThree separate inputs + unit selectorAll 3 required if any entered
VolumeFloat (auto)--Calculated: L x B x H + unit cubedRecalculates on dimension changeRead-only
Retail PriceCurrency (auto)--Autofill from Unit Pricing module"Fetched from Pricing module" hint shownRead-only. Shows as placeholder until pricing filled
ImageAttach ImageFile uploadInputPer-variant image--
DeleteButton----Removes variant rowConfirm before delete if variant has downstream data
Tab 3: Process Flow (Drag-and-Drop Steps) ▼
Steps are drag-and-drop reorderable cards. Each step links to a machine from the Machinery module. Default 6 steps seeded on form creation: Ingredient Preparation, Mixing/Blending, Cooking/Processing, Cooling/Setting, Packaging, Labelling & Packaging.
FieldTypeValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation
Step NumberInt (auto)SequentialAuto-numbered, updates on drag reorderCircular teal badge--
Step DetailDataFree textInputDescribes the process stepRequired
Machine UsedLink (Machinery)From Machinery moduleInput (select)Populates Machinery Existing tab--
Machine StatusSelectExisting, RequiredInputIf "Required" → flags for Machinery interventionRequired if machine selected
Implemented?SelectYes, NoInput----
StatusSelectPending, In Progress, DoneInputColor-coded chip--
Actions: + Add Step (bottom), Remove (per step), Drag handle (reorder)

Section I: Agricultural & Raw Material Context

Conditional: This entire section is shown only when "Is it an Agri By-Product?" = Yes. If No, the section is greyed out / hidden.
FieldTypeValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation
Is it an Agri By-Product?SelectYes, NoInputIf Yes: show agri fields below. If No: hide/grey-outRequired
Crop Being GrownDatae.g. Lemongrass, BambooInputShown only if Agri = YesRequired if Agri = Yes
Land OwnershipSelectOwn Land, Leased, Community/Collective, Sourced from FarmersInputShown only if Agri = YesRequired if Agri = Yes
Area Covered (Acres)FloatNumeric (step 0.01)InputShown only if Agri = YesMust be > 0
Production Capacity from AreaDataNumeric textInput----
Production UnitSelectKg, Tonnes, Litres, Pieces, BundlesInput----
Sowing SeasonBool month grid (Jan-Dec)Toggle on/off per monthInputClickable month cells, teal when ON--
Harvesting SeasonBool month gridToggle on/offInputSame pattern--
Processing SeasonBool month gridToggle on/offInputSame pattern--

Section II: Production Capacity

FieldTypeValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation
Existing Capacity / MonthIntNumericInput--Must be ≥ 0
Aspiring Capacity / MonthIntNumericInput--Must be ≥ 0
UnitsSelectKg, g, Litres, ml, Pieces, Bottles, Pouches, BundlesFetched from Unit Master--Required
