SeasonBool month gridToggle monthsInputShown only if Seasonal = Yes--
Suggested Product DevelopmentTextareaFree textInput----

Section III: Monthly Production Capacity Tracker

Fully dynamic table. Months auto-populate from Available Months (Tab 1). Variants and selling prices auto-populate from Product Variants (Tab 2). Per cell: Qty is editable, Selling Price is read-only (from variants), Revenue = Qty x Price (auto-calc). Row totals and annual totals auto-calculate.
ColumnTypeLogic
MonthRow label (auto)Only months checked in Available Months appear
Per variant: QtyInt (input)Editable quantity per month per variant
Per variant: Selling PriceCurrency (read-only)Auto from Variant table Retail Price
Per variant: RevenueCurrency (calc)= Qty x Selling Price
Total Monthly ProductionInt (calc)Sum of all variant Qty for that month
Total Monthly RevenueCurrency (calc)Sum of all variant Revenue for that month
Annual Total (footer)Currency (calc)Sum of all months' totals
Section Review:
PR-MFG-001

Raw Materials

Captures every raw material a manufacturing entrepreneur uses — its identity, supply/procurement profile, batch-level cost build-up and the resulting Raw Material Cost / Unit — and records the intervention work (alternate sources, alternate materials, new materials) needed to optimise it. This is the first framework in the Manufacturing module.
Golden Thread. Product names flow in from the entrepreneur's Product Profile (read-only per Rule 2). Each material's calculated RM Cost / Unit and Total Landed Cost / Unit feed Unit Pricing individually — Existing rows feed Existing Unit Pricing, Required (optimised) rows feed Optimised Unit Pricing. Toggling an intervention flag in the Existing tab auto-creates a linked row in the Required tab (intervention bridge, Rule 3); removing the Required row clears the flag and vice-versa.

On-Screen UI Structure

A teal title bar reads Raw Material Module with crumb PRIME Rural · DF Raw Material. Directly below sits a horizontal tab rail — Summary · Existing · Required · Resources · Task Log — the active tab highlighted teal. An entrepreneur chip ("Auto-fetched from profile") sits at the top of every panel.

Summary is a read-only roll-up: a material-wise overview table (one row per material, two right-most columns shaded terracotta/green for New Price & Saving), four aggregate cards (Materials / Cost / Interventions / Status) and a four-tile Intervention Flags strip.

Existing renders each material as a collapsible block (sea-green header carrying inline Product + Raw-Material name inputs and auto Middleman / Essential tags) holding four sub-sections: Material Identity, Supply & Procurement, Cost & Calculations Inputs, and a Calculated strip plus a monospace Calculation Summary. A terracotta Intervention Need banner with two toggle cells closes each block. A teal total bar sums all blocks.

Required has three numbered sections (I Alternate Sources, II Alternate Materials, III New Raw Material). Sections I & II are populated by the intervention bridge — pre-created rows show the originating Product / Raw Material / Justification as read-only (italic on cream-deep), then editable optimisation fields and a terracotta Implementation Status block. Section III rows are added freely.

Calculated fields (Amount Per Batch, RM Qty / Unit, RM Cost / Unit, Transport / Unit, Total Landed Cost / Unit, Expected Saving / Unit) render italic on cream-deep background and are never editable; each carries a formula tooltip (Rule 1).
Summary
→
Existing
→
Required
→
Resources
→
Task Log

User Journey

Open Raw Materials
Fessociate · Diagnostics → Manufacturing (or EP dashboard card)
→
Add & record Existing material
identity, supply, batch cost inputs
→
Review calculated RM Cost / Unit
auto in Calculated strip
→
Toggle intervention flag
Existing tab — auto-creates Required row
→
Research alternate & set status
Required tab; add New materials
→
Log activity & Save
Task Log; Summary & Unit Pricing update
Tab 1: Summary ▼

Entirely read-only. Auto-populated from the Existing and Required tabs — no direct data entry. Recalculates on every tab switch back to Summary and on any change in Existing/Required.

Material-wise Overview Table

Each row feeds Unit Pricing individually. New Price / Unit and Saving populate only when an intervention is in progress / completed.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductRead-only text—AutofillFrom the material's Product Name (Existing block) or, for New materials, the Product / Purpose field.— (display only)
Raw MaterialRead-only text (bold)—AutofillFrom Name of Raw Material.—
SourceRead-only text—AutofillFrom Procurement Source.—
AvailabilityRead-only badgeEasy / Moderate / DifficultAutofillColour map: Easy → good (green), Moderate → fair (amber), Difficult → poor (red). Blank shows “—”.—
EssentialRead-only badgeYes / —AutofillGreen “Yes” badge when Is It Essential? = Yes, else “—”.—
Price / Unit (₹)Read-only currency—AutofillExisting material's Price Per Unit; “—” if 0.—
Intervention TypeRead-only badge + detail↻ Alt. Source / ⇄ Alt. Material / + New Material / —AutofillResolved from the linked Required row (bridge). Source row wins if both flagged. Sub-line shows source name or alternate material name. New-material rows always tagged “+ New Material”.—
Implementation StatusRead-only badgePending / In Progress / Completed / Not Feasible (or On Hold for New)AutofillMirrors the status set on the linked Required row. Completed → green, In Progress → amber, else neutral.—
New Price / Unit (₹) [shaded]Read-only currency—AutofillNew Price Per Unit from the linked Required intervention row; “—” for New-material rows.—
Saving [shaded]Read-only—CalculatedSaving = Old Price/Unit − New Price/Unit. Down-arrow & green when positive, up-arrow & red when negative; “—” for New rows.—
Footer note row: “Unit Pricing reads each material individually — RM Cost / Unit feeds from the Existing tab calculations.” New-material rows are highlighted (cream) and never carry Price / Saving.

Aggregate Cards (4)

Card / MetricField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Materials → Total LoggedRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Existing material blocks.—
Materials → EssentialRead-only number—CalculatedCount where Is It Essential? = Yes.—
Materials → Difficult AvailabilityRead-only number (amber)—CalculatedCount where Availability = Difficult.—
Materials → New AddedRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Required Section III New-material rows.—
Cost → Monthly Spend (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedSum of every material's Amount Per Batch.—
Cost → Price / Unit — Before (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedAverage existing Price Per Unit across all materials carrying an intervention; “—” if none.—
Cost → Price / Unit — After (₹)Read-only currency (green)—CalculatedAverage New Price Per Unit across Completed interventions only; “—” until at least one is Completed.—
Cost → Saving / Unit (₹)Read-only (green/red)—Calculated= avg(Old, Completed) − avg(New, Completed). Down-arrow green if positive, up-arrow red if negative.—
Interventions → Alt. Source FlaggedRead-only number (amber)—CalculatedCount of Existing blocks with the source toggle ON.—
Interventions → Alt. Material FlaggedRead-only number (amber)—CalculatedCount of Existing blocks with the material toggle ON.—
Interventions → New Material NeededRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Section III rows.—
Interventions → TotalRead-only number—CalculatedAlt. Source rows + Alt. Material rows + New rows.—
Status → CompletedRead-only number (green)—CalculatedCount of all Required rows with status = Completed.—
Status → In ProgressRead-only number (amber)—CalculatedCount with status = In Progress.—
Status → PendingRead-only number—CalculatedCount with status = Pending.—
Status → Not FeasibleRead-only number—CalculatedCount with status = Not Feasible.—

Intervention Flags Strip (4 tiles)

TileField TypeInput / AutofillLogic
↻ Alt. Source NeededRead-only count (amber icon)Calculated= Alt. Source flagged count.
⇄ Alt. Material NeededRead-only count (blue icon)Calculated= Alt. Material flagged count.
! Difficult AvailabilityRead-only count (red icon)Calculated= Difficult availability count.
✓ Essential MaterialsRead-only count (green icon)Calculated= Essential count.
Tab 2: Existing ▼

One collapsible block per existing raw material (child table Existing Raw Material). “+ Add Raw Material” appends a block; each block has four sub-sections plus the intervention banner. The collapsible header carries inline Product and Raw-Material name inputs (two-way synced with the body fields) and auto Source / Essential tags.

I. Material Identity

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Product Name *Text (or Link to Product Profile)Free textInput / fetched from Product Profile (read-only when fetched, Rule 2)Two-way synced with the block header Product input and propagated to any linked Required row.“Product Name is required.”
Product SKUTextFree textInput——
Current Status of Use *SelectYes / NoInputWhether the material is currently in use.“Select current status of use.”
Name of Raw Material *TextFree textInputTwo-way synced with the block header Raw-Material input; propagated to linked Required rows.“Raw material name is required.”
Is It Essential? *SelectYes / NoInputYes shows a green “Essential” tag on the header and increments Essential aggregates.“Indicate whether this material is essential.”

II. Supply & Procurement

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
AvailabilitySelectEasy / Moderate / DifficultInputDifficult increments the Difficult-availability flag and the red Summary tile.—
Procurement FrequencySelectDaily / Weekly / Monthly / SeasonalInput——
Procured Season TimeTextFree text (e.g., Oct–Mar)Input——
Procurement SourceSelectMarket / Wholesaler / Middleman / Farm / Forest / Online / OthersInputSelected value shows as a header tag; “Middleman” renders in the darker terracotta variant.—
Source NameTextFree textInput——
Source LocationTextFree textInput——
Source ContactTextPhone / emailInput—Validate phone/email format if entered.

III. Cost & Calculations — Inputs

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Unit *Texte.g., gram, ml, kgInputLabel appended to RM Qty / Unit display.“Unit is required.”
Monthly RequirementNumber≥ 0InputReference quantity; not part of per-unit calc.“Cannot be negative.”
Quantity Required Per Batch *Number≥ 0InputDrives Amount Per Batch and RM Qty / Unit.“Enter quantity per batch.”
Price Per Unit *Currency (₹)≥ 0, 2 decimalsInputDrives Amount Per Batch; carried as the “Old Price” into linked Required rows and the Summary Before column.“Enter price per unit.”
Batch Size *Number> 0InputDivisor for all per-unit calculations — must be > 0 or calculations return 0.“Batch size must be greater than 0.”
Delivery / Transport Cost Per TripCurrency (₹)≥ 0, 2 decimalsInputDrives Transport Cost / Unit.“Cannot be negative.”

IV. Calculated (read-only, italic on cream-deep)

Field NameField TypeInput / AutofillLogic (Formula)
Amount Per BatchCalculated currencyCalculated= Quantity Required Per Batch × Price Per Unit. Summary line: “Amount/Batch = {qty} {unit} × ₹{price} = ₹{result}”.
Raw Material Qty Per UnitCalculated numberCalculated= Quantity Required Per Batch ÷ Batch Size (0 if Batch Size = 0). Displayed with the Unit label.
Raw Material Cost Per UnitCalculated currencyCalculated= Amount Per Batch ÷ Batch Size (0 if Batch Size = 0). This is the value Unit Pricing reads per material.
Transport Cost Per UnitCalculated currencyCalculated= Delivery / Transport Cost Per Trip ÷ Batch Size (0 if Batch Size = 0).
Total Landed Cost Per UnitCalculated currency (highlighted total line)Calculated= Raw Material Cost Per Unit + Transport Cost Per Unit. Shown as “TOTAL LANDED COST/UNIT” in the Calculation Summary.
A monospace Calculation Summary echoes each formula with live values (lines 1–4) and the bold total line, so the entrepreneur can see exactly how the per-unit cost was derived.

V. Intervention Need (terracotta banner) — the bridge to Required

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Alternate procurement source needed?ToggleOn / OffInputON → note changes to “↻ Flagged → Details in Required Tab” and auto-creates a linked Alternate Source row in Required §I carrying this block's Product, Raw Material and Justification (Rule 3). OFF → removes that linked row.If ON, Justification should be filled before save.
Source intervention — JustificationTextareaFree textInputTwo-way synced into the linked Required source row's read-only Justification field.“Add a justification for the alternate-source intervention.”
Alternate raw material needed?ToggleOn / OffInputON → note changes to “⇄ Flagged → Details in Required Tab” and auto-creates a linked Alternate Material row in Required §II. OFF → removes that row.If ON, Justification should be filled before save.
Material intervention — JustificationTextareaFree textInputTwo-way synced into the linked Required material row's Justification field.“Add a justification for the alternate-material intervention.”
Intervention bridge (Rule 3). A flag toggle and its linked Required row are bound: toggling ON creates the row (no duplicate created if one already exists); toggling OFF deletes it; deleting the Required row from the Required tab clears the flag back to “Not flagged”; removing the whole Existing block removes all of its linked Required rows. Product / Raw Material / Justification on the Required row are read-only mirrors of the Existing block.

Existing Total Bar (teal footer)

Field NameField TypeInput / AutofillLogic
Material CountCalculatedCalculatedNumber of Existing blocks.
Essential MaterialsCalculatedCalculatedCount where Is It Essential? = Yes.
Total Monthly SpendCalculated currencyCalculatedSum of all Amount Per Batch values.
Total RM Cost / UnitCalculated currencyCalculatedSum of each block's Total Landed Cost / Unit.
Auto-fetches toStatic label (amber)—“Existing Unit Pricing → Raw Materials.” (Golden Thread sink.)
Tab 3: Required ▼

Three numbered sections. Sections I & II are bridge-populated — rows are auto-created from the Existing tab's intervention toggles (a manual “+ Add” button also exists). Each row shows read-only originating context (Product / Current Raw Material / Justification) then editable optimisation fields and an Implementation Status block. Empty sections show a dashed placeholder.

Section I — Alternate Procurement Sources

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductRead-only text—Autofill (bridge)Mirrors the originating Existing block's Product Name.—
Current Raw MaterialRead-only text—Autofill (bridge)Mirrors the originating Name of Raw Material.—
Justification (from Existing)Textarea (synced)Free textAutofill, editable (two-way)Synced with the Existing block's source Justification.—
New Source TypeSelectMarket / Wholesaler / Farm / Forest / Online / Cooperative · FPO / Direct Producer / OthersInput—“Select the new source type.”
New Source NameTextFree textInputSurfaced as the Intervention detail sub-line in Summary.—
LocationTextFree textInput——
ContactTextPhone / emailInput—Validate format if entered.
New Price Per Unit (₹)Currency≥ 0, 2 decimalsInputFeeds Expected Saving / Unit and the Summary New Price column.“Cannot be negative.”
Old Price Per Unit (₹)Currency≥ 0, 2 decimalsInput (defaults to the existing material's Price Per Unit)Baseline for the saving calc.“Cannot be negative.”
Expected Saving / UnitCalculated (read-only)—Calculated= Old Price Per Unit − New Price Per Unit, shown as “₹{value} / unit”.—
NotesTextFree textInput——
Implementation StatusSelectPending / In Progress / Completed / Not FeasibleInputDrives the Required Completed count and the Summary After / Saving (Completed only).—
Implementation NotesTextareaFree textInput——
Tag: “↻ Alternate Source”. Removing this row (Remove button) clears the originating Existing block's source toggle and resets its note to “Not flagged”.

Section II — Alternate Raw Materials

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductRead-only text—Autofill (bridge)Mirrors the originating Existing block's Product.—
Current Raw MaterialRead-only text—Autofill (bridge)Mirrors the originating Raw Material.—
Justification (from Existing)Textarea (synced)Free textAutofill, editable (two-way)Synced with the Existing block's material Justification.—
Proposed Alternate MaterialTextFree textInputSurfaced as the Intervention detail sub-line in Summary.“Enter the proposed alternate material.”
Reason for ChangeSelectCost Reduction / Quality Improvement / Availability / Sustainability / Local Sourcing / OthersInput——
New Price Per Unit (₹)Currency (placeholder 0.00)≥ 0, 2 decimalsInputFeeds the Summary New Price column and Price Impact (vs the existing material's Price Per Unit).“Cannot be negative.”
Quality Trade-offSelectNone / Minor / Moderate / SignificantInput——
Trial StatusSelectPending / Sample Sourced / Trial Run / Approved / RejectedInputTracks substitution trial progress (distinct from Implementation Status).—
NotesTextFree text (substitution, sourcing, trials)Input——
Implementation StatusSelectPending / In Progress / Completed / Not FeasibleInputDrives Completed count and Summary After / Saving (Completed only).—
Implementation NotesTextareaFree textInput——
Tag: “⇄ Alternate Material”. Removing this row clears the originating Existing block's material toggle.

Section III — New Raw Material (standalone, freely added)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Product / PurposeTextFree textInputShown as Product in the Summary New-material row.—
SKU / ReferenceTextFree textInput——
Name of Raw Material *TextFree textInputShown (bold) in the Summary row.“Raw material name is required.”
Procurement SourceSelectMarket / Wholesaler / Farm / Forest / Online / Cooperative · FPO / Direct Producer / OthersInput——
Source NameTextFree textInput——
Source LocationTextFree textInput——
Source ContactTextPhone / emailInput—Validate format if entered.
AvailabilitySelectEasy / Moderate / DifficultInputRenders as a badge in the Summary row.—
UnitTexte.g., gram, ml, kgInput——
Qty Required Per BatchNumber≥ 0Input—“Cannot be negative.”
Price Per Unit (₹)Currency≥ 0, 2 decimalsInput—“Cannot be negative.”
Batch SizeNumber> 0Input—“Batch size must be greater than 0.”
Transport Cost Per Trip (₹)Currency≥ 0, 2 decimalsInput—“Cannot be negative.”
Is It Essential?SelectYes / NoInput——
Reason for AddingTextFree textInputWhy this new raw material is needed.—
Implementation StatusSelectPending / In Progress / Completed / On HoldInputNote: New-material rows use On Hold (not Not Feasible) as the fourth option.—
Implementation NotesTextareaFree textInput——
Tag: “+ New Material”. New-material rows have no Price / Saving columns in Summary (highlighted row, Price & Saving show “—”).

Required Total Bar — Optimised View (teal footer)

Field NameField TypeInput / AutofillLogic
Alternate SourcesCalculatedCalculatedCount of Section I rows.
Alternate MaterialsCalculatedCalculatedCount of Section II rows.
New Raw MaterialsCalculatedCalculatedCount of Section III rows.
Status: CompletedCalculatedCalculatedCount of all Required rows with status = Completed.
Auto-fetches toStatic label (amber)—“Optimised Unit Pricing → Raw Materials.” (Golden Thread sink.)
Tab 4: Resources ▼

Reference Materials child table — supplier directories, quotations, substitute references etc. “+ Add Resource” appends a row; at least one row must remain.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Resource TitleTextFree textInput—“Resource title is required.”
CategorySelectSupplier Directory / Wholesaler Contact / FPO · Cooperative / Quality Standards / Seasonal Calendar / Substitute MaterialInput——
TypeSelectContact / Reference / Quotation / Specification / Map · LocationInput——
Description / NotesTextFree textInput——
FileAttach (Upload / View)File uploadInputUpload stores the file; View opens it.—
Tab 5: Task Log ▼

Activity log for raw-material work. Opens with one blank row; “+ Add Entry” appends and auto-numbers rows (01, 02, …); deleting renumbers the remainder.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Auto number—AutofillZero-padded sequence; recomputed on add / delete.—
DateDateDate pickerInput——
Task NameSelect (preset list)01 Awareness on Importance of RM Data Collection · 02 Record RM Data · 03 Calculate RM Cost Per Unit · 04 Identify Difficult-Availability Materials · 05 Identify Alternate Procurement Source Need · 06 Identify Alternate RM Need · 07 Identify New RM Need · 08 Research Alternative Procurement Sources · 09 Research Alternative RMs · 10 Research New RM · 11 Record Alternate Source · 12 Record Alternate Material · 13 Record New RM · 14 Establish Supply Linkage · 15 Monitor Supply Linkage · 16 Review RM Cost Impact on Unit Pricing · If other, specify in commentsInputStandard 16-step raw-material activity taxonomy plus an “other” option.“Select a task.”
Notes / OutcomeTextFree textInputRequired when Task Name = “If other, specify in comments”.“Describe the outcome.”
AttachmentAttach (Upload)File uploadInput——
Key validations (whole form): Product Name, Name of Raw Material, Current Status of Use, Is It Essential?, Unit, Quantity Required Per Batch, Price Per Unit and Batch Size are mandatory on every Existing material. Batch Size must be > 0 — all per-unit calculations (RM Qty/Unit, RM Cost/Unit, Transport/Unit, Total Landed Cost/Unit) return 0 when Batch Size is 0 or blank. All currency / quantity inputs must be ≥ 0. An intervention flag toggled ON must have a Justification before save, and toggling it ON/OFF must keep the linked Required row in sync (create on ON, delete on OFF; deleting the Required row resets the flag). Summary “After” and “Saving / Unit” count Completed interventions only. New-material rows use “On Hold” as the fourth status (not “Not Feasible”).
Section Review:
PR-MFG-002

Machinery

Captures every machine and tool the entrepreneur owns or needs, computes per-machine depreciation that flows individually into Unit Pricing, and is part of the Manufacturing module. Golden-Thread ties: machines named in a Process Flow pre-create rows in the Existing tab, and machines flagged as Required surface in Infrastructure / EE workflows through explicit dropdown selection (never auto-pushed).
Key behaviour — the Existing → Required intervention bridge. A machine is recorded ONCE in the Existing tab. Toggling its “Servicing Needed?” or “Capacity Upgrade Needed?” flag does not capture any cost — it only raises a flag. The Fessociate then opens the Required tab and adds an Intervention Row, picks the flagged machine from a dropdown of Existing machines, and the flag type + reference (current cost · condition) auto-fetch. New (additional) machines are recorded separately in Required → Section II with the full Existing-style structure plus a Procurement Status. Each machine’s Depreciation / Unit feeds Unit Pricing individually — the engine reads every machine row, not a single total.

On-Screen UI Structure

Top bar: teal header “Machinery Module” with crumb “PRIME Rural · DF Machinery”, and an entrepreneur chip (avatar + name) auto-fetched from the profile.
Tab rail: horizontal pill bar with five tabs — Summary · Existing · Required · Resources · Task Log; active tab renders teal-on-white.
Body layout: each tab is a panel. Summary is read-only: a machine-wise overview table, four aggregate cards (Machines / Capacity / Cost / Interventions & Status), and a 4-cell flag strip. Existing and Required render each machine as a collapsible “machine block” (sea-blue header with name input + Remove) holding four sub-sections: Classification & Process Integration, Technical & Power Specifications, Operational Capacity & Usage, Financial & Maintenance Data.
Special widgets: (1) Depreciation / capacity calc strips — highlighted cells inside each block showing Monthly Output, Capacity Utilization, Capacity Gap, Depreciation Rate, Monthly Depreciation and Depreciation / Unit, each derived live. (2) Intervention banners — in Existing, a terracotta “Intervention Need — Flag Only” panel with two toggles; in Required, collapsible intervention rows with Machine / Flag / Implementation Status selectors in the header. (3) A teal total bar at the foot of Existing and Required summarising counts, investment, output and total depreciation, and naming the Unit Pricing target.
Per Rule 1, every calculated field (Monthly Output, Utilization, Gap, Depreciation Rate, Monthly Depreciation, Depreciation / Unit, all totals and aggregates) renders italic on a cream-deep background and is non-editable, with a tooltip stating its formula.
Summary
→
Existing
→
Required
→
Resources
→
Task Log

User Journey

Open
Diagnostics → Manufacturing → Machinery (entrepreneur auto-fetched)
→
Record Existing
Add each owned machine/tool; specs, capacity, cost, condition — depreciation auto-computes
→
Flag Needs
Toggle Servicing / Capacity Upgrade on machines needing intervention
→
Detail Interventions
Required tab → add row, pick flagged machine, enter cost & status
→
Research & Add New
Record required new machines + procurement status; attach Resources
→
Log & Save
Capture activities in Task Log; review Summary; save
Tab 1 — Summary (read-only roll-up)

Entirely auto-populated from the Existing and Required tabs. No data entry on this tab. It contains four blocks: (1) a Machine-wise Overview table, (2) four Aggregate cards, (3) an Intervention Flag strip. Each machine’s Depreciation / Unit feeds Unit Pricing individually; the “TOTAL (indicative)” row is informational only because Unit Pricing reads each machine separately.

1.1 Machine-wise Overview table (one row per machine, Existing + New; auto-built)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
MachineRead-only text—AutofillMachine name from each Existing & New block. New machines render on a highlighted row.—
ConditionRead-only badgeGood / Fair / PoorAutofillFrom machine’s Machinery Condition. Badge colour: Good=green, Fair=amber, Poor=red; “—” if unset.—
Monthly OutputRead-only number (italic, cream-deep)—Calculateddays × hours × Current Usage Capacity/hr, shown as “N units”.—
UtilisationRead-only badge—Calculated(Current cap / Max cap) × 100, rounded. Badge: ≥80% green, ≥60% amber, else red; “—” if Max=0. New rows show “—”.—
Capacity GapRead-only number (italic)—CalculatedMax cap − Current cap (only if >0), shown “N units/hr”, terracotta; else “—”.—
Dep / Unit (₹)Read-only currency (italic)—CalculatedPer-machine Depreciation / Unit (see formula in tab 2). Teal bold.—
Intervention TypeRead-only badge(s)Servicing / Capacity / + New MachineAutofillExisting rows show one badge per active flag (“Servicing”, “Capacity”). New rows show “+ New Machine”. “—” if none.—
Implementation StatusRead-only badgePending / In Progress / CompletedAutofillFor New machines, mirrors the block’s Implementation Status. Existing rows show “—” here (status lives on the linked Intervention Row).—
TOTAL (indicative) rowRead-only total row—CalculatedSum of Monthly Output; overall utilisation = totalOutput / totalPotential; total gap; total Dep/Unit (sum of existing). Labelled “Unit Pricing reads each machine individually”.—

1.2 Aggregate cards (four, all read-only / calculated)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Machines · Existing MachinesRead-only count—CalculatedCount of Existing blocks.—
Machines · Total Investment (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedSum of Cost of Machine across Existing blocks (Indian formatting, e.g. ₹1,00,000).—
Machines · New Machines AddedRead-only count—CalculatedCount of New blocks in Required → Section II.—
Machines · Additional Investment (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedSum of Cost across New blocks; “—” if none.—
Capacity · Current Monthly OutputRead-only number—CalculatedSum of Monthly Output across Existing + New machines, “N units”.—
Capacity · Max Potential OutputRead-only number—CalculatedSum of (days × hours × Max cap) across machines, “N units”.—
Capacity · Overall UtilisationRead-only %—CalculatedtotalOutput / totalPotential × 100; “—” if potential=0.—
Capacity · Capacity GapRead-only number—CalculatedSum of per-machine gaps; “N units/hr” or “—”.—
Cost · Dep / Unit — Before (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedSum of Existing machines’ Depreciation / Unit.—
Cost · Dep / Unit — After (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedExisting + New combined Dep/Unit; populates only when ≥1 New machine exists, else “—”. Green if lower than Before, red if higher.—
Interventions · Servicing NeededRead-only count—CalculatedCount of Existing machines with Servicing flag active.—
Interventions · Capacity Upgrade NeededRead-only count—CalculatedCount of machines with Capacity flag active OR a positive capacity gap.—
Interventions · CompletedRead-only count—CalculatedCount of Intervention Rows with Implementation Status = Completed.—
Interventions · In ProgressRead-only count—CalculatedCount of Intervention Rows with status = In Progress.—
Interventions · In ProcurementRead-only count—CalculatedCount of New machines whose Implementation Status is In Progress or Completed.—

1.3 Intervention Flag strip (four count cards)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Servicing NeededRead-only count card—Calculated= Interventions · Servicing Needed.—
Capacity GapRead-only count card—Calculated= Interventions · Capacity Upgrade Needed.—
New MachinesRead-only count card—CalculatedCount of New machine blocks.—
In ProcurementRead-only count card—Calculated= Interventions · In Procurement.—
Tab 2 — Existing (machines/tools already owned; child table of machine blocks)

Repeating child table: each row is a collapsible “machine block” added via + Add Machine. Machines named in a Process Flow arrive here as pre-created Existing rows (Golden Thread). Block header carries the machine name (synced with the name field below) and a Remove button (confirm prompt). Four sub-sections + an intervention flag panel + a foot total bar. Below are all fields of one machine row.

2.1 Classification & Process Integration

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductRead-only text (italic)—AutofillAuto-fetched from the Product module.—
Name of Machinery / Tool *TextFree textInputMandatory. Syncs to the block header name. Source for the Required tab’s machine dropdown.Required: “Enter the machine / tool name.”
Process Flow No.Texte.g. “Processed Food-Process Flow”Input / AutofillLinks the machine to a Process Flow; pre-filled for process-flow-derived rows.—
PurposeTexte.g. “Shredding”InputWhat the machine does in the process.—

2.2 Technical & Power Specifications

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Power / Phase NeededSelect— Select — / Single Phase / III PhaseInputFeeds power / solarisation planning.—
Wattage / Load (W)Number≥ 0InputElectrical load of the machine.Must be ≥ 0: “Wattage cannot be negative.”
No. of Machinery / Tool *NumberDefault 1InputMandatory quantity of identical units.Required, ≥ 1: “Quantity must be at least 1.”

2.3 Operational Capacity & Usage (drives Monthly Output + Utilisation)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Days of Equipment Usage / MonthNumber0–31InputOperating days per month (factor in Monthly Output).Range 0–31: “Days must be between 0 and 31.”
Hours of Usage / DayNumber0–24InputOperating hours per day (factor in Monthly Output).Range 0–24: “Hours must be between 0 and 24.”
Main Usage TimeSelect— Select — / Day / Night / BothInputWhen the machine is mainly run (informs solar sizing).—
Current Usage Capacity / hr (units)Number≥ 0InputActual units produced per hour now.Must be ≥ 0; should be ≤ Max capacity: “Current capacity cannot exceed max processing capacity.”
Max Processing Capacity / hr (units)Number≥ 0InputMaximum achievable units per hour (denominator for Utilisation / Gap).Must be ≥ Current capacity if both entered.
Monthly OutputRead-only calc (italic, cream-deep)—Calculated= Days × Hours × Current Usage Capacity/hr; rendered “N units / month”. Tooltip: “days × hrs × current capacity”.—
Capacity UtilizationRead-only calc (italic)—Calculated= (Current cap / Max cap) × 100, shown “NN% (cur/max)”; “—” if Max=0.—
Capacity GapRead-only calc (italic)—Calculated= Max cap − Current cap, shown “N units / hr unused”; “—” if Max=0.—

2.4 Financial & Maintenance Data (drives Depreciation — feeds Existing Unit Pricing)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Cost of Machine / Tool (₹)Number / currency≥ 0InputPurchase cost; base for depreciation.Must be ≥ 0: “Cost cannot be negative.”
Brand SeriesTextFree textInputMake / model series.—
Machinery ConditionSelect— Select — / Good (10% depreciation) / Fair (20% depreciation) / Poor (30% depreciation)InputDrives the annual Depreciation Rate (Good=10%, Fair=20%, Poor=30%).Required to compute depreciation; if blank, depreciation shows “—”.
Critical / Non-CriticalSelect— Select — / Critical / Non-CriticalInputMarks production-critical equipment.—
If Critical, provide reasonTextareaFree textInput (conditional)Shown / required when Critical / Non-Critical = Critical.Conditionally required: “Provide a reason for marking this machine critical.”
Servicing Required?Select— Select — / Yes / NoInputDeclares whether routine servicing is due.—
Upkeep Cost (₹)Number / currency≥ 0InputRecurring maintenance cost.Must be ≥ 0.
Age of Machinery (years)Number≥ 0InputYears in service.Must be ≥ 0.
Depreciation RateRead-only calc (italic)—Calculated= rate by Condition (Good 10% / Fair 20% / Poor 30%), shown “NN% per year”.—
Monthly DepreciationRead-only calc (italic)—Calculated= (Cost × annual rate) / 12, shown “₹N / month”.—
Depreciation / UnitRead-only calc (italic)—Calculated= Monthly Depreciation / Monthly Output, shown “₹N.NN / unit”; “₹0.00” if Output=0. Auto-fetches to Existing Unit Pricing → Machinery Depreciation / Unit (per machine).—

2.5 Intervention Need — Flag Only (terracotta panel; raises flags, captures no cost)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Servicing Needed?Toggle (on/off)Off (default) / OnInputWhen On, note reads “Flagged → Details in Required Tab” and the machine becomes selectable as a Servicing intervention in Required. No cost captured here.—
Capacity Upgrade Needed?Toggle (on/off)Off (default) / OnInputWhen On, note reads “Flagged → Details in Required Tab”; selectable as a Capacity intervention in Required.—

2.6 All Existing Machines — Total bar (foot, read-only)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Machine CountRead-only count—CalculatedNumber of Existing machine blocks.—
Total InvestmentRead-only currency—CalculatedSum of Cost of Machine (Indian formatting).—
Monthly OutputRead-only number—CalculatedSum of per-machine Monthly Output, “N units”.—
Total Depreciation / UnitRead-only currency—CalculatedSum of per-machine Depreciation / Unit.—
Auto-fetches toRead-only label—Static“Existing Unit Pricing → Machinery Depreciation / Unit” (note: Unit Pricing reads each machine individually).—
Tab 3 — Required (Section I: Existing Machine Interventions · Section II: New Machines)
Bridge from Existing (Rule 3). Section I rows do not re-enter machine data — they link to a machine already in the Existing tab via a dropdown built from Existing machine names. On selection, the machine’s active flag (Servicing / Capacity) and a Reference (current cost · condition) auto-fetch. Section II New machines are fully described here; if a New machine is later marked Required for the EE, it surfaces in Infrastructure / EE through explicit dropdown selection there — it is never auto-pushed.

3.1 Section I — Existing Machine Interventions (repeating intervention rows)

Added via + Add Intervention Row. Empty state: “No interventions yet — click + Add Intervention Row and select a flagged machine from the Existing tab.” Header carries Machine / Flag / Implementation Status selectors + Remove; body holds the Intervention Details sub-section.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
MachineSelect (Link to Existing)— Select machine — / <Existing machine names>InputDropdown built from Name of Machinery in the Existing tab. On select, auto-fetches Flag type + Reference. This is the Existing→Required linkage.Required: “Select a machine from the Existing tab.”
Flag (Type)Select— Auto — / Servicing / Capacity UpgradeAutofill (overridable)Auto-set from the selected machine’s active toggle (Servicing if its service note active, else Capacity if capacity note active, else blank).—
Implementation StatusSelectPending (default) / In Progress / CompletedInputDrives Summary “Completed” / “In Progress” counts.—
Reference (current cost / condition)Read-only text (auto, cream-deep)—AutofillOn machine select, populates “₹<cost> · <condition>” from the Existing block.—
Issue / DescriptionTextFree textInputDescribe the issue or scope of the intervention.—
Intervention Cost (₹)Number / currency≥ 0InputCost of servicing / upgrade for this machine. Captured here, not in Existing.Must be ≥ 0: “Intervention cost cannot be negative.”
New Electricity Cost / Unit (after solar)Number / currency (step 0.01)≥ 0InputRevised per-unit electricity cost after a solar / capacity intervention (feeds running-cost analysis).Must be ≥ 0.

3.2 Section II — New Machines (full machine block, like Existing, + Procurement; feeds Required Unit Pricing)

Added via + Add New Machine. Identical four sub-sections as the Existing tab (2.1–2.4 fields apply verbatim: Classification & Process Integration, Technical & Power Specifications, Operational Capacity & Usage with the same Monthly Output / Utilization / Gap calcs, Financial & Maintenance Data with the same Depreciation Rate / Monthly Depreciation / Depreciation / Unit formulae). The header additionally carries an Implementation Status selector. Differences from Existing are listed below; there is NO Intervention-flag panel on New machines.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Implementation Status (header)SelectPending (default) / In Progress / CompletedInputMirrors to Summary; In Progress / Completed counts toward “In Procurement”.—
Procurement StatusSelectPending / Vendor Selected / EE Procurement - Selected / EE Completed / Procured (Self)InputTracks how the machine is being procured. “EE Procurement - Selected” / “EE Completed” denote procurement routed through the Entrepreneurship Enablement (EE) channel (selected via dropdown, not auto-pushed).—
Depreciation / Unit (New)Read-only calc (italic)—CalculatedSame formula as Existing: (Cost × rate)/12 / Monthly Output, “₹N.NN / unit”. Auto-fetches to Required Unit Pricing → Machinery Depreciation / Unit.—

3.3 Total Machinery — Required (Existing + New) total bar (foot, read-only)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Existing Depreciation / UnitRead-only currency—CalculatedSum of Existing machines’ Dep / Unit.—
New Machine Depreciation / UnitRead-only currency—CalculatedSum of New machines’ Dep / Unit.—
Total MachinesRead-only count—CalculatedExisting + New block counts.—
Total Depreciation / UnitRead-only currency—CalculatedExisting Dep/Unit + New Dep/Unit.—
Auto-fetches toRead-only label—Static“Required Unit Pricing → Machinery Depreciation / Unit”.—
Tab 4 — Resources (reference materials child table)

Repeating child table added via + Add Resource; at least one row must remain (“At least one row is required.”). Per row:

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Resource TitleTextFree textInputName of the reference material.—
CategorySelect— Select — / Machinery Catalogue / Solar Vendor / Servicing Contact / Procurement Guide / Safety / ComplianceInputClassifies the resource.—
TypeSelect— Select — / Template / Reference / Vendor / Quotation / SpecificationInputResource format / nature.—
Description / NotesTextFree textInputNotes about the resource.—
FileAttach (Upload / View)FileInputUpload a file or View the attached one.—
Tab 5 — Task Log (Machinery activities child table)

Repeating child table added via + Add Entry; one seed row on load. Row No. auto-renumbers (01, 02, …) on add/delete. Per row:

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Read-only auto-number—CalculatedSequential index, zero-padded; re-numbers on row delete.—
DateDateCalendar dateInputDate the activity occurred.—
Task NameSelect— Select Task — + the 15 master tasks: 01 Awareness on Importance of Machinery for Production Capacity / 02 Record Existing Machinery Data / 03 Record Power & Solarization Requirements / 04 Identify Servicing Need / 05 Identify New Machine Requirement / 06 Research Required Machinery / 07 Record Required Machinery / 08 Research Vendors / 09 Select Vendor & Receive Quotation / 10 Facilitate Machinery Procurement / 11 Confirm Servicing Completion / 12 Confirm Machinery Delivery / 13 Complete Installation & Trial Run / 14 Complete Solarization Installation / 15 Conduct Post-Installation Follow Up / If other, specify in commentsInputStandardised activity picklist.—
Notes / OutcomeTextFree textInputOutcome / remarks; required when Task Name = “If other, specify in comments”.Conditionally required for “If other”: “Specify the task in notes.”
AttachmentAttach (Upload)FileInputOptional supporting file.—
Key validations (whole form): Name of Machinery / Tool and No. of Machinery / Tool are mandatory on every Existing and New machine block (quantity ≥ 1). Days (0–31), Hours (0–24), all capacity, cost, wattage, upkeep and age values must be ≥ 0; Current Usage Capacity must not exceed Max Processing Capacity. Machinery Condition must be set for depreciation to compute (otherwise depreciation shows “—”). “If Critical, provide reason” is required when the machine is marked Critical. In Required → Section I, every Intervention Row must reference a machine that exists in the Existing tab (dropdown selection); intervention cost and post-solar electricity cost must be ≥ 0. Resources require at least one row. Depreciation / Unit feeds Unit Pricing per machine — never as a single aggregate. Required machines reach Infrastructure / EE only by explicit dropdown selection, not auto-push.
Section Review:
PR-MFG-003

Human Resource

Captures the enterprise workforce (categories, skill levels, wages and headcount) under the Manufacturing module's diagnostic framework. Each worker category's monthly labour cost and labour-rate-per-hour feed Unit Pricing individually, while every wage line is checked against Government of Meghalaya (GoM) minimum-wage thresholds for compliance flagging.
Key behaviour — Existing → Required intervention bridge: When the Fessociate toggles "Labour intervention needed?" on an Existing worker block, a linked row is auto-created in the Required tab (Section I, Labour Interventions). The bridge is two-way: the Existing worker's Category / Skill / Nature / Justification flow into the Required row as read-only context, and removing the Required row (or unflagging the toggle) clears the bridge on both sides. New Labour (Required Section II) is standalone and is NOT bridged to Existing.

On-Screen UI Structure

Top bar: teal header "Human Resource Module" with breadcrumb "PRIME Rural · DF Human Resource". Below it an entrepreneur chip (avatar + name) auto-fetched from the entrepreneur profile.
Tab rail: a horizontal rail of five tabs — Summary · Existing · Required · Resources · Task Log. Active tab is teal-filled; switching to Summary triggers a full recalculation.
Body layout: each tab is a panel. Existing renders one collapsible worker block (child table row) per category, each with three sub-sections (Worker Classification, Wage Structure, Cost & Compliance) plus an Intervention-Need toggle. Required holds two sections — I. Labour Interventions (bridged rows) and II. New Labour (standalone blocks). Summary shows a worker-wise overview grid, four aggregate cards, and four intervention-flag tiles.
Special widgets: a per-block GoM compliance flag (green "Yes" / red "No" condition-badge) and a red wage-warning banner when the implied daily wage falls below the GoM minimum for the skill level; collapsible block headers carry Skill and GoM✓/GoM✗ tags.
Calculated fields (Total Workers, Monthly Labour Cost, Labour Rate / hr, GoM Compliant?, Required Monthly Cost) are rendered italic on a cream-deep / highlight background, read-only, with the active formula shown in the "Cost Formula Applied" strip (Rule 1).
Summary
→
Existing
→
Required
→
Resources
→
Task Log

User Journey

Open Human Resource
Diagnostics → Manufacturing → Human Resource; entrepreneur auto-fetched
→
Record existing workforce
Existing tab — add a worker block per category (classification, headcount, wages)
→
Check cost & GoM compliance
Monthly cost & rate/hr auto-calc; system flags wages below GoM minimum
→
Flag intervention
Toggle "Labour intervention needed?" → bridges row into Required tab
→
Plan required workforce
Required tab — intervention type, required headcount/wages, status; add New Labour
→
Review & Save
Summary recalculates; per-category cost feeds Unit Pricing
Tab 1 — Summary (read-only roll-up) ▼
Entirely auto-generated from the Existing and Required tabs. Recalculates whenever the tab is opened or any source field changes. No direct data entry on this tab.

A. Worker-wise Overview (table)

Each worker category's labour cost/unit feeds Unit Pricing individually. Intervention Type and Status columns populate only when a flag is raised. New Labour rows are appended below (highlighted) with a "+ New Labour" tag.

ColumnSourceLogic / Display
CategoryExisting / New Labour blockWorker Category value (bold)
Skill LevelblockUnskilled / Semi-skilled / Skilled / Highly Skilled
EmploymentblockEmployment Nature value
MaleblockNo. of Male Workers (“—” if 0)
FemaleblockNo. of Female Workers (“—” if 0)
Total WorkerscalculatedMale + Female
Monthly Cost (₹)calculatedBlock monthly labour cost
GoMcalculated flagCondition-badge: green “Yes” / red “No” / “—”
Intervention TypeRequired (bridged row)“↻ Labour” badge + selected intervention type; “—” if unflagged
Implementation StatusRequired (bridged row)Status badge: Pending / In Progress / Completed / Not Feasible
New WorkersRequired (bridged row)Required headcount + signed delta vs current (e.g. “+2 workers”); “Pending” if no value
New Monthly Cost (₹)Required (bridged row)Required monthly cost + signed delta (▲ Cost up / ▼ Cost down)
Footer note row: “Unit Pricing reads each worker category individually” — rendered as a total-row caption, reinforcing that costs are NOT summed before being passed downstream.

B. Aggregate Cards (4 cards, all calculated)

CardMetricFormula / Logic
ExistingWorker CategoriesCount of Existing worker blocks
Total WorkersΣ (Male + Female) across Existing blocks
Male WorkersΣ Male
Female WorkersΣ Female
Monthly Labour Cost (₹)Σ block monthly cost
GoM CompliantCount of blocks with GoM = Yes
GoM Non-CompliantCount of blocks with GoM = No
CostMonthly Cost — Before (₹)= Existing monthly labour cost
Monthly Cost — After (₹)Σ Required cost of Completed interventions only; “—” if none
Cost Change (₹)After − Before of Completed set (▲/▼ signed); “—” if none
Workers Before= Existing total workers
Workers After(Existing total − Completed current) + Completed required headcount
Jobs Created / ChangedWorkers After − Workers Before (signed); “—” if no Completed
Note“After = Completed interventions only.”
InterventionsLabour Interventions FlaggedCount of Existing blocks with toggle ON
Skilled / Highly SkilledΣ workers where Skill = Skilled or Highly Skilled
Avg Labour Rate / hr (₹)Total monthly cost ÷ total worker-hours (Σ count × days × hours); 0 if hours = 0
StatusCompletedCount of bridged rows with Status = Completed
In ProgressCount Status = In Progress
PendingCount Status = Pending
Not FeasibleCount Status = Not Feasible

C. Intervention Flag Tiles (4)

TileCount
Labour Intervention Needed (↻, amber)= Labour Interventions Flagged
GoM Non-Compliant (!, red)= count of GoM = No blocks
GoM Compliant (✓, green)= count of GoM = Yes blocks
Skilled / Highly Skilled (★, blue)= Skilled / Highly Skilled worker count
Tab 2 — Existing (workforce child table) ▼
Repeating collapsible worker block = one row of the workforce child table (DF Human Resource Workforce Entry). “+ Add Worker Category” adds a block; each block header shows Row no., a live label (Category · Nature), and Skill / GoM tags. Three default rows are seeded on load. A teal total-bar sits below the list and feeds Existing Unit Pricing → Human Resource.

Sub-section I — Worker Classification

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Worker Category *SelectSelf · Unpaid Family · Paid Family · Hired WorkerInputUpdates block head label; drives Summary groupingRequired — “Worker category is required”
Skill Level *SelectUnskilled · Semi-skilled · Skilled · Highly SkilledInputSets the GoM minimum-wage threshold used for compliance; sets Skill tag; counts toward Skilled tally if Skilled/Highly SkilledRequired — “Skill level is required”
Employment Nature *SelectPermanent · Contract · Casual / Daily · Seasonal · Part-timeInputShown in head label and SummaryRequired — “Employment nature is required”
No. of Male WorkersNumber (int)≥ 0InputFeeds Total Workers and Summary Male tallyMust be ≥ 0; no negatives
No. of Female WorkersNumber (int)≥ 0InputFeeds Total Workers and Summary Female tallyMust be ≥ 0; no negatives
Total WorkersNumber (calculated)AutoCalculated= Male + Female; blank when 0. Recalculates on either count changeRead-only

Sub-section II — Wage Structure

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Wage Type *SelectPer Day · Per Month · Per HourInputSelects which cost formula is applied; drives daily-equivalent for GoM checkRequired — “Wage type is required”
Wage Rate *Number / Currency (₹)≥ 0, step 0.01InputInterpreted per the Wage Type (per day / month / hour)Required — “Wage rate is required”; must be ≥ 0
Avg Working Days / MonthNumber≥ 0 (e.g. 22)InputUsed by Per Day & Per Hour cost formulas and by rate/hr derivationMust be ≥ 0; required when Wage Type = Per Day or Per Hour
Avg Hours / DayNumber≥ 0 (e.g. 8)InputUsed by Per Hour cost formula and by rate/hr derivationMust be ≥ 0; required when Wage Type = Per Hour

Sub-section III — Cost & Compliance (all calculated)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Monthly Labour Cost (₹)Currency (calculated)AutoCalculated= Monthly-per-worker × Total Workers. Per Day: Rate × Days. Per Month: Rate. Per Hour: Rate × Days × Hours/Day. Feeds Unit Pricing per categoryRead-only
Labour Rate / hr (₹)Currency (calculated)AutoCalculatedPer Hour: = Rate. Else: monthly-per-worker ÷ (Days × Hours). Per Day fallback: Rate ÷ HoursRead-only
GoM Compliant?Flag (calculated)Yes / No / —CalculatedCompare implied daily wage to GoM minimum for the Skill Level (see compliance callout). Renders green “Yes” / red “No” condition-badge; “—” until enough inputsRead-only
Cost Formula AppliedText (display)AutoCalculatedShows the live formula string and a TOTAL MONTHLY COST line with worker countRead-only
GoM Warning BannerConditional bannerHidden / shownCalculatedShown only when GoM = No: “Implied daily wage is below GoM minimum (₹{min} / day) for {skill} workers.”—
GoM minimum-wage compliance logic (per skill level, per day): Unskilled ₹360 · Semi-skilled ₹410 · Skilled ₹480 · Highly Skilled ₹550 (reference thresholds — in production sourced from the configurable GoM Wage Settings doctype).
Implied daily wage = Per Day: Wage Rate · Per Month: Rate ÷ Days · Per Hour: Rate × Hours/Day. Compliant when implied daily wage ≥ the skill-level minimum; otherwise Non-Compliant (red badge + warning banner). Flag stays “—” until Skill, Wage Type and a usable rate are present.

Intervention Need

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Labour intervention needed?Toggle (Yes/No)On / OffInputON → creates a linked row in Required → I. Labour Interventions and shows “↻ Flagged → Details in Required Tab”. OFF → removes the bridged row. Increments the Flagged tallyJustification required when flagged (recommended)
JustificationTextareaFree textInputSynced live into the bridged Required row's read-only justification field—
Existing total-bar (read-only roll-up, feeds Existing Unit Pricing): Worker Categories (block count) · Total Workers (Σ counts) · Monthly Labour Cost (Σ block cost) · GoM Compliant (compliant / evaluated). Auto-fetches to Existing Unit Pricing → Human Resource.
Tab 3 — Required (Labour Interventions + New Labour) ▼
Two sections. I. Labour Interventions is auto-populated from the Existing tab when “Labour intervention needed?” is flagged (rows can also be added manually). II. New Labour holds standalone new worker categories not currently in the enterprise (not linked to Existing). A teal total-bar feeds Optimised Unit Pricing → Human Resource.

Section I — Labour Interventions (bridged rows)

Head block carries read-only context bridged from the Existing worker (Category, Skill Level, Employment Nature, Justification — justification is editable and syncs back). Empty-state message shown until a flag is raised.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Worker CategoryData (read-only)—Autofill from bridged Existing blockDisplay-only contextRead-only
Skill LevelData (read-only)—Autofill from bridged blockDisplay-only contextRead-only
Employment NatureData (read-only)—Autofill from bridged blockDisplay-only contextRead-only
Justification (from Existing)TextareaFree textAutofill + editableTwo-way sync with the Existing block's justification—
Intervention TypeSelectHire Additional Workers · Wage Revision · Skill Upgrade / Training · Replace with Skilled Worker · Convert Family → Hired · Convert Casual → Permanent · Reduce Workforce · Compliance Correction (GoM)InputShown in Summary Intervention Type cellRequired — “Select an intervention type”
Current No. of WorkersNumber (read-only)AutoAutofill from bridged block Total WorkersBaseline for the “New Workers” deltaRead-only
Required No. of WorkersNumber (int)≥ 0InputDrives Required cost × count and the worker delta in SummaryMust be ≥ 0
Required Wage TypeSelectPer Day · Per Month · Per HourInputSelects Required cost formulaRequired when computing cost
Required Wage Rate (₹)Number / Currency≥ 0, step 0.01InputPer the Required Wage TypeMust be ≥ 0
Working Days / MonthNumber≥ 0InputPer Day & Per Hour formulasMust be ≥ 0
Hours / DayNumber≥ 0InputPer Hour formulaMust be ≥ 0
Required Monthly Cost (₹)Currency (calculated)AutoCalculated= per-worker × Required workers. Per Day: Rate × Days. Per Month: Rate. Per Hour: Rate × Days × Hours. Feeds Optimised Unit PricingRead-only
StatusSelectPending · In Progress · Completed · Not FeasibleInputOnly Completed rows count toward Summary “After” cost / Workers After / Jobs Created and the Required “Completed” totalDefaults to Pending
Removal behaviour: removing a bridged Required row also turns OFF the source Existing toggle and resets its note. Manually-added (non-bridged) rows are deleted independently.

Section II — New Labour (standalone blocks)

Same three-sub-section block structure as an Existing worker (Worker Classification, Wage Structure, Cost & Compliance, all identical fields, dropdowns, calculations and GoM check) plus an Implementation Status select. NOT bridged — these are entirely new categories. Appended to the Summary grid as highlighted rows tagged “+ New Labour”.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Worker Category *SelectSelf · Unpaid Family · Paid Family · Hired WorkerInputSame as ExistingRequired
Skill Level *SelectUnskilled · Semi-skilled · Skilled · Highly SkilledInputSets GoM thresholdRequired
Employment Nature *SelectPermanent · Contract · Casual / Daily · Seasonal · Part-timeInput—Required
No. of Male / Female WorkersNumber (int)≥ 0InputFeed Total WorkersMust be ≥ 0
Total WorkersNumber (calculated)AutoCalculated= Male + FemaleRead-only
Wage Type * / Wage Rate * / Days / HoursSelect + NumbersAs Existing Wage StructureInputDrive the same cost formulasWage Type & Rate required
Monthly Labour Cost / Labour Rate / hr / GoM Compliant?CalculatedAutoCalculatedIdentical formulas & GoM check as Existing Cost & ComplianceRead-only
Implementation StatusSelectPending · In Progress · Completed · Not FeasibleInputShown in Summary status badge for New Labour rowsDefaults to Pending
Required total-bar (read-only, feeds Optimised Unit Pricing): Interventions (bridged-row count) · Required Workers (Σ Completed required headcount) · Required Monthly Cost (Σ Completed required cost) · Status: Completed (count). Auto-fetches to Optimised Unit Pricing → Human Resource.
Tab 4 — Resources (Reference Materials child table) ▼
Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Resource TitleDataFree textInput—Required per row
CategorySelectGoM Wage Notification · Skilling / Training · Labour Contractor · SHG / Cooperative · Compliance ReferenceInput——
TypeSelectNotification · Reference · Contact · Training Material · SpecificationInput——
Description / NotesData / TextFree textInput——
FileAttach + ViewUpload / ViewInputUpload button + View action per row—
“+ Add Resource” appends a blank row; at least one row must remain (delete blocked on the last row).
Tab 5 — Task Log (HR Activities child table) ▼
Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Number (auto)AutoCalculatedZero-padded serial; re-sequences on row deleteRead-only
DateDateDate pickerInput——
Task NameSelect01 Awareness on Importance of Fair Labour Practices · 02 Record Existing Workforce Data · 03 Assess Labour Cost & GoM Compliance · 04 Identify Labour Intervention Need · 05 Identify Workforce Gap & Job Creation Potential · 06 Support Hiring of Additional Workers · 07 Record New Workforce Data After Intervention · 08 Conduct HR Practice Follow Up · If other, specify in commentsInputPredefined HR activity listRequired per logged row
Notes / OutcomeData / TextFree textInput——
AttachmentAttachUploadInputUpload button per row—
One row is seeded on load; “+ Add Entry” appends rows with auto-incrementing serials.
Key validations (whole form): (1) Worker Category, Skill Level, Employment Nature, Wage Type and Wage Rate are mandatory on every Existing and New Labour block. (2) Male / Female / Days / Hours / Wage Rate must be ≥ 0 — no negative values. (3) Days is required for Per Day & Per Hour cost formulas; Hours/Day is required for Per Hour. (4) GoM compliance is enforced as a flag, not a hard block: any block whose implied daily wage < the skill-level GoM minimum (Unskilled ₹360 / Semi-skilled ₹410 / Skilled ₹480 / Highly Skilled ₹550) is marked Non-Compliant with a red warning banner and counts in the GoM Non-Compliant tile. (5) A flagged Existing block should carry a justification; the bridged Required row then requires an Intervention Type. (6) Only Status = Completed Required rows propagate to Summary “After” cost, Workers After and Jobs Created. (7) Each worker category's Monthly Labour Cost feeds Unit Pricing individually — costs are never pre-summed before being passed downstream.
Section Review:
PR-MT-001

Branding Identity

The Branding Identity framework under the Marketing Tools module captures an enterprise's brand story, USP and tagline, then drives three implementation tracks — Brand, Logo and Business Card — each with a Designer-led draft cycle and dual approval from the entrepreneur and the core team. A Summary view rolls up approval status across all three tracks, and a Task Log records the 24-step branding journey.
Key behaviour: the Designer (or AI Generated option) uploads up to three sequential draft versions per track; each track advances only when both the Entrepreneur Approval and Core Team Approval reach Approved. Either party can return a track by marking Rejected, which re-opens the next draft slot.

On-Screen UI Structure

Top bar: teal app header reading “PRIME Rural | Marketing Tools” with a breadcrumb “Diagnostics / Marketing Tools” on the right.
Tab rail: a horizontally scrollable uppercase tab strip — Summary (default active), Brand, Logo, Business Card, Resources, Task Log. The active tab is filled teal.
Summary body: a brand profile card (teal hero) showing a logo upload placeholder, Enterprise label with brand name, italic tagline, and a right-aligned Market Scope value; below it a cream USP strip and a two-column row showing Current Brand Name and New Brand Name. Beneath sits a 3-column grid of track status cards (Brand / Logo / Business Card), each surfacing approval status chips. All Summary values are derived read-outs of data entered on the working tabs.
Brand / Logo / Business Card bodies: grouped into banner-led blocks — teal banners denote assessment/positioning data, terracotta banners denote implementation/action (Designer, Drafts, Approvals). Inside, label-and-value field rows are used, with auto-fetched fields shown italic on a cream-deep background per Rule 1.
Special widgets: a market-scope chip group (single-select pills), Yes/No intervention toggles, Upload / View buttons for HD assets and QR codes, a sequential Draft Versions list with status dots (active vs. pending), and Entrepreneur / Core Team approval status dropdowns. The Logo track Designer dropdown additionally offers an AI Generated option.
Summary
→
Brand
→
Logo
→
Business Card
→
Resources
→
Task Log

User Journey

Capture brand brief
Fessociate records storyline, current name, USP & tagline
→
Decide interventions
Fessociate flags “Need new brand name?” / “Need new logo?” & sets Market Scope
→
Brainstorm names
Fessociate proposes new name & justification (if advised)
→
Designer uploads drafts
Assigned Designer (or AI Generated) uploads Draft 1–3 per track
→
Dual approval
Entrepreneur Approval + Core Team Approval — both must be Approved
→
Deliver & log
Final files delivered; Task Log entry recorded
Brand Tab

Section I — Brand Identity Data (Assessment & positioning)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Enterprise NameData (read-only)—NoYesAuto-fetched from the linked entrepreneur / enterprise profile.Non-editable; sourced from profile.
Storyline of the EnterpriseText (read-only)—NoYesAuto-fetched from the enterprise profile; seeds the brand story & USP discussion.Non-editable; sourced from profile.
Current Brand NameDataFree textYesNoExisting brand/trade name in use today. Mirrored into the Summary “Current Brand Name” read-out.Required before track can be approved. Error: “Enter the current brand name.”
Need New Brand Name?Toggle (Yes/No)On / OffYesNoFlags whether a brand-name intervention is needed. When On, “New Brand Name” and “Justification” become required.If On and New Brand Name is blank → “Provide the proposed new brand name.”
New Brand Name (if advised)DataFree textConditionalNoProposed name; captured only when “Need New Brand Name?” is On. Mirrored into Summary “New Brand Name” (shown in teal).Required when toggle On. Error: “Proposed name required when a new brand name is advised.”
JustificationText (multi-line)Free textConditionalNoReason for the brand-name change; supports the entrepreneur/core-team decision.Required when “Need New Brand Name?” is On. Error: “Justification required for a brand change.”
USP of BrandDataFree textYesNoUnique selling proposition; mirrored into the Summary USP strip.Recommended before approval; error if blank at approval: “Enter the brand USP.”
Brand TaglineDataFree textYesNoShort tagline; reused on the Business Card (Brand Tagline auto-fetch) and Summary hero.—
Entrepreneur Media Folder LinkLink / URL (read-only)—NoYesAuto-fetched media-folder link for reference assets.Non-editable; sourced from profile.
Market ScopeSingle-select chip groupLocal / State | NationalYesNoExactly one scope selected; defaults to “Local / State”. Surfaced on Summary hero (Market Scope).One option must remain selected.

Section II — Implementation Status (Approvals)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Entrepreneur ApprovalSelectApproved | Pending | RejectedYesNoEntrepreneur's sign-off on the brand name/USP/tagline. Drives the Summary Brand-track entrepreneur status chip.Defaults to blank/Pending; must be Approved (alongside Core Team) to close the track.
Core Team ApprovalSelectApproved | Pending | RejectedYesNoCore team's sign-off. Drives the Summary Brand-track core status chip. Both approvals required to finalise.Defaults to blank/Pending; must be Approved to close the track.
The Brand track has no Designer/Drafts block — it is name & positioning text only. Dual approval here gates the downstream Logo and Business Card tracks, which reuse the confirmed name & tagline.

Section I — Existing Logo (Current state & intervention flag)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Existing Logo in HDAttach (Upload / View)Image fileYesNoHigh-definition copy of the current logo, if any. Upload and View actions.Recommended file format/HD; error if View used with no file: “No file uploaded.”
Open File (if available)Attach (Upload)Source / editable fileYesNoEditable source file (e.g. vector / .ai) when available.Optional.
Need New Logo?Toggle (Yes/No)On / OffYesNoFlags a logo intervention. When On, Designer assignment, Draft Versions and the Justification become relevant. Drives Summary “New Logo Needed” chip.If On and Justification blank → “Enter the reason a new logo is needed.”

Section II — Implementation Status (Designer · Drafts · Approvals)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
DesignerSelectDesigner A | Designer B | AI GeneratedYesNoAssigns the responsible Designer (or the AI Generated option) for logo drafts. Only the assigned Designer / core team may upload drafts.Required before uploading drafts. Error: “Assign a designer before uploading drafts.”
Draft 1Attach (Upload / View)Logo draft fileYes (Designer)NoFirst draft; active slot. Uploaded by the assigned Designer.At least one draft required before approval.
Draft 2Attach (Upload / View)Logo draft fileConditional (Designer)NoSequential — unlocks (Pending until then) after Draft 1 exists / is rejected.Optional; enabled in sequence.
Draft 3Attach (Upload / View)Logo draft fileConditional (Designer)NoSequential — unlocks (Pending until then) after Draft 2 exists / is rejected.Optional; enabled in sequence.
Entrepreneur ApprovalSelectApproved | Pending | RejectedYesNoEntrepreneur's sign-off on the selected logo draft. Drives Summary Logo-track entrepreneur chip.Must be Approved (with Core Team) to finalise; Rejected re-opens next draft.
Core Team ApprovalSelectApproved | Pending | RejectedYesNoCore team's sign-off. Drives Summary Logo-track core chip. Both approvals required to deliver final logo files.Must be Approved to finalise.
Draft Versions are sequential: only Draft 1 is initially active; Draft 2 and Draft 3 display as “Pending” until the prior draft is uploaded or rejected. Maximum 3 drafts per track.

Task Name — fixed dropdown (24 options)

#Task
01Awareness on Importance of Brand Identity
02Record Existing Brand Information
03Draft Brand Story & USP
04Brainstorm and Research Brand Name Ideas
05Brand Name Approved by Entrepreneur
06Final Brand Name & Tagline Confirmed

18Business Card Approved by Entrepreneur
19Deliver Business Card Files to Entrepreneur
20Business Card Printed by Entrepreneur Locally
21Business Card Printed and Collected from PRIME Office

23Follow-up on Business Card Usage and Reprinting Needs
24If others, specify
Key validations (whole framework): (1) Each implementation track (Brand, Logo, Business Card) is finalised only when both Entrepreneur Approval and Core Team Approval are Approved — a single Rejected blocks delivery and re-opens the next draft slot. (2) A Designer must be assigned before any draft can be uploaded; only the assigned Designer (or core team) holds upload permission. (3) Draft Versions are sequential and capped at 3 (Draft 2/3 stay “Pending” until the prior draft exists/is rejected). (4) “Need New Brand Name?” On → New Brand Name + Justification required; “Need New Logo?” On → Justification required. (5) Market Scope must always have exactly one chip selected. (6) Task Log rows require a Task Name selection; serial No. is system-generated. (7) Resources / Task Log child tables must retain at least one row.
Section Review:
PR-MT-002

Labels

Marketing Tools framework that captures the full label-design lifecycle for an entrepreneur's product — existing labels, master & variant-level label content, designer drafts and entrepreneur approval. Variants are not auto-prepopulated: each variant content / status row is created by selecting a variant from a dropdown sourced from the entrepreneur's Product Profile.
Key behaviour. The Variant Content and Label Status child tables are populated by choosing a variant from a dropdown fed by the Product Profile variant list — rows are not pre-created. A Wine / Alcohol Product? toggle on Master Content reveals the statutory alcohol warning block and an ABV field. Storyline text can be drafted by hand or generated by the ✨ Generate with AI action. The Summary tab is fully read-only and rolls up from the Existing and Required tabs.

On-Screen UI Structure

A teal title bar reads Labels Module with crumb PRIME Rural · Marketing Tools. Below sits a horizontal tab rail — Summary · Existing · Required · Resources · Task Log — the active tab highlighted teal.

Summary is read-only: a Variant-wise Overview table (one row per variant, the two right-most columns — Implementation Status & Approved — shaded terracotta / green), four aggregate cards (Existing / Cost / Interventions / Status) and a four-tile Intervention Flags strip.

Existing opens with an auto-fetched Entrepreneur chip, then an Existing Label child table whose Need New Label? column is a toggle; each row carries an Upload / View existing-file widget and a justification field.

Required holds two banded sections. The teal Label Content band wraps Master Content (product-wide fields including the Wine / Alcohol Product? toggle that reveals the statutory warning + ABV) and a nested Variant Content child table whose first column is a variant-selector dropdown (from Product Profile); the table also computes Volume from package L×B×H. The terracotta Label Status band carries a Designer dropdown, a Label Type dropdown and the Label Status child table (variant dropdown + Draft 1/2/3 upload & status cells + Approved-by-Entrepreneur checkbox).

Resources is a reference/asset placeholder. Task Log is a numbered activity table driven by a fixed 14-item task picklist.

Calculated fields (e.g. Volume from package dimensions) render italic on cream-deep background, are never editable and carry a formula tooltip (Rule 1).
Summary
→
Existing
→
Required
→
Resources
→
Task Log

User Journey

Open Labels
Marketing Tools → Labels (or EP dashboard card)
→
Record Existing labels
upload file, toggle Need New Label?, justify
→
Fill Master Content
storyline (AI optional), alcohol toggle → warning + ABV
→
Select variants & add content
dropdown from Product Profile; dims auto Volume
→
Assign designer & track drafts
Label Status: D1/D2/D3 + status, EP approval
→
Log activity & Save
Task Log; Summary recalculates
Tab 1: Summary ▼

Entirely read-only. Auto-populated from the Existing and Required tabs — no direct data entry. Recalculates on every switch back to Summary and on any change in Existing / Required. One row per variant present in the Label Status table; falls back to Existing-tab rows if Required is not yet filled.

Variant-wise Overview Table

One row per variant. Intervention Type shows the furthest draft raised; Implementation Status shows the latest draft's approval state.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
VariantRead-only text (bold)—AutofillVariant name selected in the Label Status table; “Not set” (italic) when blank.— (display only)
New Label NeededRead-only badgeYes / —AutofillTerracotta “Yes” when the matching Existing-tab row's Need New Label? toggle is ON; else “—”. Matched by variant name.—
Label Dim.Read-only text—AutofillFrom the matching Variant Content row's Label Dim. (l×b); “—” if not set.—
Designer NameRead-only text—AutofillSection-level Designer selected in Label Status; same for all rows; “—” if unset.—
Label TypeRead-only textSticker / Tag / Pouch PrintingAutofillSection-level Label Type selected in Label Status; “—” if unset.—
Intervention TypeRead-only badge✓ Draft 1 / Draft 2 / Draft 3 / —AutofillFurthest draft reached — the highest-numbered draft whose Status ≠ Pending. “—” if no draft started.—
Implementation Status [shaded]Read-only badgePending / Under Review / Redo / Approved / Not ApprovedAutofillStatus of the latest non-Pending draft. Colour map: Approved → teal, Under Review → amber, Redo → terracotta, Not Approved → red, Pending → neutral.—
Approved [shaded]Read-only badge✓ Yes / —AutofillGreen “✓ Yes” when the row's Approved-by-Entrepreneur checkbox is ticked; else “—”.—
Each variant is matched across tabs by its name; the row therefore aligns the Existing-tab Need New Label? flag, the Variant Content dimensions and the Label Status draft/approval state into a single read-only line.

Aggregate Cards (4)

Card / MetricField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Existing → Total VariantsRead-only number—CalculatedCount of rows in the Existing Label table.—
Existing → Need New LabelRead-only number (amber)—CalculatedCount of Existing rows with the Need New Label? toggle ON.—
Existing → No Change NeededRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Existing rows with the toggle OFF.—
Existing → Product NameRead-only text—AutofillProduct Name from Master Content; “—” if blank.—
Cost → Label TypeRead-only textSticker / Tag / Pouch PrintingAutofillSelected Label Type; “—” if unset.—
Cost → DesignerRead-only text—AutofillSelected Designer; “—” if unset.—
Cost → Variants with Label Dim SetRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Variant Content rows where both l and b are entered (Label Dim ≠ “—”).—
Interventions → Total Variants in DesignRead-only number—CalculatedCount of rows in the Label Status table.—
Interventions → Draft 1 RaisedRead-only number—CalculatedCount of variants whose furthest non-Pending draft is Draft 1.—
Interventions → Draft 2 RaisedRead-only number—CalculatedCount whose furthest non-Pending draft is Draft 2.—
Interventions → Draft 3 RaisedRead-only number—CalculatedCount whose furthest non-Pending draft is Draft 3.—
Status → Approved by EntrepreneurRead-only number (green)—CalculatedCount of Label Status rows with the Approved checkbox ticked.—
Status → Under ReviewRead-only number (amber)—CalculatedCount whose latest non-Pending draft status = Under Review.—
Status → RedoRead-only number (amber)—CalculatedCount whose latest status = Redo.—
Status → PendingRead-only number—CalculatedCount whose latest status = Pending (no draft started).—
Status → Not ApprovedRead-only number (red)—CalculatedCount whose latest status = Not Approved.—

Intervention Flags Strip (4 tiles)

TileField TypeInput / AutofillLogic
! Need New LabelRead-only count (amber icon)Calculated= Existing rows with Need New Label? ON.
✐ In Design ProcessRead-only count (blue icon)Calculated= Total rows in the Label Status table.
✓ ApprovedRead-only count (green icon)Calculated= Label Status rows with Approved checkbox ticked.
↻ Redo / Not ApprovedRead-only count (amber icon)Calculated= count(latest status = Redo) + count(latest status = Not Approved).
Tab 2: Existing ▼

Captures the entrepreneur's current labels per variant. Opens with a read-only Entrepreneur chip, then the Existing Label child table. “+ Add Row” appends a row; at least one row must remain.

Entrepreneur

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Entrepreneur NameRead-only text—AutofillAuto-fetched from the entrepreneur profile (read-only, Rule 2).—

Existing Label (child table)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
VariantTextFree textInputVariant name as it appears on the current label; matched by name to Summary & Variant Content rows.“Enter variant.”
Upload Existing FileFile upload + ViewImage / PDFInputUpload the existing label artwork; View opens the stored file.Accept image / PDF only.
Need New Label?Toggle (Yes / No)On / OffInputON flags this variant for a new label — feeds the Summary “New Label Needed” badge, the Need-New-Label aggregate and the flags strip.—
JustifyTextFree textInputReason a new label is needed (e.g. “Old label missing FSSAI number”).Recommended when Need New Label? = Yes.
The Need New Label? toggle is the trigger that surfaces a variant as requiring design work in the Summary roll-up.
Tab 3: Required ▼

Two banded sections. Section I — Label Content (teal band) holds Master Content (product-wide) plus a nested Variant Content child table. Section II — Label Status (terracotta band) holds Designer / Label Type selectors and the Label Status child table. Variants in both child tables are added by selecting from a dropdown sourced from the Product Profile — rows are not pre-created.

Section I.a — Master Content

Product-wide label content shared across all variants. Applies once per product.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Product Name *TextFree textInput (may fetch from Product Profile)Drives the Summary “Product Name” aggregate.“Product Name is required.”
Product SnapshotTextareaFree textInputShort descriptive snapshot of the product.—
Storyline Text for LabelTextarea + ✨ Generate with AIFree textInput / AI-generatedThe brand story printed on the label. “✨ Generate with AI” drafts the text via the AI service; the user may edit afterwards.—
Product TaglineTextFree textInputShort marketing tagline.—
Benefits of the ProductTextareaFree textInputKey product benefits for the label.—
Ingredients / MaterialTextareaFree textInputIngredient list (food) or material composition (non-food).—
Directions of UseTextareaFree textInputUsage instructions printed on the label.—
Product PicturesMulti-file upload + ViewImagesInputOne or more product photographs for the label.Accept image files only.
Manufactured by → NameTextFree textInputName of the manufacturing entity.—
Manufactured by → FSSAI NumberTextFSSAI licence no.InputFSSAI registration of the manufacturer (food products).Validate FSSAI number format if entered.
Marketed by → NameTextFree textInputName of the marketing entity.—
Marketed by → FSSAI NumberTextFSSAI licence no.InputFSSAI registration of the marketer (food products).Validate FSSAI number format if entered.
Wine / Alcohol Product?Toggle (Yes / No)On / OffInputConditional reveal: ON unhides the Alcohol block below (statutory warning + ABV). OFF hides and clears it.—
