Statutory Alcohol Warning [conditional]Read-only bannerFixed textAutofillShown only when the alcohol toggle is ON. Fixed text: “Consumption of alcohol is injurious to health / Be safe — Don't drink and drive.”—
Alcohol Content (ABV) [conditional]Texte.g. 12% ABVInputVisible only when the alcohol toggle is ON.Required when Wine / Alcohol Product? = Yes.
Alcohol rule. Turning the Wine / Alcohol Product? toggle ON reveals the mandatory statutory warning banner and the ABV field; turning it OFF hides and clears both.

Section I.b — Variant Content (child table)

Per-variant label data. “+ Add Row” appends a row; the first column is a variant-selector dropdown populated from the Product Profile (variants are selected, not auto-prepopulated).

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Variant Name *Select (dropdown from Product Profile)Product Profile variant listInput (selected)Variant chosen from the dropdown of Product Profile variants — not auto-prepopulated. Matched by name to Summary & Label Status rows.“Select a variant.”
Label Dim. (l×b)Two Numbers (l, b)≥ 0InputLabel artwork dimensions; feeds the Summary Label Dim. column & the “Variants with Label Dim Set” aggregate (counted when both l and b are entered).“Cannot be negative.”
Net Wt.Textg / mlInputNet weight / volume printed on the label.—
MFDText (date)DD/MM/YYInputManufacture date.Validate date format.
Best BeforeText (date)DD/MM/YYInputBest-before / expiry date.Validate date format; should be after MFD.
MRPCurrency (₹)≥ 0InputMaximum retail price printed on the label.“Cannot be negative.”
Batch No.TextFree textInputBatch / lot number.—
Pkg Dimensions (L×B×H)Three Numbers (L, B, H)≥ 0InputPackage dimensions; drives the calculated Volume below.“Cannot be negative.”
Volume [calculated]Read-only (italic, cream-deep)—CalculatedVolume = L × B × H (2 decimals); blank until all three dimensions > 0. Read-only with formula tooltip (Rule 1).—
Packaging PhotoFile upload + ViewImageInputPhoto of the packaged product.Accept image files only.
360° Video LinkFile upload + ViewVideo / URLInput360° product video for the label / catalogue.—
DPP FileFile upload + ViewFileInputDigital Product Passport file for the variant.—

Section II.a — Label Status: Designer & Type

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Add DesignerSelectDesigner master listInput (selected)Section-level designer assigned to the product; surfaces as Designer Name across all Summary rows.“Select designer” default carries no value.
Label TypeSelectSticker / Tag / Pouch PrintingInput (selected)Section-level label type; surfaces as Label Type across all Summary rows.“Select label type” default carries no value.

Section II.b — Label Status (child table)

Tracks up to three design drafts per variant and final entrepreneur approval. “+ Add Row” appends a row; the first column is a variant-selector dropdown from the Product Profile.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Variant Name *Select (dropdown from Product Profile)Product Profile variant listInput (selected)Variant chosen from the dropdown — not auto-prepopulated. Drives one Summary row each.“Select a variant.”
Draft 1File upload + ViewImage / PDFInputFirst design draft artwork.Accept image / PDF only.
Status (Draft 1)Select (terracotta chip)Pending / Under Review / Redo / Approved / Not ApprovedInputApproval state of Draft 1; default Pending.—
Draft 2File upload + ViewImage / PDFInputSecond design draft artwork.Accept image / PDF only.
Status (Draft 2)Select (terracotta chip)Pending / Under Review / Redo / Approved / Not ApprovedInputApproval state of Draft 2; default Pending.—
Draft 3File upload + ViewImage / PDFInputThird design draft artwork.Accept image / PDF only.
Status (Draft 3)Select (terracotta chip)Pending / Under Review / Redo / Approved / Not ApprovedInputApproval state of Draft 3; default Pending. The latest non-Pending draft drives Summary Implementation Status & Intervention Type.—
Approved by EntrepreneurCheckboxTicked / UntickedInputFinal sign-off by the entrepreneur; feeds the Summary Approved badge, Approved aggregate and flags tile.—
Tab 4: Resources ▼

Reference view — reference materials, design assets, brand guidelines and label templates for the entrepreneur and designer. Placeholder in the current mockup; no data-entry fields.

Tab 5: Task Log ▼

Chronological log of label-related activities. “+ Add Entry” appends a row; the row number auto-renumbers on delete. The entry count is shown in the section heading.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Read-only number—AutofillSequential, zero-padded (01, 02…); auto-renumbers on row delete.—
DateDate pickerCalendar dateInputDate the activity occurred.—
Task NameSelect (fixed picklist)14-item label task list (see below)Input (selected)Standard label-lifecycle task chosen from a fixed dropdown.“Select Task.”
Notes / OutcomeTextFree textInputNotes or outcome for the logged task.—
AttachmentFile uploadFileInputOptional supporting file for the entry.—
Task Name picklist (14 fixed options): 01 Awareness on Importance of Labels · 02 Data Collection for Label Creation · 03 Brainstorm and Sketch Label Ideas · 04 Designer briefing on Label · 05 Draft coordinations with Designer · 06 Label approved by Core Team · 07 Label approved by Entrepreneur · 08 Label Finalized · 09 Delivered Label Files to Entrepreneur · 10 Labels printed and collected by Entrepreneur locally · 11 Labels printed and delivered in PRIME office · 12 Labels printed and delivered to the Entrepreneur · 13 Follow up on Label usage and printing · 14 If others, specify in comments.
Key validations (whole form): Product Name is required on Master Content. Variant Name must be selected (from the Product Profile dropdown) on every Variant Content and Label Status row — rows are never auto-created. When Wine / Alcohol Product? is ON, the statutory warning banner is shown and Alcohol Content (ABV) is required. Each child table must retain at least one row (“At least one row is required” on delete of the last row). File uploads accept image / PDF (artwork) or image-only (photos) as noted. Date fields use DD/MM/YY; Best Before should fall after MFD. Numeric dimensions, MRP and weights cannot be negative; Volume is auto-calculated (L×B×H) and read-only. Variants are matched across the Existing, Variant Content and Label Status tables by name to assemble the Summary roll-up.
Section Review:
PR-MT-003

Brochure

A Marketing Tools framework that captures an entrepreneur's brochure / catalogue content — setup details, the list of products featured in the brochure (with briefs, dimensions, price, MOQ and product pictures) and the draft-by-draft design status — and tracks the Designer's involvement from data collection through entrepreneur approval and printing.
Key behaviour. The Brochure Type chosen at setup is a multi-select; the same set of types is mirrored down into the Product Listings child table so each product can be tagged with the brochure type(s) it should appear in. A product is considered “included” in a brochure only when at least one Brochure Type is selected on its listing row. Design work runs through up to three drafts; the brochure is final only when Approved by Entrepreneur is ticked.

On-Screen UI Structure

A teal title bar reads Brochures Module with crumb PRIME Rural · Marketing Tools. Below it a horizontal tab rail — Summary · Existing · Required · Resources · Task Log — with the active tab highlighted teal.

Summary is read-only: a Brochure-wise Overview table (one row per brochure, with the two right-most columns — Implementation Status and Approved — shaded terracotta/green), four aggregate cards (Existing / Cost / Interventions / Status) and a four-tile Intervention Flags strip.

Existing opens with an Entrepreneur chip (Entrepreneur Name + Enterprise Name, both auto-fetched from profile, shown italic on the cream-deep background), followed by the Existing Brochure child table where each current brochure/catalogue is listed, its file uploaded, and a Need New Brochure? toggle plus justification recorded.

Required is split into two banner-blocks. Brochure Details (teal banner) holds the Brochure Setup form (enterprise, multi-select Brochure Type, Market Scope, tagline, AI-assisted storyline, about, contact, email, address) and a nested Product Listings child table — the brochure's product listing grid carrying per-product brochure type(s), product name, brief, dimensions, price, MOQ and a multi-file product picture upload/view cell. Brochure Status (terracotta banner) holds Designer, Print Format and Page Count fields and the draft-tracking child table (Draft 1/2/3 file uploads, each with a status dropdown, plus the Approved-by-Entrepreneur checkbox).

Resources is a placeholder for brochure templates, design references, print specifications and brand guidelines. Task Log is a numbered activity grid driven by a fixed 12-item brochure task list.

Calculated / read-only fields (Entrepreneur Name, Enterprise Name, every Summary metric) render italic on the cream-deep background and are never editable; each Summary roll-up carries a derivation tooltip (Rule 1).
Summary
→
Existing
→
Required
→
Resources
→
Task Log

User Journey

Open Brochures
Marketing Tools → Brochure (entrepreneur context)
→
Record existing brochures
Existing tab — upload file, flag Need New + justify
→
Set up brochure & product listings
Required — type(s), storyline, products + pictures
→
Assign Designer & raise drafts
Brochure Status — Draft 1/2/3 + status
→
Entrepreneur approves
tick Approved by Entrepreneur
→
Log activity & Save
Task Log; Summary recalculates
Tab 1: Summary ▼

Entirely read-only. Auto-populated from the Existing and Required tabs — no direct data entry. Recalculates on every switch back to Summary and on any change in Existing / Required (toggles, status dropdowns, selections).

Brochure-wise Overview Table

One row per brochure (sourced from Brochure Status rows). Product Names lists all products marked as included; Intervention Stage shows the furthest draft raised.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Brochure TitleRead-only text (bold)—AutofillFrom the Brochure Status row title; shows italic “Untitled” if blank.— (display only)
Product NamesRead-only list—AutofillBulleted list of every Product Listing row that has at least one Brochure Type selected and a chosen Product Name; “—” if none.—
Brochure TypeRead-only text—AutofillComma-joined list of the master Brochure Type multi-select; “—” if none chosen.—
Market ScopeRead-only textLocal / State · NationalAutofillFrom Brochure Setup → Market Scope; “—” until chosen.—
DesignerRead-only textDesigner A / Designer B / AI GeneratedAutofillFrom Brochure Status → Add Designer.—
Intervention StageRead-only badge✐ Draft 1 / ✐ Draft 2 / ✐ Draft 3 / —AutofillFurthest non-Pending draft on the row: Draft 3 wins over Draft 2 over Draft 1. “—” if all three drafts are Pending.—
Implementation Status [shaded]Read-only status pillPending / Under Review / Redo / Approved / Not ApprovedAutofillStatus of the latest non-Pending draft (else Pending). Colour map: Approved → teal, Under Review → amber, Redo → terracotta, Not Approved → red, Pending → neutral.—
Approved [shaded]Read-only badge✓ Yes / —AutofillGreen “✓ Yes” when the row's Approved by Entrepreneur checkbox is ticked, else “—”.—
Empty state: when no Brochure Status rows exist the table shows “Add brochures in Required tab → Brochure Status to populate”.

Aggregate Cards (4) & Intervention Flags (4)

Card / MetricField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Existing → Total Existing BrochuresRead-only number—CalculatedCount of rows in the Existing Brochure table.—
Existing → Need New BrochureRead-only number (amber)—CalculatedCount of Existing rows whose Need New Brochure? toggle is ON.—
Existing → No Change NeededRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Existing rows whose toggle is OFF.—
Existing → Products IncludedRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Product Listing rows that have at least one Brochure Type selected.—
Cost → Brochure TypeRead-only text—AutofillMaster Brochure Type multi-select, comma-joined; “—” if none.—
Cost → Market ScopeRead-only textLocal / State · NationalAutofillFrom Brochure Setup.—
Cost → Print FormatRead-only textA4 / A5 / DL (99 × 210 mm) / CustomAutofillFrom Brochure Status → Print Format.—
Cost → Page CountRead-only number—AutofillFrom Brochure Status → Page Count; “—” if blank.—
Cost → DesignerRead-only text—AutofillFrom Brochure Status → Add Designer.—
Interventions → Total in DesignRead-only number—CalculatedSum of Draft 1 + Draft 2 + Draft 3 counts (= rows with at least one non-Pending draft, counted at their furthest draft).—
Interventions → Draft 1 / 2 / 3 RaisedRead-only number—CalculatedEach Brochure Status row is counted once, against its furthest non-Pending draft only (Draft 3 → Draft 2 → Draft 1).—
Status → Approved by EntrepreneurRead-only number (green)—CalculatedCount of Brochure Status rows with the Approved checkbox ticked.—
Status → Under Review / Redo / Pending / Not ApprovedRead-only number (amber / amber / neutral / red)—CalculatedCount of rows whose latest draft status equals the named value.—
Flags → Need New BrochureRead-only flag count (amber)—Calculated= Existing rows with toggle ON.—
Flags → In Design ProcessRead-only flag count (blue)—Calculated= total number of Brochure Status rows.—
Flags → ApprovedRead-only flag count (green)—Calculated= rows with Approved checkbox ticked.—
Flags → Redo / Not ApprovedRead-only flag count (amber)—Calculated= rows whose latest status is Redo, plus rows whose latest status is Not Approved.—
All Summary tiles use Indian number formatting (e.g. 1,00,000). Every metric is read-only and recomputed live — no manual override.
Tab 2: Existing ▼

Captures the entrepreneur's current brochures / catalogues and whether each one needs to be re-created. Opens with a read-only Entrepreneur chip.

Entrepreneur (chip)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Entrepreneur NameRead-only text—AutofillAuto fetched from profile (italic on cream-deep background, Rule 2).— (display only)
Enterprise NameRead-only text—AutofillAuto fetched from profile.—

Existing Brochure (child table)

Repeatable; at least one row. “+ Add Row” appends a blank row, “✕” deletes (blocked when only one row remains).

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Brochure / Catalogue TitleText inputFree textInputName of the existing brochure or catalogue. Feeds the Need-New map keyed by title.Recommended before saving; blank title still counts toward Total Existing.
Upload Existing FileFile upload + ViewPDF / image / documentInput“Upload” opens the file picker; “View ▷” previews the attached file.Single file per row; recommended where the brochure exists.
Need New Brochure?Toggle (on / off)On = needs new · Off = no changeInputON flags this brochure for re-creation; drives the Need-New aggregate and the Need New Brochure flag tile.—
JustifyText inputFree textInputReason a new brochure is needed (e.g. “Outdated design, no QR code”).Expected when Need New Brochure? = On.
Seed examples shown in the mockup: “Herbal Products Catalogue” (Need New: On — outdated design, no QR code), “Soap Product Flyer” (Need New: On — products added since last print), “Price List 2024” (Need New: Off).
Tab 3: Required ▼

Two banner-blocks. I — Brochure Details (teal banner: “Setup + product listings”) holds the Brochure Setup form and the nested Product Listings child table. II — Brochure Status (terracotta banner: “Designer · Drafts · Approval”) holds Designer/format fields and the draft-tracking table.

I. Brochure Details → Brochure Setup

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Enterprise NameText inputFree textInputEnterprise the brochure is for.Required before raising a draft — “Enter the enterprise name.”
Brochure TypeMulti-select list (size 5)Single Product Brochure / Multi-Product Catalogue / Tri-fold Leaflet / Bi-fold Leaflet / Single-page FlyerInputHold Ctrl / Cmd to pick more than one. The chosen set is mirrored into every Product Listing row's per-product Brochure Type list. Feeds the Brochure Type summary metric.At least one type recommended; drives product-level options.
Market ScopeDropdownLocal / State · NationalInputDefault placeholder “Select scope”. Feeds Market Scope summary.—
Brochure Tagline / HeadlineText inputFree textInputHeadline printed on the brochure.—
Brochure Storyline (AI)Textarea + AI buttonFree text / AI-generatedInput“✨ Generate with AI” produces a suggested storyline the user can edit (Apoorva AI assist).—
About the EnterpriseTextareaFree textInputShort description of the enterprise for the brochure.—
Contact NumberText inputPhone numberInputPrinted contact number.Numeric / valid phone format expected.
EmailText inputEmail addressInputPrinted contact email.Valid email format expected.
Address / LocationTextareaFree textInputPrinted enterprise address / location.—

I. Brochure Details → Product Listings (child table)

The list of products that feature in the brochure. “+ Add Product” appends a row; each row's Brochure Type list is kept in sync with the master multi-select. A product counts as included only when at least one Brochure Type is selected on its row.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Brochure TypeMulti-select list (size 3)Single Product Brochure / Multi-Product Catalogue / Tri-fold Leaflet / Bi-fold Leaflet / Single-page FlyerInputWhich brochure type(s) this product appears in. Options mirror the master Brochure Type selection. Selecting any value marks the product as included & surfaces it in Summary → Product Names.Select ≥ 1 type for the product to feature in the brochure.
Product NameDropdownLinked product list (e.g. Turmeric Soap, Neem Face Wash)InputProduct to feature; chosen from the entrepreneur's products. Feeds the Summary Product Names list.Required when a Brochure Type is selected — “Choose the product.”
Product BriefTextarea (2 rows)Free textInputShort description / selling points printed for the product.—
DimensionsText inputL × B × H (e.g. 10×6×4 cm)InputPhysical dimensions of the product.—
PriceText inputCurrency (₹, e.g. ₹120)InputSelling price shown on the brochure. Use Indian number formatting (e.g. ₹1,20,000).Numeric / currency value expected.
MOQText inputQuantity (e.g. 12 pcs)InputMinimum order quantity for the product.—
Product PicturesMulti-file upload + ViewImage files (multiple)InputOne or more product photos for the brochure (the picture / asset set per product). “Upload” allows multiple files; “View ▷” previews them.At least one image recommended per featured product.
Seed examples: “Turmeric Soap” (Multi-Product Catalogue; 10×6×4 cm; ₹120; 12 pcs) and “Neem Face Wash” (Multi-Product Catalogue + Single-page Flyer; 8×5×5 cm; ₹180; 6 pcs).

II. Brochure Status → Header fields

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Add DesignerDropdownDesigner A / Designer B / AI GeneratedInputDesigner responsible for the brochure (or “AI Generated”). Feeds Designer summary metric.Required before raising drafts — “Assign a designer.”
Print FormatDropdownA4 / A5 / DL (99 × 210 mm) / CustomInputIntended print format. Feeds Print Format summary.—
Page CountNumber inputInteger (e.g. 4)InputNumber of pages. Feeds Page Count summary.Whole number ≥ 1 expected.

II. Brochure Status → Draft tracking (child table)

Terracotta-headed table. One row per brochure; up to three drafts, each with its own file upload and status dropdown, closing with the entrepreneur-approval checkbox. “+ Add Row” appends; “✕” deletes.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Brochure TitleText inputFree textInputTitle of the brochure being designed; surfaces as the Summary overview row title.Recommended — rows without a title show “Untitled” in Summary.
Draft 1File upload + ViewPDF / image / design fileInputFirst design draft from the Designer. “View ▷” previews.—
Status (Draft 1)Dropdown (terracotta chip)Pending / Under Review / Redo / Approved / Not ApprovedInputReview outcome of Draft 1. Defaults to Pending.—
Draft 2File upload + ViewPDF / image / design fileInputSecond draft, raised after Draft 1 feedback.—
Status (Draft 2)Dropdown (terracotta chip)Pending / Under Review / Redo / Approved / Not ApprovedInputReview outcome of Draft 2.—
Draft 3File upload + ViewPDF / image / design fileInputThird / final draft.—
Status (Draft 3)Dropdown (terracotta chip)Pending / Under Review / Redo / Approved / Not ApprovedInputReview outcome of Draft 3. The latest non-Pending draft drives Implementation Status & Intervention Stage in Summary.—
Approved by EntrepreneurCheckboxTicked / untickedInputFinal sign-off by the entrepreneur. Drives the Approved metric & Approved flag tile.Tick only after a draft is Approved.
Seed examples: “Herbal Products Catalogue” (Draft 1 Approved, Draft 2 Approved, Draft 3 Under Review — not yet entrepreneur-approved) and “Soap Product Flyer” (Draft 1 Approved, Draft 2 Redo, Draft 3 Pending).
Tab 4: Resources ▼

Placeholder reference view for the Brochure framework.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ResourcesReference panel (placeholder)—Read-onlyBrochure templates, design references, print specifications and brand guidelines for the entrepreneur and Designer.— (content to be defined)
Tab 5: Task Log ▼

Numbered activity log for brochure work (header shows live entry count). “+ Add Entry” appends an auto-numbered row; deleting renumbers the remaining rows.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Read-only number—AutofillAuto-incrementing two-digit sequence (01, 02, …); recomputed on delete.—
DateDate pickerCalendar dateInputDate the task was performed.—
Task NameDropdown (fixed list)See Brochure Task List belowInputPick from the 12 standard brochure tasks; default “— Select Task —”.Select a task before saving.
Notes / OutcomeText inputFree textInputOutcome / remarks for the task (used for “If others, specify”).—
AttachmentFile uploadAny fileInputOptional supporting file for the task.—
Brochure Task List (fixed dropdown): 01 Awareness on Importance of Brochure · 02 Data Collection for Brochure Creation · 03 Brainstorm and Sketch Brochure Ideas · 04 Draft coordinations with Designer · 05 Brochure approved by Core Team · 06 Brochure approved by Entrepreneur · 07 Delivered Brochure Files to Entrepreneur · 08 Brochure printed and collected by Entrepreneur locally · 09 Brochure printed and delivered in PRIME office · 10 Brochure printed and delivered to the Entrepreneur via local cabs · 11 Follow up on Brochure usage and printing · 12 If others, specify in comments.
Key validations (whole form): Enterprise Name and at least one Brochure Type must be set before raising a draft; each Product Listing row that carries a Brochure Type must also have a Product Name (and at least one Product Picture is recommended); a Designer must be assigned before drafts are uploaded; Page Count must be a whole number ≥ 1; Contact Number and Email must be valid; Status defaults to Pending and a draft must reach Approved before the Approved-by-Entrepreneur checkbox is ticked; the Existing Brochure and Brochure Status tables must each retain at least one row (delete is blocked on the last row); a Need-New justification is expected whenever the Need New Brochure? toggle is On.
Section Review:
PR-PRC-001

Unit Pricing

The Pricing module's cost-buildup engine. It sums seven cost components (raw material, human resource/labour, packaging, transportation, machine depreciation, gas, and rent/electricity overheads) into a Cost per Unit, then applies one of three pricing methods (Cost Plus / Competitor Based / Value Based) across two channels (Offline / Online) to derive Wholesale and Retail prices. It is the heaviest Golden-Thread consumer in the system, and the Retail Price it produces flows back to the Product Profile variants.
Golden-Thread hub. Pulls RM rate & unit (Raw Materials), labour rate & unit (HR), packaging rate & unit (Packaging), machine depreciation rate (Machinery), rent & electricity per month (Infrastructure / Business Establishment profile), and competitor average prices (Market Research). Pushes the derived Retail Price back to Product Profile variants. Existing-tab cost feeds Existing Unit Pricing; the matching SKU's Optimized-tab cost feeds the Summary's saving comparison.

On-Screen UI Structure

A teal title bar reads Unit Pricing Module with crumb PRIME Rural · Pricing Tools. Below it sits a horizontal tab rail — Summary · Existing · Optimized · Resources · Task Log — the active tab highlighted teal.

The Existing and Optimized tabs each hold a list of collapsible product blocks. Each block has a sea-green header carrying an inline SKU selector (auto-fetched from the Product module), a meta label, and a Remove button; clicking the header collapses/expands the body. The block body is a vertical stack of nine Roman-numbered cost-buildup sections: I Raw Materials, II Human Resource, III Packaging, IV Transportation, V Machine Depreciation, VI Gas, VII Others (Rent + Electricity), VIII Unit Cost, and IX Pricing Strategy.

Sections I, II, III & V are child-table grids (No. / item-select / Qty / Unit / Rate / Amount / delete) with an + Add Row button and a highlighted Subtotal strip. Section IV is two transport inputs + subtotal; Section VI is a single Gas row; Section VII is two overhead blocks (Rent, Electricity) each pulling a from-BE monthly figure. Section VIII is the teal gradient Unit Cost callout stacking Total Input Cost, Total Units Produced, Per-Unit Overheads and Cost per Unit. Section IX has a Pricing Method dropdown driving a two-column channel grid (Offline | Online); each channel card shows only the method block selected (A / B / C) and a gradient summary strip (Offline = Wholesale + Retail, Online = Retail only).

Calculated fields (every Amount, every Subtotal, Cost per Unit, Selling/Wholesale, Retail, Rent/Unit, Electricity/Unit) render italic on cream-deep background, are never editable, and carry a formula tooltip (Rule 1). Fetched fields (Unit, Rate, Rent per month, Electricity per month, Competitor Avg when a scope is picked) show read-only with a "Fetched from X" source note (Rule 2). The Summary tab is a fully read-only roll-up.
Summary
→
Existing
→
Optimized
→
Resources
→
Task Log

User Journey

Open Unit Pricing & add product
select SKU from Product module
→
Build cost (I–VII)
add rows; Rate/Unit auto-fetched
→
Enter Total Units Produced
Section VIII derives Cost per Unit
→
Choose pricing method
Cost Plus / Competitor / Value
→
Set markups / margins per channel
Offline & Online; Wholesale + Retail derived
→
Save — push Retail to Product
Summary & variant prices update
Tab 1: Summary ▼

Entirely read-only. Auto-populated from the Existing tab (one row per priced SKU) with the Optimized tab supplying the matched-SKU optimized cost for the saving comparison. Recalculates on every switch back to Summary and on any change in Existing/Optimized.

SKU-wise Overview Table

One row per product; prices read from the active pricing method's channel summary. Optimized columns shaded green.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
SKURead-only text—AutofillFrom the block's selected SKU; rows with no SKU are skipped.— (display only)
ProductRead-only text (bold)—AutofillProduct name resolved from the selected SKU (block meta label).—
Existing Cost / Unit (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedCost per Unit from this block's Section VIII; “—” if 0.—
Optimized Cost / Unit (₹) [shaded]Read-only currency (green)—CalculatedCost per Unit from the Optimized-tab block whose SKU matches this row; “—” if no match.—
Difference (₹) [shaded]Read-only (green/red)—CalculatedDifference = Optimized Cost/Unit − Existing Cost/Unit (only when both > 0). Down-arrow & green when ≤ 0 (a saving), up-arrow & red when > 0 (cost increase); “—” otherwise.—
Pricing MethodRead-only badgeCost Plus / Competitor / Value Based / —AutofillMirrors Section IX method. Cost Plus → blue, Competitor → teal, Value → amber, none → “—”.—
Offline Wholesale (₹) [shaded]Read-only currency—CalculatedOffline card Wholesale summary; “—” if 0.—
Offline Retail (₹) [shaded]Read-only currency—CalculatedOffline card Retail summary; “—” if 0.—
Online Retail (₹) [shaded]Read-only currency—CalculatedOnline card Retail summary; “—” if 0. (Online has no Wholesale.)—

Aggregate Cards (4)

Card / MetricField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Existing → Total SKUsRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Existing blocks with a SKU selected.—
Existing → Total Units ProducedRead-only number—CalculatedSum of each block's Total Units Produced (Section VIII).—
Existing → SKUs with Cost CalculatedRead-only number (green)—CalculatedCount where Cost per Unit > 0.—
Existing → SKUs with Price SetRead-only number (green)—CalculatedCount where Offline Retail > 0 OR Online Retail > 0.—
Existing → SKUs Not Yet PricedRead-only number (amber)—Calculated= Total SKUs − SKUs with Price Set.—
Cost → Avg Existing Cost / Unit (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedAverage of Cost per Unit across blocks where it is > 0; “—” if none.—
Cost → Avg Optimized Cost / Unit (₹)Read-only currency (green)—CalculatedAverage of matched Optimized-tab Cost per Unit (> 0); “—” if none.—
Cost → Avg Saving / Unit (₹)Read-only (green/red)—Calculated= average of (Optimized − Existing) over SKUs with both set. Down-arrow if ≤ 0 (saving), up-arrow if > 0; “—” if none.—
Interventions → Cost Plus / Competitor Based / Value BasedRead-only numbers—CalculatedCount of blocks by selected Pricing Method.—
Interventions → Priced for Offline / OnlineRead-only numbers—CalculatedCount where Offline Retail > 0 / Online Retail > 0 respectively.—
Status → Highest / Lowest Offline Retail (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedMax / Min of Offline Retail across all priced blocks; “—” if none.—
Status → Highest / Lowest Online Retail (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedMax / Min of Online Retail across all priced blocks; “—” if none.—
Footer: a four-tile Intervention Flags strip mirrors the aggregates — SKUs Priced (= SKUs with Price Set), Cost Plus, Competitor Based, Value Based counts.
Tab 2: Existing — Cost Buildup & Pricing (per product) ▼

The working tab. A list of collapsible product blocks, each running the full nine-section cost buildup for the current (as-is) cost structure. + Add Product seeds a block with one row in each child table.

Product Block Header

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
SKUSelect (link to Product)SKU · Product Name listInputOptions fetched from the Product module's SKU list (e.g. VI-BS-B · Bamboo Shoot Pickle 200g). Selecting one sets the block meta label to the product name.Required before the block can feed Summary; unselected blocks are skipped in roll-ups.
Product (meta label)Read-only text—AutofillProduct name resolved from the chosen SKU; shows “No SKU selected” when blank.—
Remove ProductAction button—InputDeletes the whole block after a confirm prompt.Confirm: “Remove this product?”

Section I — Raw Materials (child table)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Read-only sequence—CalculatedAuto row index; renumbered on delete.—
Raw MaterialSelectBamboo Shoot / Oil / Modi masala / Salt / Chilli powder / Bamboo strips / Eri silk yarn / Lakadong turmericInputOptions fetched from Raw Materials module. Selecting auto-fills Unit and Rate.Required for the row to count.
QtyNumber—InputQuantity consumed per batch in the material's unit.Numeric ≥ 0; blank treated as 0.
UnitRead-only textgms / ml / pcs ...AutofillFetched from Raw Materials for the selected material.— (read-only)
Rate (₹)Read-only currency—AutofillFetched from Raw Materials (rate per unit, e.g. Bamboo Shoot ₹0.50/gm).— (read-only)
Amount (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedAmount = Qty × Rate.—
Subtotal — Raw Materials (₹)Read-only currency—Calculated= ∑ row Amounts. Feeds Total Input Cost (Section VIII).—
Sections II (Human Resource), III (Packaging) and V (Machine Depreciation) use the identical grid & formula (Amount = Qty × Rate; Subtotal = ∑ Amounts), differing only in source & option list:

Section II — Human Resource / Labour (child table)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
LabourSelectSelf / Artisan / WeaverInputOptions fetched from HR module; selecting auto-fills Unit (hrs) and Rate.Required for the row to count.
QtyNumber—InputHours of labour per batch.Numeric ≥ 0.
UnitRead-only texthrsAutofillFetched from HR.— (read-only)
Rate (₹)Read-only currency—AutofillFetched from HR (e.g. Self ₹80/hr, Artisan/Weaver ₹180/hr).— (read-only)
Amount (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedAmount = Qty × Rate.—
Subtotal — Human Resource (₹)Read-only currency—Calculated= ∑ row Amounts.—

Section III — Packaging (child table)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
PackagingSelectBottle / Label / Kraft pouch / Hang tag / Tissue paper / Seal / PouchInputOptions fetched from Packaging module; selecting auto-fills Unit (pcs) and Rate.Required for the row to count.
QtyNumber—InputPieces consumed per batch.Numeric ≥ 0.
UnitRead-only textpcsAutofillFetched from Packaging.— (read-only)
Rate (₹)Read-only currency—AutofillFetched from Packaging (e.g. Bottle ₹18.00, Label ₹5.00).— (read-only)
Amount (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedAmount = Qty × Rate.—
Subtotal — Packaging (₹)Read-only currency—Calculated= ∑ row Amounts.—

Section IV — Transportation

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Total Cost of Raw Material Transportation (₹)Number (currency)—InputPer-batch cost of moving raw materials in.Numeric ≥ 0; blank = 0.
Total Cost of Packaging Transportation (₹)Number (currency)—InputPer-batch cost of moving packaging in.Numeric ≥ 0; blank = 0.
Subtotal — Transportation (₹)Read-only currency—Calculated= RM Transport + Packaging Transport.—

Section V — Machine Depreciation (child table)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
MachineSelectGrinder / Hand carving tools / Traditional loomInputOptions fetched from Machinery module; selecting auto-fills Unit and per-unit depreciation Rate.Required for the row to count.
QtyNumberdefault 1InputNumber of machine-units allocated to the batch (defaults to 1).Numeric ≥ 0.
UnitRead-only textunitAutofillFetched from Machinery.— (read-only)
Rate (₹)Read-only currency—AutofillFetched from Machinery (per-unit depreciation, e.g. Grinder ₹0.86, Traditional loom ₹6.39).— (read-only)
Amount (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedAmount = Qty × Rate.—
Subtotal — Machine Depreciation (₹)Read-only currency—Calculated= ∑ row Amounts.—

Section VI — Gas

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ItemRead-only labelGas—Single fixed row.—
QtyNumber—InputHours of gas use per batch.Numeric ≥ 0.
UnitRead-only texthrsAutofillFixed as hrs.—
Rate (₹/hr)Number (currency)—InputCost per hour of gas (manually entered).Numeric ≥ 0.
Amount (₹) / SubtotalRead-only currency—CalculatedAmount = Qty × Rate; the single row's amount is also the section Subtotal.—

Section VII — Others (Rent + Electricity overheads)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Rent per month (₹)Read-only currency—AutofillFetched from Infrastructure / Business Establishment profile (e.g. ₹2,000.00).— (read-only)
Units produced per month (Rent)Number—InputMonthly output used to apportion rent.Numeric > 0 to compute Rent/Unit; 0 yields 0.
Rent / Unit (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedRent/Unit = Rent per month ÷ Units produced per month (0 if units = 0).—
Electricity per month (₹)Read-only currency—AutofillFetched from Infrastructure / Business Establishment profile (e.g. ₹500.00).— (read-only)
Units produced per month (Electricity)Number—InputMonthly output used to apportion electricity.Numeric > 0 to compute Electricity/Unit.
Electricity / Unit (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedElectricity/Unit = Electricity per month ÷ Units produced per month.—
Subtotal — Others / Per-Unit Overheads (₹)Read-only currency—Calculated= Rent/Unit + Electricity/Unit. Carried directly into Section VIII as Per-Unit Overheads (NOT divided again by units).—
Note the two distinct denominators: Sections I–VI build a per-batch Total Input Cost (divided by Total Units Produced in VIII), whereas Section VII is already expressed per unit and is simply added on top.

Section VIII — Unit Cost (calculated callout)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Total Input Cost (Sections I–VI) (₹)Read-only currency—Calculated= RM Subtotal + HR Subtotal + Packaging Subtotal + Transportation Subtotal + Machine Depreciation Subtotal + Gas Subtotal.—
Total Units ProducedNumber—InputBatch output used to spread the input cost.Numeric > 0 required; if 0 the Cost per Unit stays ₹0.00.
Per-Unit Overheads (Section VII) (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedEquals the Section VII Subtotal (Rent/Unit + Electricity/Unit).—
Cost per Unit (₹)Read-only currency (headline)—CalculatedCost per Unit = (Total Input Cost ÷ Total Units Produced) + Per-Unit Overheads. Feeds Section IX (Cost Plus unit cost) and the Summary.—

Section IX — Pricing Strategy (method × channel grid)

One Pricing Method selector drives two channel cards. Only the chosen method block (A/B/C) is shown in each card. Offline card yields a Wholesale + Retail summary; Online card yields Retail only. Margins/markups are entered per channel, so Offline and Online can differ.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Pricing MethodSelectA. Cost Plus / B. Competitor Based / C. Value BasedInputShows the matching method block in both channel cards; cleared = no block shown.Required to derive any price.
A. Cost Plus (per channel: Offline & Online)
Unit Cost (auto-fetched) (₹)Read-only currency—AutofillMirrors Cost per Unit from Section VIII.— (read-only)
Markup (%)Number—InputProducer margin over cost.Numeric ≥ 0.
Selling Price / Wholesale (auto-calc) (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedSelling = Unit Cost × (1 + Markup% ÷ 100).—
Retailer Margin (%)Number—InputMargin added by the retailer on top of wholesale.Numeric ≥ 0.
Retail Price (auto-calc) (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedRetail = Selling × (1 + Retailer Margin% ÷ 100). This is the value pushed back to Product Profile variants.—
B. Competitor Based (per channel)
Competitor ScopeSelectLocal Market / State Market / National MarketInputPicking a scope auto-fetches Competitor Avg Price and locks it read-only; clearing scope re-enables manual entry.—
Competitor Avg Price (₹)Number / Read-only when fetched—Input or AutofillFetched from Market Research (Competitor Pricing) for the chosen scope & channel (placeholder e.g. Offline local ₹80, Online national ₹165). Manual entry allowed when no scope is set.—
PositioningSelectBelow −20% / −10% / Match (1.0×) / +10% / Premium +20% / +30% / +40% / +50%InputPosition multiplier delta applied to competitor average.Required to compute Selling.
Selling Price / Wholesale (auto-calc) (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedSelling = Competitor Avg × (1 + Position Delta). Blank until a positioning is chosen.—
Retailer Margin (%)Number—InputRetailer's add-on margin.Numeric ≥ 0.
Retail Price (auto-calc) (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedRetail = Selling × (1 + Retailer Margin% ÷ 100).—
C. Value Based (per channel; back-calculates from target)
Target Retail Price (₹)Number (currency)—InputDesired shelf price reflecting perceived value.Numeric ≥ 0.
Retailer Margin (%)Number—InputRetailer margin embedded in the target retail.Numeric ≥ 0.
Selling Price / Wholesale (auto-calc) (₹)Read-only currency—CalculatedSelling = Target Retail ÷ (1 + Retailer Margin% ÷ 100); Retail = Target Retail (as entered).—
Value JustificationTextarea—InputFree-text rationale for why the price reflects perceived value.— (recommended)
Channel Summary strip
Wholesale Price (₹) [Offline card only]Read-only currency (headline)—CalculatedThe active method's Selling/Wholesale for that channel.—
Retail Price (₹)Read-only currency (headline)—CalculatedThe active method's Retail for that channel. Online card shows Retail only (no Wholesale).—
Retail Price → Product Profile. On save, each channel's Retail Price is written back to the matching Product Profile variant (per SKU × channel), closing the Golden Thread loop.
Tab 3: Optimized ▼

Structurally identical to the Existing tab — the same nine-section cost buildup and pricing grid — but holds the target / optimised cost structure (after intervention savings on raw material, labour, packaging etc.). Blocks are SKU-keyed so the Summary can match an Optimized block to its Existing counterpart.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
(all sections I–IX)As Existing tabAs ExistingAs ExistingSame field set, formulas and fetch sources as the Existing tab. Cost per Unit here is matched by SKU against the Existing tab to populate the Summary's Optimized Cost / Unit, Difference and Avg Saving.As Existing; SKU must match an Existing block to surface in the comparison.
Tab 4: Resources ▼

Reference view (placeholder in mockup): pricing benchmarks, market-rate references and Business Economics (BE) reference data — the lookup tables that back the auto-fetched rates and competitor averages.

Tab 5: Task Log ▼

Chronological record of pricing decisions, cost revisions and an audit trail (placeholder in mockup).

Key validations (whole form): a block must have a SKU selected before it counts in Summary roll-ups. Child-table rows need a selected item to contribute; at least one row is required per table (deleting the last row is blocked with “At least one row is required”). Qty, Rate, Markup, Margin and units fields are numeric ≥ 0; blanks are treated as 0. Total Units Produced must be > 0 for Cost per Unit to compute; Units produced per month must be > 0 for Rent/Unit and Electricity/Unit. A Pricing Method must be selected to derive any price; Competitor Based needs a Positioning chosen (and a Competitor Avg, fetched or manual) before Selling computes. Fetched fields (Unit, Rate, Rent/month, Electricity/month, and Competitor Avg when a scope is set) are read-only. All currency in Indian Rupee with Indian number formatting.
Section Review:
PR-MKT-001

Market Research

Part of the Market module, the Market Research framework lets an entrepreneur capture the market landscape (Global / India / Meghalaya), map selling channels, research competing brands, interview consumers, run a competitor price scan across Local / State / National markets, and conclude with a SWOT. The per-scope competitor average prices computed here are auto-fetched into the Unit Pricing framework when the entrepreneur chooses the Competitor-Based pricing method.
Golden thread to Unit Pricing: Each pricing table (Offline / Online, per market scope) auto-calculates a Competitor Average Price = mean of all non-zero price rows. These six averages (Local / State / National × Offline / Online) are read-only inputs into Unit Pricing → Competitor-Based method. They are not re-keyed there; they flow from this framework. Captured per product.

On-Screen UI Structure

Top bar: forest-teal header "Market Research" with a "PRIME Rural · Marketing Framework" crumb, and an entrepreneur chip below it (avatar + name auto-fetched from profile).
Tab rail (uppercase pills): Summary · Market Research · Competitive Pricing Assessment · Resources · Task Log. Active tab is teal-filled.
Body: Both the Market Research and Competitive Pricing tabs are product-centric repeaters — the entrepreneur clicks "+ Add Product", picks a product from the Product Master in a collapsible card header, and fills nested sections inside that card. There is no single flat form.
Special widgets: (1) collapsible product cards with a product dropdown in the header; (2) multi-select chip pickers for Countries / States / Meghalaya locations; (3) nested entry repeaters (Channel / Brand / Respondent) each as its own collapsible card; (4) currency inputs with a ₹ prefix; (5) competitor price tables with an auto-calculated average row in the table footer; (6) a 2×2 SWOT quadrant grid; (7) a Summary tab with an overview table, aggregate cards and intervention-flag tiles.
Calculated fields (Product Name, Product Snapshot, Competitor Average Price, all Summary aggregates) render italic on cream-deep background, read-only, with a tooltip per Rule 1.
Summary
Auto roll-up
→
Market Research
Landscape + Channels + Brands + Customers
→
Competitive Pricing
Local / State / National + SWOT
→
Resources
Reference materials
→
Task Log
Activity register

User Journey

1. Add Product
Pick from Product Master; snapshot auto-fills
→
2. Market Landscape
Global / India / Meghalaya size, CAGR, brands
→
3. Channels + Brands + Customers
Add up to 5 outlets, brands, respondents
→
4. Competitor Prices
Offline + Online rows per market scope
→
5. SWOT
Strengths / Weaknesses / Opportunities / Threats
→
6. Avg feeds Unit Pricing
Competitor averages auto-fetched downstream
Tab 1 — Market Research (per-product repeater)

Product-centric repeater. "+ Add Product" creates a collapsible card; selecting the product from the Product Master dropdown drives the auto-fetched Product Name & Snapshot. Each product card contains four numbered sections: I Market Landscape, II Channel Research, III Key Brands Research, IV Customer Research.

Product Card Header

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductSelect (Link)Product Master (e.g., Bamboo Shoot Pickle, Lemongrass Tea, Lakadong Turmeric, Eri Silk Muffler, Bamboo Earring, Local Honey, Jadoh Spice Mix)Manual—Header dropdown per card. On change, drives Product Name & Product Snapshot autofill below; key for Summary roll-up & matching to the Competitive Pricing tab card.Required before saving the card. Error: "Select a product." Avoid duplicate product cards within the same tab.

Section I — Market Landscape

I.a Product Scope (badge: Auto-fetched)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Product NameData (read-only)—CalculatedFrom Product Master via the card header selectionItalic on cream-deep, tooltip "Fetched from Product Master · sourced via the product card header" (Rule 1).Non-editable.
Product SnapshotSmall Text (read-only)—CalculatedFrom Product Master snapshot; populates on product selectItalic on cream-deep, tooltip (Rule 1).Non-editable.

I.b Global (badge: Secondary)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Market Size (USD)DataFree text, e.g., "$3.2 Billion (2024)"Manual—Secondary research value.Optional. Counts toward "Products with Global Data" in Summary.
CAGR (%)Float (step 0.01)e.g., 6.8Manual——Optional; numeric ≥ 0. Error: "Enter a valid percentage."
Key Exporting CountriesMulti-select chip pickerCountry Master (India, China, Thailand, Indonesia, Vietnam, Bangladesh, Nepal, Sri Lanka, USA, UK, Germany, France, Netherlands, UAE, Saudi Arabia, Australia, Japan, Singapore, Other)Manual—One or more chips.Optional.
Key BrandsDatae.g., "Organic India, Pukka Herbs, Numi"Manual——Optional.
Key FindingsSmall TextTrends, consumer behaviour, certifications dominant internationallyManual——Optional.
Source / ReferenceDatae.g., "FAO, Statista, IBEF"Manual——Optional.

I.c India (badge: Secondary + Primary)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Market Size (INR)Datae.g., "₹8,400 Crore (2024)"Manual—Counts toward "Products with India Market Size" and is shown in the Summary overview row.Optional.
CAGR (%)Float (step 0.01)e.g., 12Manual—Shown as "CAGR (India %)" in Summary overview.Optional; numeric ≥ 0.
Key StatesMulti-select chip pickerState Master (all Indian states + Delhi)Manual—One or more chips.Optional.
Key BrandsDatae.g., "Tata Sampann, MTR, Everest"Manual——Optional.
Key FindingsSmall TextPlatforms sold on, packaging norms, India consumer trendsManual——Optional.
Source / ReferenceDatae.g., "IBEF, e-commerce listings, brand websites"Manual——Optional.

I.d Meghalaya / North East (badge: Primary)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Market Size (INR)DataApproximate market size in district / stateManual—Counts toward "Products with Meghalaya Data" in Summary.Optional.
CAGR (%)Float (step 0.01)e.g., 8Manual——Optional; numeric ≥ 0.
Key Selling LocationsMulti-select chip pickerMeghalaya Locations Master (Iew Duh / Bara Bazaar, Police Bazaar, Laitumkhrah Market, Mawkhar Market, MeghaMart, NEHHDC Outlets, Tura Main Market, Jowai Market, Williamnagar Market, Cherrapunji Tourist Outlets, Mawsynram Outlets, Ri Bhoi Local Markets, Sohra Bazaar, Khliehriat Market, Other)Manual—One or more chips.Optional.
Key BrandsDataNames of local brands / sellersManual——Optional.
Key FindingsSmall TextDominant item sizes, packaging, customer profile, distribution patternsManual——Optional.
Source / ReferenceDataField visit dates, contact persons, photosManual——Optional.

Section II — Channel Research (child table; up to 5 outlets per product)

Repeater of collapsible "Channel" entry cards. Each entry has the fields below.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Outlet NameDatae.g., "Iew Duh Market, Stall 12"Manual——Required if entry added. Error: "Enter the outlet name."
Outlet TypeSelectWeekly bazaar; Permanent retail shop; SHG / cooperative store; Govt outlet (NEHHDC etc.); Supermarket / modern retail; Pharmacy / health store; Tourism shop / souvenir; Wholesale market; OtherManual——Optional.
DistrictSelectMeghalaya District Master (East Khasi Hills, West Khasi Hills, South West Khasi Hills, Eri Bhoi, Jaintia Hills, East Jaintia Hills, West Garo Hills, East Garo Hills, South Garo Hills, North Garo Hills, South West Garo Hills)Manual——Optional.
Products FoundDataSimilar products on shelfManual——Optional.
Price Range ObservedDatae.g., "₹80–₹150 per unit"Manual——Optional.
Shelf PlacementSelectEye-level / prominent; Bottom shelf; Top shelf; Counter display; End-cap / promotional; Not prominently placedManual——Optional.
Field NotesSmall TextPackaging, competitor branding, customer behaviour observationsManual——Optional.
A channel count > 0 sets "Channel Data Captured" for that product in the Summary; the count surfaces as "Channels Mapped" in the overview row and aggregate cards. Soft limit of 5 outlets per product.

Section III — Key Brands Research (child table)

Repeater of collapsible "Brand" entry cards. Research competing / comparable brands across India (online + offline).

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Brand NameDatae.g., "Organic India"Manual——Required if entry added. Error: "Enter the brand name."
OriginSelectState MasterManual——Optional.
PlatformsSelectAmazon India; Flipkart; Brand website (D2C); Jiomart / BigBasket; Modern retail (offline); Instagram / Facebook shop; WhatsApp / direct; Exhibition / trade show; OtherManual——Optional.
Brand's Product NameDatae.g., "Tulsi Original Green Tea, 100g"Manual——Optional.
Price (INR)Currency (₹ prefix, step 0.01)NumericManual—Reference price for benchmarking; not auto-fed to Unit Pricing (only the Competitive Pricing tab averages are).Optional; numeric ≥ 0.
Key USPDatae.g., "Farm to table", curcumin %, origin storyManual——Optional.
PackingSelectGlass jar; Tin can; Kraft paper pouch; Standard plastic pouch; PET bottle; HDPE bottle; Aluminium foil pouch; Cardboard box; Cloth pouch; Vacuum-sealed; Gift hamper; OtherManual——Optional.
Learning for our ProductSmall TextWhat to learn / differentiate from this brand's positioning, pricing, packagingManual——Optional.

Section IV — Customer Research (child table)

Repeater of collapsible "Respondent" entry cards. Interview end consumers; document basic profile and product usage.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Age GroupSelectBelow 18; 18–25; 26–35; 36–45; 46–55; 56–65; Above 65Manual——Optional.
GenderSelectFemale; Male; Non-binary; Prefer not to sayManual——Optional.
OccupationSelectSalaried; Self-employed; Farmer; Student; Homemaker; Retired; Daily Wage; Government Employee; Business OwnerManual——Optional.
Location (State / District / Block)3 cascading SelectsState Master / Meghalaya District Master / Block Master (Mawkynrew, Mawphlang, Pynursla, Mylliem, Sohra (Cherrapunji), Mawsynram, Khliehriat, Jowai, Resubelpara, Tura, Williamnagar, Baghmara, Other)Manual—Three-column grid.Optional.
Have you used the product?SelectYes; NoManual—Conditional: when "Yes", reveals the Purchase Frequency row; when "No"/blank it is hidden.Optional.
Purchase FrequencySelect (conditional)Daily; Weekly; Fortnightly; Monthly; Seasonally; Festive / Special Occasion only; Once a yearManual—Shown only if "Have you used the product? = Yes".Optional; only relevant when shown.
RemarksSmall TextVerbatim notes / key quotesManual——Optional.
Respondent count > 0 sets "Customer Data Captured"; the count appears as "Respondents" in the Summary overview and aggregates.
Tab 2 — Competitive Pricing Assessment (per-product repeater → feeds Unit Pricing)

Same product-card repeater. Each product card holds three market-scope sections (I Local Market, II State Market, III National Market) — each with an Offline Channels and an Online Channels price table — followed by Section IV SWOT Analysis. Product selection here is matched by name to the Market Research tab card for the Summary roll-up.

Competitor Price Table (repeated × 6: Local / State / National × Offline / Online)

Each scope card seeds one Offline + one Online row on add. Columns below; channel column options differ by Offline vs Online.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
#Auto number01, 02, …CalculatedRow index (zero-padded)Renumbers on add/delete.Non-editable.
Brand / CompetitorDataBrand nameManual——Required for a row to count. Error: "Enter the competitor brand."
Outlet / Channel (Offline)  /  Platform (Online)SelectOffline: Outlet Types (Weekly bazaar … Other). Online: Platforms (Amazon India, Flipkart, Brand website (D2C), Jiomart / BigBasket, Modern retail (offline), Instagram / Facebook shop, WhatsApp / direct, Exhibition / trade show, Other).Manual—Option list switches based on Offline vs Online table.Optional.
Item SizeDatae.g., "200g pouch"Manual—Normalises comparison; align unit size for like-for-like pricing.Optional.
Price (₹)Float (step 0.01)Numeric per itemManual—Feeds the Competitor Average. Only non-zero, positive prices are included in the average.Optional per row; numeric ≥ 0. Error on negative: "Price cannot be negative."
NotesDataUSPs, packaging, certsManual——Optional.
Competitor Average PriceCurrency (read-only, footer row)₹ 0.00 defaultCalculatedMean of all non-zero Price rows in this tableItalic on cream-deep, tooltip (Rule 1). Recalculates live on price input / row add / row delete. This value is auto-fetched into Unit Pricing → Competitor-Based method ("Auto-fetched into Unit Pricing · Competitor Based method" caption shown under each table).Non-editable.
Feed to Unit Pricing: Six averages per product — Local-Offline, Local-Online, State-Offline, State-Online, National-Offline, National-Online — are read-only outputs consumed by the Unit Pricing framework's Competitor-Based method. The Summary surfaces Local Offline Avg, Local Online Avg and National Offline Avg per product, and flags Local / State / National pricing presence.

Section IV — SWOT Analysis (this product vs competitors)

2×2 quadrant grid; four free-text cells.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Strengths (vs Competitor)Small TextWhat this product does better — origin story, GI tag, hand-craft, ingredient qualityManual—Any non-empty SWOT cell sets "SWOT Completed" for the product in Summary.Optional.
Weaknesses (vs Competitor)Small TextWhere competitors out-perform — distribution, packaging, brand recall, certifications, scaleManual——Optional.
OpportunitiesSmall TextMarket openings — un-met segments, channel gaps, festival demand, export potentialManual——Optional.
ThreatsSmall TextExternal risks — new entrants, regulation, raw material price volatility, substitutesManual——Optional.
Tab 3 — Summary (auto roll-up, read-only)

Entirely calculated — no manual entry. Refreshes on open. Comprises (1) a Product-wise Research Overview table, (2) four Aggregate cards, (3) four Intervention-flag tiles.

Product-wise Research Overview (one row per researched product)

ColumnField TypeSourceInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductRead-onlyCard headerCalculatedProduct nameRow keyed to selected product.—
India Market SizeRead-onlyI.c IndiaCalculatedMarket Size (INR)"—" if blank.—
CAGR (India %)Read-onlyI.c IndiaCalculatedCAGR with "%" suffix——
Channels MappedRead-onlySection IICalculatedCount of channel entriesTeal bold.—
Brands ResearchedRead-onlySection IIICalculatedCount of brand entriesTeal bold.—
RespondentsRead-onlySection IV (Customer)CalculatedCount of respondent entries——
Local Offline Avg (₹)Read-onlyCompetitive Pricing · Local OfflineCalculatedCompetitor averageGreen tint. "Add in Competitive Pricing tab" if no matching card.—
Local Online Avg (₹)Read-onlyCompetitive Pricing · Local OnlineCalculatedCompetitor averageGreen tint.—
National Offline Avg (₹)Read-onlyCompetitive Pricing · National OfflineCalculatedCompetitor averageGreen tint.—

Aggregate Cards & Intervention Flags (all read-only counters)

MetricField TypeGroupInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Products ResearchedRead-only countExisting / FlagCalculatedCount of products with a selected nameAlso shown as a flag tile.—
Channels Mapped (Total)Read-only countExisting / FlagCalculatedSum of channel entries across productsAlso a flag tile.—
Brands Researched (Total)Read-only countExisting / FlagCalculatedSum of brand entriesAlso a flag tile.—
Respondents InterviewedRead-only countExisting / FlagCalculatedSum of respondent entriesAlso a flag tile.—
Products with India Market SizeRead-only countMarketCalculatedProducts with non-blank India sizeGreen.—
Products with Meghalaya DataRead-only countMarketCalculatedProducts with non-blank Meghalaya sizeGreen.—
Products with CAGR (India)Read-only countMarketCalculatedProducts with India CAGR filled——
Products with Global DataRead-only countMarketCalculatedProducts with non-blank Global size——
Products with Local PricingRead-only countInterventionsCalculatedProducts with a Local Offline or Online avg > 0——
Products with State PricingRead-only countInterventionsCalculatedProducts with a State Offline avg > 0——
Products with National PricingRead-only countInterventionsCalculatedProducts with a National Offline avg > 0——
Competitor Rows Logged (Total)Read-only countInterventionsCalculatedSum of all competitor price rows across scopes & offline/online——
SWOT CompletedRead-only countStatusCalculatedProducts with any non-empty SWOT cellGreen.—
Customer Data CapturedRead-only countStatusCalculatedProducts with respondents > 0Green.—
Channel Data CapturedRead-only countStatusCalculatedProducts with channels > 0Green.—
Brand Data CapturedRead-only countStatusCalculatedProducts with brands > 0Green.—
Tab 4 — Resources (Reference Materials child table)

Single child table; at least one row required.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Resource TitleDataFree textManual——Required if row kept. Error: "Enter a resource title."
CategorySelectIndustry Report; Market Survey Data; Competitor Product Sample; Channel / Buyer Contact; Platform Listing Reference; Consumer Interview Transcript; Field Visit Photo; Pricing Benchmark; GI / Certification Reference; OtherManual——Optional.
TypeSelectReport / Study; Contact; Reference; Photo / Evidence; Transcript; Quotation; SpecificationManual——Optional.
Description / NotesDataFree text notesManual——Optional.
FileAttach (Upload / View)File uploadManual—Upload and View actions.Optional.
Delete is blocked when only one row remains. Error: "At least one row is required."
Tab 5 — Task Log (Market Research Activities register)

Activity register with a live entry counter. Each entry below.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Auto number01, 02, …CalculatedSequential, zero-paddedRenumbers on delete; drives the entry counter.Non-editable.
DateDateDate pickerManual——Optional.
Task NameSelectMR task list: 01 Define Research Scope & Objectives; 02 Secondary Research — Global; 03 Secondary Research — India; 04 Secondary Research — Meghalaya / NE; 05 Identify Selling Channels (Offline); 06 Identify Selling Channels (Online / E-commerce); 07 Field Visit — Channel Mapping; 08 Document Competitor Brands (Online); 09 Document Competitor Brands (Offline); 10 Record Competitor Prices (Local); 11 Record Competitor Prices (State); 12 Record Competitor Prices (National); 13 Conduct Customer / Consumer Interviews; 14 Compile Customer Research Findings; 15 SWOT Analysis — vs Competitor; 16 Identify Key Differentiators; 17 Share Research Summary with Core Team; 18 Update Market Research in CRM; "If other, specify in comments"Manual——Required per entry. Error: "Select a task."
Notes / OutcomeDataFree textManual——Optional.
AttachmentAttach (Upload)File uploadManual——Optional.
Key validations (whole form): (1) Each product card must have a product selected from the Product Master before save — "Select a product"; avoid duplicate product cards in the same tab. (2) Channel and Brand entries require Outlet Name / Brand Name respectively. (3) All numeric fields (CAGR %, all Prices) must be ≥ 0 — negative prices rejected ("Price cannot be negative"). (4) Competitor Average Price is system-calculated from non-zero positive prices only and is read-only; it is the value auto-fetched into Unit Pricing → Competitor-Based method — it must never be hand-edited. (5) Purchase Frequency is only valid / shown when "Have you used the product? = Yes". (6) Resources table must retain at least one row. (7) Task Log entries require a Task Name. (8) All Indian currency uses ₹ with Indian number formatting (e.g., ₹8,400 Crore, ₹ 1,00,000).
Section Review:
PR-MKT-002

Customer Analysis

Market module framework where the field facilitator maps each product's current customer base across demographic, geographic, psychographic and behavioural segmentation, defines the existing target market, flags products needing an upgrade, and captures an aspirational (Required) target market with an implementation pipeline.

On-Screen UI Structure

Top bar: teal header reading “Customer Analysis” with breadcrumb “PRIME Rural · Marketing Framework”. Below it an entrepreneur chip showing initials avatar and “Auto-fetched from profile” (the entrepreneur is auto-linked, not chosen on this screen).
Tab rail (5 tabs): Summary · Existing · Required · Resources · Task Log. The active tab is solid teal; Existing is the default working tab; Summary opens on load.
Body — Existing & Required tabs: a stacked list of collapsible product blocks. Each product block (sea-green header with product dropdown + status chip + Remove) expands into six numbered sub-sections (I–VI). Sub-sections I–V use a multi-select chip picker (pick one or more values from a master, each shown as a removable chip). Sub-section VI uses single-select dropdowns plus a free-text persona. The Existing block ends with an Intervention Need toggle; the Required block ends with an Implementation Status block.
Special widgets: chip multi-picker (demographic/geographic/psychographic/behavioural/segmentation fields); toggle switch (intervention flag) that reveals a justification box and lights an “Intervention Flagged” chip in the product header; status chip (Pending / In Progress / Completed / Not Feasible) on Required products.
Summary tab is fully calculated — product-wise Existing-vs-Required comparison table, four aggregate cards, and four intervention-flag tiles, all auto-populated from the Existing and Required tabs. Calculated fields are shown in italic on cream-deep background with a tooltip explaining the source (Rule 1). Resources holds a reference-material table; Task Log holds a dated activity log against a fixed task master.
Summary
→
Existing
→
Required
→
Resources
→
Task Log

User Journey

1
Open Customer Analysis; entrepreneur auto-fetched from profile
→
2
On Existing tab, Add Product and pick it from the product master
→
3
Fill segmentation I–V (demographic, geographic, psychographic, behavioural, customer segments)
→
4
Define Target Market (VI) — segment, readiness, persona — then flag Intervention Need if upgrade required
→
5
On Required tab, add the same/new product, define aspirational target & set Implementation Status
→
6
Attach references (Resources), log activity (Task Log), review the auto Summary comparison
Tab 1 — Existing (current customer base, per product)
Caption on screen: “Map the current customer base for each product the entrepreneur is selling. Each product holds its own customer segmentation and target market definition.” The tab renders a list of collapsible product blocks plus an + Add Product bar. Each block carries the six sub-sections below plus an Intervention Need toggle.

Product Block Header

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductSelect (Link to Product Master)Bamboo Shoot Pickle, Lemongrass Tea, Lakadong Turmeric, Eri Silk Muffler, Bamboo Earring, Local Honey, Jadoh Spice Mix (placeholder master — final list from Product master)YesNoHeader dropdown that names the block; selecting blank leaves an unnamed block. One product block per product.Product required before the block counts toward Summary. Error: “Select a product.”
Intervention Flagged (chip)Status chip (read-only)“↻ Intervention Flagged” (hidden until flagged)NoYesAuto-shown when the Intervention Need toggle is ON for this product.—
RemoveButton—YesNoRemoves the product block after confirmation “Remove this product's customer analysis?”; clears its picker state.—

I. Demographic Segmentation

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Age GroupMulti-select chip pickerBelow 18; 18–25; 26–35; 36–45; 46–55; 56–65; Above 65YesNoPick one or more; each value shown as a removable chip.No duplicate values; at least one demographic field recommended.
GenderMulti-select chip pickerFemale; Male; Non-binary; Prefer not to sayYesNoPick one or more.No duplicate values.
Family SizeMulti-select chip picker1 (Single); 2 (Couple); 3–4 (Small Family); 5–6 (Mid Family); 7+ (Joint Family)YesNoPick one or more.No duplicate values.
OccupationMulti-select chip pickerSalaried; Self-employed; Farmer; Student; Homemaker; Retired; Daily Wage; Government EmployeeYesNoPick one or more.No duplicate values.
Income BracketMulti-select chip pickerBelow ₹15,000; ₹15,000–₹30,000; ₹30,000–₹60,000; ₹60,000–₹1,00,000; Above ₹1,00,000YesNoPick one or more; Indian number formatting on bands.No duplicate values.
Education LevelMulti-select chip pickerNo formal education; Primary; Secondary; Higher Secondary; Diploma; Graduate; PostgraduateYesNoPick one or more.No duplicate values.
NotesText area (multi-line)Free text — “Additional observations on demographic segmentation”YesNoOptional qualitative notes.—

II. Geographic

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Primary GeographyMulti-select chip pickerLocal (Block); District; State; Inter-state; National; InternationalYesNoFeeds the Existing “Geography” column in the Summary table (first two picks shown).No duplicate values; at least one value recommended.
Key CitiesMulti-select chip pickerShillong; Tura; Jowai; Nongpoh; Williamnagar; Baghmara; Guwahati; Kolkata; Delhi; MumbaiYesNoPick one or more cities.No duplicate values.
Location TypeMulti-select chip pickerRural; Semi-urban; Urban; Tier-1 City; Tier-2 City; Tier-3 CityYesNoPick one or more.No duplicate values.
NotesText areaFree text — “Additional observations on geography”YesNoOptional.—

III. Psychographic

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Values & LifestyleMulti-select chip pickerHealth-conscious; Eco-conscious; Traditional; Modern; Premium / Luxury; Budget-conscious; Convenience-driven; AdventurousYesNoPick one or more.No duplicate values.
Buying MotivationMulti-select chip pickerQuality; Price; Local Origin; Brand; Health Benefits; Convenience; Social Status; RecommendationYesNoPick one or more.No duplicate values.
NotesText areaFree text — “Additional observations on psychographics”YesNoOptional.—

IV. Behavioural

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Purchase BehaviourMulti-select chip pickerFirst-time Buyer; Repeat Purchase; Bulk Buyer; Occasional; Seasonal; ImpulseYesNoPick one or more.No duplicate values.
Usage RateMulti-select chip pickerHeavy User; Moderate User; Light User; Non-userYesNoPick one or more.No duplicate values.
Loyalty StatusMulti-select chip pickerHard-core Loyal; Soft-core Loyal; Shifting Loyal; SwitcherYesNoPick one or more.No duplicate values.
OccasionMulti-select chip pickerDaily Use; Festive; Gifting; Special Occasion; Travel; Health NeedYesNoPick one or more.No duplicate values.
NotesText areaFree text — “Additional observations on customer behaviour”YesNoOptional.—

V. Customer Segmentation

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Primary CustomersMulti-select chip pickerHouseholds; Restaurants & Cafes; Hotels; Retail Stores; Wholesalers; Tourists; Online Buyers; Institutional Buyers; Health-conscious ConsumersYesNoFeeds the Existing “Customers” column in the Summary table (first two picks shown).At least one primary customer recommended; no duplicates.
Secondary CustomersMulti-select chip pickerSame master as Primary Customers (Households … Health-conscious Consumers)YesNoOptional broader/secondary base.No duplicate values.

VI. Target Market Definition

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Primary Target SegmentSelect (single)Not applicable; Urban health-conscious women, 26–45; Tourists & diaspora; Institutional / HoReCa buyers; Export / B2B buyers; Gift buyers (festive); Local daily consumers; Pan-India e-commerce buyersYesNoDerived from the segmentation above; counts toward “Target Segment Set” aggregate when not “Not applicable”.Recommended once segmentation done.
Secondary Target SegmentSelect (single)Same master as Primary Target SegmentYesNoOptional second segment.—
Market ReadinessSelect (single)Not applicable; High — immediate market exists; Medium — needs awareness building; Low — market development neededYesNoDrives the Existing Market Readiness badge and the High/Medium/Low/Not Set aggregates.—
Target Customer PersonaText areaFree text — “Describe the typical customer in 3–4 sentences…”YesNoQualitative persona narrative.—

Intervention Need (Existing only)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Upgrade Target Customers?Toggle (on/off)Off (default) / OnYesNoWhen ON: flag note changes to “↻ Flagged → Aspirational profile in Required tab”, the “Intervention Flagged” header chip appears, the Justification box is revealed, and the product is counted as “Upgrade Needed” in Summary.—
JustificationText area (conditional)Free text — “Reason for upgrading the target customer profile for this product…”YesNoShown only when the toggle is ON.Recommended when flagged: explain why an upgrade is needed.
A flagged Existing product signals that a matching aspirational profile should be created on the Required tab. The flag lights the “↻ Upgrade” badge and the → arrow in the Summary comparison row.
Tab 2 — Required (aspirational target market + implementation)
Caption on screen: “Define the aspirational target market for each product. Track Implementation Status as interventions move through the pipeline.” A Required product block uses the same product header and the same six sub-sections (I–VI) as Existing, but the closing block is Implementation Status instead of Intervention Need. Required rows are matched to Existing rows by product name in the Summary comparison; unmatched Required products appear as “+ New Target Only”.

Segmentation I–VI (aspirational)

Sub-sections I–VI are identical in structure to the Existing tab (Demographic, Geographic, Psychographic, Behavioural, Customer Segmentation, Target Market Definition), but here they describe the aspirational future customer rather than the current base. The same masters and validations apply. See the Existing tab tables above for field-level detail.

Product Block Header (Required)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductSelect (Link to Product Master)Same product master as ExistingYesNoIf the product name matches an Existing block, the Summary pairs them in one comparison row; otherwise the row is tagged “+ New Target Only”.Product required. Error: “Select a product.”
Status (chip)Status chip (read-only)Pending (default) / In Progress / Completed / Not FeasibleNoYesMirrors the Implementation Status select below; colour-coded chip in the product header.—

Implementation Status (Required only)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
StatusSelect (single)Pending; In Progress; Completed; Not FeasibleYesNoOn change, updates the header status chip colour and the Implementation Status aggregates (Completed/In Progress/Pending/Not Feasible) and the Summary “Impl. Status” badge.—
NotesText areaFree text — “Implementation notes, blockers, next steps…”YesNoOptional progress log.—
Tab 3 — Summary (auto-calculated)
Entirely read-only and auto-populated from the Existing and Required tabs. Opens by default on load. Three blocks: a product-wise comparison table, four aggregate cards, and four intervention-flag tiles. All values are calculated; none are entered here.

① Product-wise Customer Overview (calculated table)

ColumnField TypeSource / LogicNotes
ProductText + flag badgeProduct name; badge shows “↻ Upgrade” if Existing flagged, or “+ New Target Only” if Required has no Existing match.One row per product (Existing-led; unmatched Required appended).
Existing — GeographyCalculatedFirst two picks of Existing Primary Geography.Peach (Existing) background.
Existing — CustomersCalculatedFirst two picks of Existing Primary Customers.Peach background.
Existing — Market ReadinessCalculated badgeExisting Market Readiness (High/Medium/Low label).Green/amber/red badge.
Required — GeographyCalculatedFirst two picks of Required Primary Geography.Mint (Required) background; “Not set” if blank.
Required — Target SegmentCalculatedRequired Primary Target Segment (excludes “Not applicable”).Mint background.
Required — Market ReadinessCalculated badgeRequired Market Readiness label.Mint background.
Impl. StatusCalculated badgeRequired Implementation Status (Pending/In Progress/Completed/Not Feasible).Colour-coded.

② Aggregate Cards (calculated)

CardMetricSource / Logic
ExistingProducts Analysed; Intervention Flagged; Not FlaggedCounts of Existing product blocks; flagged = toggle ON.
MarketHigh / Medium / Low Market Readiness; Not SetCounts of Existing products by their Market Readiness band.
InterventionsRequired Profiles Added; Target Segment Set; Persona WrittenCount of Required blocks; with a target segment set; with a persona text filled.
Implementation StatusCompleted; In Progress; Pending; Not FeasibleCounts of Required products by Implementation Status.

③ Intervention Flag Tiles (calculated)

TileSource / Logic
Products AnalysedTotal Existing product blocks.
Upgrade NeededExisting products with the Intervention toggle ON.
High Market ReadinessExisting products with High readiness.
New Target ProfilesTotal Required product blocks.
Tab 4 — Resources (reference materials)
An add-row table of supporting evidence used to build the customer analysis. Each row has Resource Title, Category, Type, Notes, a File upload, and a delete control.
Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Resource TitleTextFree textYesNoName of the reference.Recommended.
CategorySelectConsumer Survey Data; Market Research Report; Customer Interview Transcript; Competitor Customer Profile; Census / Demographics Data; Platform Analytics; OtherYesNoClassifies the source.—
TypeSelectReport / Study; Transcript; Reference; Template; DataYesNoFormat of the file.—
NotesTextFree textYesNoOptional context.—
FileAttach (Upload button)Uploaded documentYesNoAttach the source file.—
(Delete)Button—YesNoRemoves the row; “+ Add Resource” appends a new blank row.—
Tab 5 — Task Log (activity log)
A numbered, dated log of customer-analysis activities. Header shows a live entry count. Each “+ Add Entry” appends an auto-numbered row; deleting a row re-numbers the rest.
Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Auto number01, 02, … (zero-padded)NoYesAuto-sequenced; re-numbers on delete.—
DateDateCalendar pickerYesNoDate the activity was carried out.—
Task NameSelect (task master)01 Awareness on Importance of Customer Analysis; 02 Identify Products for Customer Analysis; 03–06 Map Existing Customer Base (Demographic / Geographic / Psychographic / Behavioural); 07 Define Primary Customer Segmentation; 08 Define Secondary Customer Segmentation; 09 Define Primary Target Segment (Existing); 10 Assess Market Readiness (Existing); 11 Write Customer Persona (Existing); 12 Identify Gaps — Flag for Intervention; 13 Define Aspirational Target Segment (Required); 14 Set Market Readiness (Required); 15 Write Aspirational Customer Persona; 16 Link to Market Research Module; 17 Link to Market Linkage Module; 18 Review and Update Status in Module; If other, specify in commentsYesNoFixed task master driving the log.Select a task; use “If other” for ad-hoc activities.
Notes / OutcomeTextFree textYesNoOutcome of the activity.—
(Delete)Button—YesNoRemoves the row and re-sequences numbering.—
Cross-module links: tasks 16 and 17 connect this analysis to the Market Research and Market Linkage modules respectively, keeping the customer definition consistent across the Market framework.
Key validations (whole form): (1) Each product block requires a Product selection before it counts toward the Summary — “Select a product.” (2) Multi-select chip pickers reject duplicate values within the same field. (3) When the Existing Intervention toggle is ON, a Justification is expected and a matching aspirational profile should exist on the Required tab. (4) Income brackets follow Indian number formatting (₹1,00,000 not ₹100,000). (5) The Summary tab and all header status/flag chips are calculated — never edited directly; they recompute whenever Existing/Required data changes. (6) Required products are matched to Existing by exact product name; an unmatched Required product is surfaced as “+ New Target Only” rather than silently dropped.
Section Review:
PR-MKT-003

Market Linkage
