Intervention Flag Tiles (four read-only tiles)

TileField TypeSourceInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Existing PromotionsRead-only countExistingCalculatedTotal named Existing rowsGreen tile.—
New Methods NeededRead-only (Yes / No)Intervention toggleCalculatedYes when flagged, else NoTerracotta tile.—
Online (Total)Read-only countExisting + RequiredCalculatedOnline rows across both phasesBlue tile.—
Offline (Total)Read-only countExisting + RequiredCalculatedOffline rows across both phasesCream tile.—
Tab 4 — Resources (Reference Materials child table)

Single child table of reference materials; at least one row required. Also surfaced as a Drive Resources / link in the underlying doctype.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Resource TitleDataFree textManual——Required if row kept. Error: "Enter a resource title."
CategorySelectSocial Media Guide; Platform Reference; Content Template; Budget Estimate; Campaign Tracker; Competitor Promotion; Govt. Scheme for Promotion; OtherManual——Optional.
TypeSelectGuide / Manual; Template; Reference; Contact; Photo / EvidenceManual——Optional.
Description / NotesDataFree text notesManual——Optional.
FileAttach (Upload / View)File uploadManual—Upload and View actions.Optional.
DeleteAction (×)—Manual—Removes the row.Blocked when only one row remains. Error: "At least one row is required."
Delete is blocked when only one row remains.
Tab 5 — Task Log (Promotions Activities register)

Activity register ("Task Log — Promotions Activities") with a live entry counter. Each entry below.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Auto number01, 02, …CalculatedSequential, zero-paddedRenumbers on delete; drives the "<n> entries" counter.Non-editable.
DateDateDate pickerManual——Optional.
Task NameSelectPromotions task list: 01 Awareness on Importance of Promotions; 02 Document Existing Promotion Activities; 03 Assess Reach and Effectiveness of Existing Promotions; 04 Identify Gaps in Promotion Coverage; 05 Research Platform Options (Online); 06 Recommend New Promotion Methods; 07 Set Up Social Media Profile / Page; 08 Create First Content Piece (Photo / Video); 09 Publish First Post / Campaign; 10 Train Entrepreneur on Content Creation; 11 Review First Month Promotion Performance; 12 Plan Next Promotion Cycle; 13 Coordinate with Marketing / Design Partners; 14 Track Leads / Inquiries from Promotions; 15 Update Promotions Module in CRM; "If other, specify in comments"Manual——Required per entry. Error: "Select a task."
Notes / OutcomeDataFree textManual——Optional.
AttachmentAttach (Upload)File uploadManual——Optional.
DeleteAction (×)—Manual—Removes the row, renumbers, updates the counter.—
Key validations (whole form): (1) Entrepreneur is required and unique — one DF Promotional Marketing record per entrepreneur; duplicate rejected. (2) Module ("Marketing") and Applicable To ("Product") are read-only system defaults; District & Block are read-only fetches from the Entrepreneur Profile and must never be hand-edited. (3) Every promotion row needs a Promotion Name to count in the Summary — "Enter the promotion name." (4) Phase is system-set (Existing on the Existing tab, Required on the Required tab). (5) Implementation Status is mandatory on Required rows ("Set the implementation status"); blank is treated as Pending. (6) Digital Platform Category is conditional — relevant when Channel Type = Online, NA when Offline. (7) When the Intervention toggle is On, a Justification is expected and the record is flagged "New Methods Needed = Yes". (8) Resources table must retain at least one row ("At least one row is required."). (9) Task Log entries require a Task Name ("Select a task."). (10) All Summary figures are system-calculated, read-only, and refresh on open; counts use Indian number formatting.
Section Review:
PR-LC-002

Business Registration (Legal Compliance module)

Entrepreneur-level framework, now under the Legal Compliance module (previously named "Registration & Licensing" under Product Development). Tracks all business registrations and licences held and required. Features intervention bridging — flagging in Existing auto-creates linked entry in Required. The companion Product Compliance framework (same module) handles product-level certifications — spec to be drafted in a follow-up pass.

Tab Structure: Existing | Required

Registration Master (20 types): FSSAI, Udyam, GST, Trade License, Shop & Establishment, Trademark, GI, Organic Certification, India Organic, Khadi Mark, Hallmark, BIS, PAN, Aadhaar, Bank Account, Pollution NOC, Fire Safety NOC, Health & Trade License, IEC, Other.
Existing Tab — Registration Cards ▼
FieldTypeValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation
Registration TypeSelect20 types from masterInputCollapsible card headerRequired
Registration LevelSelectProduct, EntrepreneurInput--Required
ID No.DataFree textInputRegistration/license number--
Issuing AuthorityDataFree textInput----
Issued DateDate--Input--Must be ≤ today
Lifetime ValidityCheckboxYes/NoInputIf checked: hides "Valid Till" field--
Valid TillDate--InputHidden when Lifetime Validity checkedMust be > Issued Date
Govt Certified?SelectYes, NoInput----
StatusSelectReceived, Not yet Received, RejectedInputColor chip: Received=green, Not yet=amber, Rejected=redRequired
Scanned CopyAttach.pdf, .jpg, .pngInputUpload + View + file pill display--
Intervention NeedToggleOn/OffInputIf ON: show Type (Renewal/Reapply) + Justification. Auto-creates bridged entry in Required tab.Justification required if ON
Bridging logic: Flagged entries auto-create linked Required entries. Bridged entries in Required are locked (disabled selects) with a "Bridged from Existing" marker. Removing either side un-links the other.
Required Tab ▼

Same registration fields plus:

Additional FieldTypeValuesLogic
PhaseSelectNew Registration, Renewal--
StatusSelectApplied, Not Yet Applied, Received, Not yet Received, RejectedChip in header. Bridged entries show source marker.
NotesTextareaFree text--
Section Review:
PR-PD-002

Product Prototyping

Part of the Product Development module, this framework lets the Fessociate track every prototyping iteration for each of an entrepreneur's products — covering both packaging prototypes and product prototypes — from first concept through to scale implementation. One product card is added per product, and each prototype version (v1, v2, …) is logged inside that product's child table with a design upload and key learnings.

On-Screen UI Structure

The screen header reads Product Prototyping with breadcrumb PRIME Rural · Product Development. A horizontal tab rail sits directly below the header with four tabs: Summary, Prototypes, Resources and Task Log.

The Prototypes tab is the data-entry body. It opens with an auto-fetched entrepreneur chip (read-only, pulled from the profile) and a guidance caption. Below sits a repeatable list of collapsible product blocks, each added via a + Add Product button. Every product block has a coloured header carrying three controls: a Product dropdown, an Implementation Status dropdown, and a status chip that mirrors the chosen status. The block body holds two subsections: Prototype Configuration and a Prototype Iterations child table.

A conditional dropdown drives the configuration: Type of Prototyping (Packaging / Product) changes the prompt and expected values of the Prototyping Type Name field below it — Packaging guides the user toward the Packing Master list, Product toward the Business Basket list.

The Summary tab is fully read-only and auto-calculated from the Prototypes tab: a product-wise overview table, four aggregate cards (Products, Iterations, Progress, Intervention Status) and four intervention-flag tiles. The Resources and Task Log tabs each hold a single flat child table for reference materials and dated activity entries respectively.
Summary
→
Prototypes
→
Resources
→
Task Log

User Journey

Open Prototypes tab
Entrepreneur auto-fetched
→
+ Add Product
Pick product, set implementation status
→
Configure prototype
Type (Packaging/Product) → Type Name
→
+ Add Iteration
Version, design upload, key learnings
→
Repeat per version
v1 → v2 → Final/Pilot
→
Save
Summary tab auto-aggregates all products
Tab 1 — Prototypes (Data Entry) ▼

Product Block Header

Each product is a collapsible block. The header carries the product, implementation status and a mirroring status chip. Blocks are repeatable via + Add Product and removable via Remove (with confirm prompt).

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Entrepreneur Link (read-only chip) From Entrepreneur master No Yes — auto-fetched from profile context Displayed as a chip at top of tab; identifies whose products are shown. Mandatory; if context missing, block entry: “Entrepreneur not resolved — open from a profile.”
Product Select Product Master (e.g. Bamboo Shoot Pickle, Lemongrass Tea, Lakadong Turmeric, Eri Silk Muffler, Bamboo Earring, Local Honey, Jadoh Spice Mix) Yes No Identifies the product being prototyped; feeds the Summary product-wise overview (one row per product). Mandatory before save: “Select a product.” A product not yet selected is excluded from Summary aggregates.
Implementation Status Select Ideating · Iteration · Pilot Implemented · Scale Implementation Yes No On change, renders the status chip beside it (Ideating = neutral, Iteration = amber, Pilot = blue, Scale = green). Drives the Progress / Intervention Flags counters and “Furthest Stage Reached” on Summary (stage order: Ideating < Iteration < Pilot Implemented < Scale Implementation). Optional; blank counts toward “No Status Set” on Summary.
Status Chip Display (calculated) Mirrors Implementation Status No Yes Hidden when status is blank; otherwise shows the status text colour-coded. —

Subsection A — Prototype Configuration

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Type of Prototyping Select (conditional driver) Packaging · Product Yes No Conditional dropdown — the selected value re-points the Prototyping Type Name field below to the relevant master list and updates its prompt. Also classified on Summary as a Packaging or Product badge and counted in “Packaging Prototypes” vs “Product Prototypes”. Mandatory before Type Name is meaningful: “Select prototyping type.”
Prototyping Type Name Text / Select (conditional target) If Packaging → Packing Master (Glass jar, Tin can, Kraft paper pouch, Standard plastic pouch, PET bottle, HDPE bottle, Aluminium foil pouch, Cardboard box, Cloth pouch, Vacuum-sealed, Gift hamper, Other). If Product → Business Basket (Food & Beverages, Spices & Herbs, Handloom & Textile, Handicraft & Bamboo, Personal Care & Wellness, Honey & Apiary, Dairy & Allied, Agro-processed Foods, Snacks & Ready-to-eat, Health Foods, Other) Yes No Value set / prompt switches based on Type of Prototyping: Packaging prompts “e.g., Kraft paper pouch, Glass jar, Tin can…”; Product prompts “e.g., Food & Beverages, Spices & Herbs…”. Shown in the Summary “Type Name” column. Mandatory once Type is chosen: “Enter / select the prototyping type name.”
Conditional dropdown logic: Type of Prototyping is the driver field. Selecting Packaging binds Prototyping Type Name to the Packing Master; selecting Product binds it to the Business Basket. Changing the driver after a name is entered must re-validate the name against the newly applicable list and clear it if it no longer matches.

Subsection B — Prototype Iterations (Child Table)

Repeatable rows logging each prototype version. Seeded with one row; rows added via + Add Iteration, deleted via the row × control, and auto-renumbered (01, 02, …).

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
# Auto-number (calculated) 01, 02, 03… No Yes Sequential serial, zero-padded; re-numbers on add/delete. —
Prototype Version Text Free text (e.g., v1, v2, Final, v3 (Pilot)) Yes No Label for this iteration; counted as one iteration per row in Summary (“Total Iterations Logged”, “Avg Iterations / Product”). Recommended; an empty version row still counts as an iteration but should warn: “Name this prototype version.”
Design Upload File attachment .pdf, .jpg, .jpeg, .png, .svg, .ai, .psd Yes No One file per row; on upload a file pill appears and the View button enables. Rows with an attachment are counted in Summary “Uploads Attached” and shown with a clip indicator in the overview. Optional; restrict to the accepted file types — “Unsupported file type.”
Key Learnings Text (long) Free text (what worked / what didn't / what changed for next version) Yes No Captures iteration insight; rows with non-empty learnings are tallied as “Learnings Captured” on Summary. Optional; encouraged for every iteration.
Tab 2 — Summary (Read-Only / Calculated) ▼

Entirely auto-populated from the Prototypes tab; no inputs. Refreshes whenever the tab is opened. Comprises a product-wise overview table, four aggregate cards and four intervention-flag tiles.

Product-wise Overview (one row per selected product)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductDisplayFrom product blockNoYesOne row per product where a Product is selected.—
Prototype TypeDisplay (badge)Packaging / ProductNoYesColour-coded badge from Type of Prototyping.—
Type NameDisplayPacking Master / Business Basket valueNoYesMirrors Prototyping Type Name; shows “—” if blank.—
IterationsCount (calculated)IntegerNoYesNumber of iteration rows logged for the product.—
UploadsCount (calculated)Integer + clip indicatorNoYesCount of iteration rows that have a design file attached.—
Implementation StatusDisplay (chip)Ideating / Iteration / Pilot Implemented / Scale ImplementationNoYesColour-coded chip mirroring the block status.—

Aggregate Cards & Intervention Flags (all calculated)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Total ProductsCountIntegerNoYesCount of product blocks with a Product selected.—
Packaging Prototypes / Product PrototypesCountIntegerNoYesSplit of products by Type of Prototyping.—
Total Iterations LoggedCountIntegerNoYesSum of all iteration rows across products.—
Uploads AttachedCountIntegerNoYesSum of iteration rows with a file attached.—
Avg Iterations / ProductNumber (calculated)1 decimal, or “—”NoYesTotal Iterations ÷ Total Products; “—” when no products.—
Ideating / Iteration / Pilot Implemented / Scale ImplementationCount + flag tileIntegerNoYesPer-status product counts; mirrored in the four Intervention Flag tiles.—
Furthest Stage ReachedDisplay (calculated)Highest status by stage orderNoYesMax of Ideating < Iteration < Pilot Implemented < Scale Implementation across all products.—
Learnings CapturedCountIntegerNoYesIteration rows with non-empty Key Learnings.—
No Status SetCountIntegerNoYesProducts with a blank Implementation Status.—
Tab 3 — Resources (Reference Materials) ▼

A single flat child table of reference materials supporting prototyping. At least one row is always retained. Rows added via + Add Resource, deleted via the row × (blocked at the last remaining row).

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Resource TitleTextFree textYesNoName of the reference material.Recommended; warn on empty row before save.
CategorySelectPackaging Supplier · Material Sample · Design Reference · Testing / Lab Report · Costing Sheet · Competitor Product · Govt. Scheme / Support · OtherYesNoClassifies the resource.Optional; default “— Select —”.
TypeSelectContact · Reference · Quotation · Specification · Photo / Evidence · ReportYesNoNature of the material.Optional; default “— Select —”.
Description / NotesTextFree textYesNoNotes about the resource.Optional.
FileFile attachmentUpload + ViewYesNoAttach the supporting file; View opens it.Optional.
Tab 4 — Task Log (Prototyping Activities) ▼

A dated log of prototyping activities, with an entry count shown in the header. Rows added via + Add Entry, auto-numbered (01, 02, …) and re-numbered on delete.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInputAutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Auto-number (calculated)01, 02, 03…NoYesSequential serial; re-numbers on delete.—
DateDateDate pickerYesNoWhen the activity took place.Recommended.
Task NameSelect01 Awareness on Importance of Product Prototyping · 02 Identify Products Requiring Prototyping · 03 Define Prototyping Type (Packaging / Product) · 04 Research Packaging Options / Business Basket · 05 Procure Prototype Sample / Material · 06 Create First Prototype (v1) · 07 Review Prototype with Entrepreneur · 08 Document Key Learnings from v1 · 09 Create Revised Prototype (v2) · 10 Review Revised Prototype · 11 Document Key Learnings from v2 · 12 Finalise Prototype Design · 13 Pilot Production Run · 14 Evaluate Pilot Outcome · 15 Approve for Scale Implementation · 16 Coordinate with Supplier / Vendor · 17 Update Prototype Records in CRM · If other, specify in commentsYesNoPredefined activity selected from the task master.Mandatory per entry: “Select a task.”
Notes / OutcomeTextFree textYesNoOutcome / detail of the activity; required when Task Name is “If other, specify in comments”.Conditional: required when task = “If other” — “Specify the task in comments.”
AttachmentFile attachmentUploadYesNoOptional evidence for the activity.Optional.
Key validations (whole form): • Each product block requires a Product before it counts toward Summary — “Select a product.” • Once Type of Prototyping is chosen, Prototyping Type Name becomes mandatory and must belong to the correct master (Packing Master for Packaging, Business Basket for Product); changing the type re-validates and clears a non-matching name. • Design uploads are restricted to .pdf / .jpg / .jpeg / .png / .svg / .ai / .psd. • The Resources table must always retain at least one row. • Task Log entries require a Task Name, and the “If other” option requires Notes / Outcome to be filled.
Section Review:
PR-PD-003

Product Testing

Part of the Product Development module — captures every lab test conducted on an entrepreneur’s product (existing & required), the NABL / accredited lab, the consent letter signed with the entrepreneur, and the full sample-to-result date chain (sample deposited to office → deposited to lab → test results received). Tests that fail a parameter are flagged for retesting, automatically bridging from the Existing tab into the Required tab.
Key behaviour — the Existing → Required intervention bridge. A test conducted is recorded ONCE in the Existing tab. Toggling its “Need Retesting?” flag does not capture any consent or new test data — it only raises a flag and auto-creates a linked card in the Required tab (Product + Test Type locked / sourced from Existing, with the justification carried across read-only). New tests not previously conducted are added manually in the Required tab. The Required tab is where consent management (core-team approval, consent-letter signed toggle + conditional reason, consent letter upload) and the full date chain live; the Existing tab carries only a shorter date chain (deposit → result).

On-Screen UI Structure

Top bar: teal header “Product Testing” with crumb “PRIME Rural · Quality & Compliance”, and an entrepreneur chip (avatar + name) auto-fetched from the profile.
Tab rail: horizontal pill bar with five tabs — Summary · Existing · Required · Resources · Task Log; the active tab renders teal-on-white (Existing opens by default).
Body layout: each tab is a panel. Summary is read-only: a Test-wise Overview table (one row per test), four Aggregate cards (Existing / Cost / Interventions / Implementation Status), and a 4-cell Intervention-flag strip. Existing and Required render each test as a collapsible “test block” (sea-blue header carrying Product + Test Type selectors, a status / flag chip, and a Remove button) with a Test Details sub-section.
Special widgets: (1) Consent toggle — in the Required tab, a “Consent Letter Signed” checkbox (default on) that reveals a conditional “If not signed, reason” textarea when unchecked, plus a Consent Letter upload / view control and a separate “Approved by Core Team” checkbox. (2) Date chain — ordered date fields: Existing = Date of Sample Deposit → Date of Test Results; Required = Date of Sample Deposit to Office → Date of Sample Deposit to Lab → Date of Test Results. (3) Retesting toggle — a terracotta “Intervention Need” panel in Existing with a “Need Retesting?” toggle + Justification, which bridges to Required. (4) Implementation Status selector + chip on each Required card (Pending / In Progress / Completed / On Hold / Not Feasible). (5) File upload / view pills for Testing Report and Consent Letter.
Per Rule 1, every calculated / autofilled field (all Summary aggregates, the overview table cells, bridged Product / Test Type / Justification, the Reference cells) renders italic on a cream-deep background and is non-editable, with a tooltip describing its source / formula.
Summary
→
Existing
→
Required
→
Resources
→
Task Log

User Journey

Open
Diagnostics → Product Development → Product Testing (entrepreneur auto-fetched)
→
Record Existing
Add each test already conducted; product, test type, lab, deposit & result dates, report upload
→
Flag Retesting
Toggle “Need Retesting?” with justification on any failed / expired test — bridges to Required
→
Plan Required
Required tab → get core-team approval, sign & upload consent, pick lab, run the date chain
→
Track & Upload
Update Implementation Status, upload the test report, attach Resources
→
Log & Save
Capture activities in Task Log; review Summary roll-up; save
Tab 1 — Summary (read-only roll-up)

Entirely auto-populated from the Existing and Required tabs — no data entry on this tab. It contains three blocks: (1) a Test-wise Overview table, (2) four Aggregate cards, (3) an Intervention-flag strip. The Intervention Type and Implementation Status columns reflect the Required tab; rows for manually-added Required tests render on a highlighted background.

1.1 Test-wise Overview table (one row per test; Existing + manual Required; auto-built)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductRead-only text—AutofillProduct name from each Existing block (and any manual Required test). Manual Required tests render on a highlighted row. Rows with neither Product nor Test Type set are skipped.—
Test TypeRead-only text—AutofillTest Type from the block; “—” if unset.—
LabRead-only text—AutofillLab Name from the Existing block; “—” if unset.—
Result DateRead-only date—AutofillDate of Test Results from the block; “—” if unset.—
ReportRead-only badgeYes / —Calculated“Yes” (green, with attachment icon) when a Testing Report file is attached on the block; else “—”.—
Retesting FlaggedRead-only badgeYes / —Calculated“Yes” (amber, warning icon) when the block’s “Need Retesting?” toggle is on; else “—”.—
Intervention TypeRead-only badgeRetesting / + New Test / —AutofillFlagged Existing rows show “Retesting”; manual Required tests show “+ New Test”; else “—”. Cell on a cream / peach background.—
Implementation StatusRead-only badgePending / In Progress / Completed / On Hold / Not FeasibleAutofillMirrors the linked Required card’s status chip. Colour: Completed=green, In Progress=amber, On Hold=blue, Not Feasible=red, Pending=cream. “—” if no linked Required card.—

1.2 Aggregate cards (four, all read-only / calculated)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Existing · Total Tests LoggedRead-only count—CalculatedCount of Existing blocks with a Product or Test Type set.—
Existing · Unique Products TestedRead-only count—CalculatedDistinct count of products across Existing tests.—
Existing · Reports UploadedRead-only count (green)—CalculatedCount of Existing tests with a Testing Report attached.—
Existing · Results ReceivedRead-only count (green)—CalculatedCount of Existing tests with a Date of Test Results filled.—
Existing · Flagged for RetestingRead-only count (amber)—CalculatedCount of Existing tests with the retesting toggle on.—
Cost · Most Used LabRead-only text—CalculatedLab with the highest test count across Existing tests; “—” if none.—
Cost · Unique Labs UsedRead-only count—CalculatedDistinct count of labs used (ignoring “— Select —”).—
Cost · Tests with Deposit DateRead-only count—CalculatedCount of Existing tests with a Date of Sample Deposit filled.—
Cost · Tests Awaiting ResultsRead-only count (amber)—CalculatedCount of Existing tests with a deposit date but NO result date.—
Interventions · Required Tests (Total)Read-only count—CalculatedCount of all Required cards (bridged + manual).—
Interventions · Bridged from ExistingRead-only count (amber)—CalculatedCount of Required cards auto-created from a flagged Existing test.—
Interventions · Added ManuallyRead-only count—CalculatedCount of Required cards added directly via “+ Add New Test”.—
Interventions · Consent SignedRead-only count (green)—CalculatedCount of Required cards whose Consent Letter Signed checkbox is checked.—
Interventions · Approved by Core TeamRead-only count (green)—CalculatedCount of Required cards whose “Approved by Core Team” checkbox is checked.—
Implementation Status · Completed / In Progress / Pending / On Hold / Not FeasibleRead-only counts (Completed green, In Progress amber, Not Feasible red)—CalculatedCount of Required cards by their Implementation Status value.—

1.3 Intervention Flag strip (four count cards)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Tests LoggedRead-only count card—Calculated= Existing · Total Tests Logged.—
Retesting FlaggedRead-only count card—Calculated= Existing · Flagged for Retesting.—
Tests RequiredRead-only count card—Calculated= Interventions · Required Tests (Total).—
CompletedRead-only count card—Calculated= Implementation Status · Completed.—
Tab 2 — Existing (tests already conducted; child table of test blocks)

Caption: “Map all product tests already conducted. Flag any that need retesting via the per-card Intervention toggle — flagged tests automatically appear in the Required tab.” Repeating child table: each row is a collapsible “test block” added via + Add Test (one seed block on load). The block header carries a Product selector, a Test Type selector, a “Retesting Flagged” chip (shown only when flagged) and a Remove button (confirm prompt). Below are all fields of one test row.

2.1 Block header

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductSelect— Select Product — / <Product Master> (Bamboo Shoot Pickle, Lemongrass Tea, Lakadong Turmeric, Eri Silk Muffler, Bamboo Earring, Local Honey, Jadoh Spice Mix …)InputIdentifies the tested product. On change, cascades to the bridged Required card’s (locked) Product if one exists.Required to count in Summary: a block with neither Product nor Test Type is ignored in aggregates.
Test TypeSelect— Test Type — / Microbiological Test / Heavy Metal Test / Pesticide Residue Test / Nutritional Analysis / Shelf Life Test / Moisture Content / pH Test / Curcumin Content (HPLC) / Aflatoxin Test / Sensory / Organoleptic / Packaging Integrity / Fibre Strength (Textile) / Colour Fastness (Textile) / OtherInputThe test performed. Cascades to the bridged Required card’s (locked) Test Type if one exists.—
Retesting Flagged (chip)Read-only chip—CalculatedHidden until the “Need Retesting?” toggle is on; then shows “Retesting Flagged” in terracotta.—

2.2 Test Details (sub-section) — includes the Existing date chain

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Purpose for TestingTextFree text (e.g. “Pre-FSSAI compliance, export sample, shelf life validation”)InputWhy the test was conducted.—
Lab NameSelect— Select — / <Lab Master> (NIFTEM Sonipat / CFTRI Mysuru / Spices Board Lab Cochin / NABL Accredited Local Lab — Shillong / — Guwahati / IIT Guwahati Food Lab / Assam Agricultural University / NEHU Lab / Govt Agmark Lab / NTH North-East / Private Testing Lab / Other)InputLab that conducted the test; feeds Summary “Most Used Lab” and “Unique Labs”.—
Date of Sample Deposit [chain 1]DateCalendar dateInputFirst link in the Existing date chain — when the sample was deposited to the lab.Must be on or before Date of Test Results: “Sample deposit date cannot be after the result date.”
Date of Test Results [chain 2]DateCalendar dateInputSecond link — when results were received. Feeds Summary “Results Received” and the overview Result Date column.Must be on or after Date of Sample Deposit: “Result date cannot be before the sample deposit date.”
Testing ReportAttach (Upload / View)File (.pdf / .jpg / .jpeg / .png)InputLab report upload; shows a file pill with remove (×) and enables the View button once attached. Drives Summary “Reports Uploaded”.—
Date chain (Existing). The two date fields must be ordered: Date of Sample Deposit ≤ Date of Test Results. A test with a deposit date but no result date is counted in Summary as “Awaiting Results”.

2.3 Intervention Need — Retesting toggle (terracotta panel; raises flag, bridges to Required)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Need Retesting?Toggle (on/off)Off (default) / OnInputWhen On: the flag note reads “Flagged → Required tab”, the header “Retesting Flagged” chip appears, the Justification field is revealed, and a linked Required card is auto-created (Product, Test Type, Purpose, Lab and Justification copied across). When toggled Off, the linked Required card is removed.—
JustificationTextarea (conditional)Free text (e.g. “failed parameter, expired report, regulator change, new batch”)InputShown only when the toggle is on. Live-syncs into the bridged Required card’s read-only “Justification (from Existing)” field.—
Tab 3 — Required (tests to be conducted — consent management + full date chain)

Caption: “Tests required — auto-populated from the Existing tab when retesting is flagged, or added manually for new tests to be conducted.” Empty state: “No tests required yet. Flag a test in the Existing tab, or add a new one manually below.” Each card is a collapsible test block added via + Add New Test; header carries Product + Test Type selectors (locked / disabled for bridged cards, title “Locked — sourced from Existing”), a “Bridged from Existing” marker on bridged cards, an Implementation Status chip and a Remove button.

Consent management lives here. Before a required test proceeds, the Fessociate records core-team approval, signs a consent letter with the entrepreneur (toggle), and uploads the signed letter. The consent toggle defaults to ON; un-checking it reveals a mandatory reason. The full date chain (sample → office → lab → result) is also captured on this tab.

3.1 Block header

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductSelect (locked if bridged)— Select Product — / <Product Master>Input / AutofillManual cards: editable. Bridged cards: disabled and sourced from the linked Existing test (cascades on change there).—
Test TypeSelect (locked if bridged)— Test Type — / <Test Type Master>Input / AutofillManual cards: editable. Bridged cards: disabled and sourced from the linked Existing test.—
Bridged from Existing (marker)Read-only marker—AutofillShown only on cards auto-created from a flagged Existing test.—
Implementation Status (chip)Read-only chipPending / In Progress / Completed / On Hold / Not FeasibleAutofillMirrors the Implementation Status selector in 3.3; colour-coded.—

3.2 Test Details (sub-section) — consent management + full date chain

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Purpose for TestingTextFree text (e.g. “Pre-FSSAI compliance, export sample”)Input / AutofillPrefilled from the source Existing test on bridged cards; editable.—
Lab NameSelect— Select — / <Lab Master>Input / AutofillLab chosen for the required test; prefilled from source on bridged cards.—
Approved by Core TeamCheckboxUnchecked (default) / Checked — “Yes — approved by core team”InputRecords core-team sign-off for the test. Feeds Summary “Approved by Core Team” count.—
Consent Letter SignedCheckbox (consent toggle)Checked (default) / Unchecked — “Yes — consent letter signed”InputDefaults to checked. When UNCHECKED, reveals the “If not signed, reason” row below; when re-checked, hides it. Feeds Summary “Consent Signed” count.—
If not signed, reasonTextarea (conditional)Free text (e.g. “Reason for missing consent…”)InputHidden by default; shown only when Consent Letter Signed is unchecked.Required when consent is unchecked: “Provide a reason if the consent letter is not signed.”
Upload Consent LetterAttach (Upload / View)File (.pdf / .jpg / .jpeg / .png)InputSigned consent letter upload; file pill with remove (×) and a View button enabled once attached.—
Date of Sample Deposit to Office [chain 1]DateCalendar dateInputFirst link in the full date chain — sample handed to the PRIME office.Must be ≤ Date of Sample Deposit to Lab: “Office deposit date cannot be after the lab deposit date.”
Date of Sample Deposit to Lab [chain 2]DateCalendar dateInputSecond link — sample forwarded from office to the lab.Must be ≥ office deposit and ≤ result date: “Lab deposit date must fall between the office deposit and the result date.”
Date of Test Results [chain 3]DateCalendar dateInputFinal link — results received from the lab.Must be ≥ Date of Sample Deposit to Lab: “Result date cannot be before the lab deposit date.”
Testing ReportAttach (Upload / View)File (.pdf / .jpg / .jpeg / .png)InputLab report upload; file pill with remove and View.—
Justification (from Existing)Read-only textarea (bridged only, cream-deep)—AutofillShown only on bridged cards; live-synced from the Existing test’s Justification field.—
Date chain (Required, three links). The dates must be strictly ordered: Date of Sample Deposit to Office ≤ Date of Sample Deposit to Lab ≤ Date of Test Results. Any out-of-order entry is rejected with the field-specific message above.

3.3 Implementation Status (terracotta panel)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
StatusSelectPending (default) / In Progress / Completed / On Hold / Not FeasibleInputOn change, updates the header chip (colour-coded) and feeds the Summary Implementation Status counts and the overview table.—
NotesTextareaFree text (“Implementation notes, blockers, next steps…”)InputFree-form progress notes for this required test.—
Tab 4 — Resources (reference materials child table)

Block title “Reference Materials”. Repeating child table added via + Add Resource; at least one row must remain (“At least one row is required.”). Per row:

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Resource TitleTextFree textInputName of the reference material.—
CategorySelect— Select — / Lab Contact / Quotation / Previous Test Report / FSSAI / Regulatory Guideline / NABL Lab List / Consent Letter Template / Test Parameter Reference / Packaging / Shelf Life Standard / Export / Certification Standard / OtherInputClassifies the resource.—
TypeSelect— Select — / Report / Study / Contact / Template / Guideline / Photo / Evidence / QuotationInputResource format / nature.—
Description / NotesTextFree textInputNotes about the resource.—
FileAttach (Upload / View)FileInputUpload a file or View the attached one.—
Tab 5 — Task Log (Product Testing activities child table)

Block title “Task Log — Product Testing Activities” with a live “N entries” counter. Repeating child table added via + Add Entry. Row No. auto-renumbers (01, 02, …) on add / delete. Per row:

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Read-only auto-number—CalculatedSequential index, zero-padded; re-numbers on row delete.—
DateDateCalendar dateInputDate the activity occurred.—
Task NameSelect— Select Task — + the 17 master tasks: 01 Awareness on Importance of Product Testing / 02 Identify Products Requiring Testing / 03 Define Test Parameters Required / 04 Identify Suitable NABL / Accredited Lab / 05 Obtain Lab Quotation / Estimate / 06 Get Core Team Approval for Testing / 07 Prepare and Sign Consent Letter with Entrepreneur / 08 Collect Product Sample from Entrepreneur / 09 Deposit Sample to PRIME Office / 10 Deposit Sample to Lab / 11 Follow Up with Lab on Timeline / 12 Receive and Review Test Report / 13 Share Test Report with Entrepreneur / 14 Document Results in CRM / 15 Flag Failed Parameters — Retesting Required / 16 Initiate Retesting Process / 17 Update Final Status in Module / If other, specify in commentsInputStandardised activity picklist mirroring the product-testing process flow.—
Notes / OutcomeTextFree textInputOutcome / remarks; required when Task Name = “If other, specify in comments”.Conditionally required for “If other”: “Specify the task in notes.”
AttachmentAttach (Upload)FileInputOptional supporting file.—
Key validations (whole form): The date chain must be strictly ordered everywhere — in Existing, Date of Sample Deposit ≤ Date of Test Results; in Required, Date of Sample Deposit to Office ≤ Date of Sample Deposit to Lab ≤ Date of Test Results (out-of-order dates are rejected). Consent-required rules: when “Consent Letter Signed” is unchecked on a Required test, the “If not signed, reason” field is mandatory; the signed consent letter should be uploaded and core-team approval recorded before a required test proceeds to the lab. A flagged Existing test must carry a Justification, which bridges (read-only) into the auto-created Required card; toggling the flag off removes that card, and toggling it on re-creates it with Product / Test Type locked. Required cards added manually keep Product / Test Type editable. Resources require at least one row. File uploads accept .pdf / .jpg / .jpeg / .png only.
Section Review:
PR-PD-004

Product Standardization

The Product Development module that documents each entrepreneur's standardisation efforts — recipe / process / packaging SOPs, hygiene, HACCP, GMP, ISO, organic and GI-tag standards — tracks whether existing SOPs are actually being followed, and bridges flagged gaps into a Required pipeline with an implementation status. It carries an entrepreneur-level Task Log and a Resources library of templates and regulatory references.

On-Screen UI Structure

A teal title bar reads Product Standardization with crumb PRIME Rural · Quality & Compliance. Beneath it a horizontal tab rail — Summary · Existing · Required · Resources · Task Log — with the active tab highlighted teal. An entrepreneur chip (avatar + “Auto-fetched from profile”) sits above the tab body and is the implicit parent of every record.

The Existing and Required tabs each hold a list of collapsible standardisation blocks. Each block carries a sea-green header with two inline selectors — Product (fetched from the Product master) and Standardisation Type (fetched from the Standardization Type master) — a status chip, and a Remove button; clicking the header collapses/expands the body. An Existing block's body is a Standardisation Details sub-section (Product Sector, Upload SOP, SOP URL, Developed By, Version, Is-being-followed Yes/No with conditional reason) plus a terracotta Intervention Need panel carrying a toggle that bridges the block into the Required tab.

A Required block's body holds the same details plus Scope for Standardisation and Approved by Core Team checkboxes, a Collaborating Partner field, an optional read-only Justification (from Existing) when bridged, and a terracotta Implementation Status panel (Status select + Notes) that drives the header chip. Bridged blocks show a “↻ Bridged from Existing” marker and lock their Product / Type selectors.

Special widgets: an upload-with-view control and a URL field with a validated “Open” button on every block; the Resources tab is a reference-materials data table (Title / Category / Type / Notes / File); the Task Log tab is an auto-numbered activity table fed by a fixed 19-step task picker. The Summary tab is entirely read-only — a Standardisation-wise overview table, four aggregate cards and a four-tile flags strip. Auto-fetched fields (Product Sector, the bridged Justification) render italic on a cream-deep background and are never editable, with a “Fetched from X” tooltip (Rule 1).
Summary
→
Existing
→
Required
→
Resources
→
Task Log

User Journey

Open Existing & add standardisation
pick Product + Standardisation Type
→
Document the SOP
upload / URL, Developed By, Version
→
Record if being followed
Yes / No — capture reason if No
→
Flag intervention need
toggle & justify → bridges to Required
→
Plan in Required tab
scope, core-team approval, partner
→
Track to completion
set Implementation Status; log tasks
Tab 1: Summary ▼

Entirely read-only. Recalculated on every switch back to Summary. Holds three blocks — a Standardisation-wise overview table, four aggregate cards, and a four-tile Intervention Flags strip. The overview is one row per existing standardisation, with the last two columns pulled from its linked Required (intervention) block; manually-added Required rows are appended below.

Standardisation-wise Overview Table

Auto-populated from the Existing and Required tabs. Intervention Type and Implementation Status columns (shaded) reflect the Required tab. Blocks with neither a Product nor a Type are skipped.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductRead-only text (bold)—AutofillFrom the Existing block's Product selector; “—” if blank.— (display only)
SectorRead-only text—AutofillMirrors the block's auto-fetched Product Sector; “—” if none.—
Standardisation TypeRead-only text—AutofillFrom the block's Standardisation Type selector.—
SOPRead-only badge📎 Yes / —Calculated“📎 Yes” (green) when an SOP file is attached on the block, else “—”.—
Being FollowedRead-only badgeYes / NoAutofillMirrors the block's Yes/No selector — Yes green, No amber.—
Intervention FlaggedRead-only badge⚠ Yes / —Calculated“⚠ Yes” (amber) when the Intervention toggle is on, else “—”.—
Intervention Type [shaded]Read-only badge↻ Intervention / + New / —Calculated“↻ Intervention” (amber) when flagged from Existing; “+ New” (blue) for a manually-added Required row; “—” otherwise.—
Implementation Status [shaded]Read-only badgePending / In Progress / Completed / On Hold / Not FeasibleCalculatedFrom the linked Required block's status chip — Completed green, In Progress amber, On Hold blue, Not Feasible red, Pending neutral; “—” if no linked Required block.—

Aggregate Cards (4)

Card / MetricField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Existing → Total Standardisations LoggedRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Existing blocks that have a Product or a Type set.—
Existing → Unique Products CoveredRead-only number—CalculatedDistinct Products across logged Existing blocks.—
Existing → SOPs UploadedRead-only number (green)—CalculatedCount of blocks with an SOP file attached.—
Existing → Being Followed (Yes)Read-only number (green)—CalculatedCount where the Yes/No selector = Yes.—
Existing → Not Being Followed (No)Read-only number (amber)—CalculatedCount where the selector = No.—
Cost → Standardisations with SOP URLRead-only number—CalculatedCount of blocks with a non-empty SOP URL.—
Cost → Unique Standardisation TypesRead-only number—CalculatedDistinct Standardisation Types across logged blocks.—
Cost → Most Common TypeRead-only text—CalculatedThe Standardisation Type with the highest frequency; “—” if none.—
Cost → Flagged for InterventionRead-only number (amber)—CalculatedCount of Existing blocks with the Intervention toggle on.—
Interventions → Required Standardisations (Total)Read-only number—CalculatedCount of all Required-tab blocks.—
Interventions → Bridged from ExistingRead-only number (amber)—CalculatedRequired blocks carrying a bridge-source attribute (created via the toggle).—
Interventions → Added ManuallyRead-only number—CalculatedRequired blocks with no bridge source.—
Interventions → Scope IdentifiedRead-only number (green)—CalculatedRequired blocks with the “Scope for Standardisation” checkbox ticked.—
Interventions → Approved by Core TeamRead-only number (green)—CalculatedRequired blocks with the “Approved by Core Team” checkbox ticked.—
Implementation Status → Completed / In Progress / Pending / On Hold / Not FeasibleRead-only numbers—CalculatedCount of Required blocks by status chip value (Completed green, In Progress amber, Not Feasible red).—
Footer: a four-tile Intervention Flags strip mirrors the aggregates — Standardisations Logged (= Total Logged), Intervention Flagged, Required (= Required total) and Completed counts.
Tab 2: Existing (Baseline) — per-standardisation blocks ▼

The baseline working tab: a list of collapsible standardisation blocks documenting existing standardisation efforts per product. The caption instructs the user to flag missing or incomplete standardisations via the per-card Intervention toggle to push them to the Required tab. + Add Standardisation seeds one new collapsed-capable block; the form opens with one block already present.

Block Header

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductSelect (link to Product)Bamboo Shoot Pickle / Lemongrass Tea / Lakadong Turmeric / Eri Silk Muffler / Bamboo Earring / Local Honey / Jadoh Spice Mix …InputOptions fetched from the Product master. On change it auto-fetches Product Sector and cascades Product + Type to any bridged Required block.A block needs a Product or a Type to count in Summary roll-ups.
Standardisation TypeSelect (link to Standardization Type)Recipe Standardisation / Process SOP / Packaging SOP / Hygiene & Sanitation SOP / HACCP / GMP / ISO 22000 / ISO 9001 / BRC / Organic (NPOP) / Hand-Loom · Hand-Made / GI Tag Compliance SOP / OtherInputOptions fetched from the Standardization Type master. On change it cascades the Type to any bridged Required block.—
Intervention Flagged chipRead-only chip⚠ Intervention FlaggedCalculatedHidden by default; shown (terracotta) in the header when the block's Intervention toggle is on.—
RemoveAction button—InputDeletes the block (and its bridged Required block, if any) after confirmation.Confirm: “Remove this standardisation entry?”

Standardisation Details

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Product SectorRead-only texte.g. Food & Beverages — Pickles / Spices & Herbs / Handloom & TextileAutofillFetched from the Product master via the product→sector map when a Product is chosen; blank until then.— (read-only)
Upload SOPFile attach + View.pdf / .doc / .docx / .jpg / .jpeg / .pngInputUploads a single SOP file shown as a removable pill; the View button enables once a file is attached. Presence of a file drives the Summary “SOP” and “SOPs Uploaded” metrics.One file per block (re-upload replaces).
SOP URLURL text + Open buttonhttps://…InputFree URL to an externally hosted SOP. The “Open” button enables only when the value matches ^https?:// and opens it in a new tab.Open disabled until a valid http(s) URL is entered.
Developed ByTexte.g. Prime Rural team, partner consultant, selfInputWho authored the SOP.—
VersionTexte.g. v1.0, 2024.03InputSOP version / revision tag.—
Is the standardisation being followed?Segmented Yes / NoYes (default) / NoInputYes/No toggle on compliance. Choosing “No” reveals the conditional reason field; feeds the Summary “Being Followed” column and Followed/Not-Followed aggregates.Defaults to Yes.
If no, give reasons [conditional]Textarea—InputShown only when “No” is selected. Captures why the SOP is not followed (training gap, equipment unavailable, unclear instructions…).— (recommended when No)

Intervention Need (terracotta panel)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
New Intervention in Standardising?Toggle (on / off)Off (default) / OnInputTurning on sets the flag note to “↻ Flagged → Required tab”, shows the header chip, reveals Justification, and bridges a new block into the Required tab (locked Product/Type copied across). Turning off removes that bridged block.—
Flag noteRead-only labelNot flagged / ↻ Flagged → Required tabCalculatedMirrors the toggle state; emphasised (terracotta) when flagged.—
Justification [conditional]Textarea—InputShown only when flagged. Reason for intervention (outdated SOP, regulatory change, scale-up, certification requirement…). Live-synced into the bridged Required block's read-only “Justification (from Existing)” field.— (recommended when flagged)
Tab 3: Required — intervention pipeline ▼

Standardisations required — either auto-populated (bridged) from the Existing tab when its Intervention toggle is on, or added manually via + Add New Standardisation. When empty, an italic empty-message prompts the user to flag an Existing standardisation or add one manually. Bridged blocks show a “↻ Bridged from Existing” marker and lock their Product / Type (sourced from Existing); manual blocks keep both selectors editable.

Block Header

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductSelect (link to Product)Same Product master listInput / AutofillEditable for manual blocks (auto-fetches Sector on change); locked & pre-filled for bridged blocks (“Locked — sourced from Existing tab”).—
Standardisation TypeSelect (link to Standardization Type)Same Standardisation Type master listInput / AutofillEditable for manual blocks; locked & pre-filled for bridged blocks.—
Bridged markerRead-only chip↻ Bridged from ExistingCalculatedShown only on bridged blocks (terracotta).—
Implementation chipRead-only chipPending (default) / In Progress / Completed / On Hold / Not FeasibleCalculatedReflects the Implementation Status select; colour-coded (Completed green, In Progress amber, On Hold blue, Not Feasible red, Pending neutral).—
RemoveAction button—InputFor a bridged block, also turns the source Existing toggle off (clearing its flag) before removal.Confirm: “Remove this required standardisation?”

Standardisation Details

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Product SectorRead-only text—AutofillFetched from the Product master when a Product is set (copied across on bridge).— (read-only)
Scope for StandardisationCheckboxYes — scope identifiedInputMarks that scope for standardisation has been identified; feeds the “Scope Identified” aggregate.—
Approved by Core TeamCheckboxYes — approved by core teamInputCore-team sign-off; feeds the “Approved by Core Team” aggregate. (In the doctype this is a perm-level-1 / restricted field.)—
Collaborating PartnerTexte.g. NIFTEM, Spices Board, partner consultant, self-developedInputPartner / expert assisting with the standardisation.—
Upload SOPFile attach + View.pdf / .doc / .docx / .jpg / .jpeg / .pngInputUploads the developed / final SOP; View enables once attached.One file per block (re-upload replaces).
SOP URLURL text + Open buttonhttps://…InputExternally hosted SOP link; Open enables only for valid http(s) URLs.Open disabled until a valid URL is entered.
