VersionTexte.g. v1.0, 2024.03InputSOP version tag.—
RemarksTextarea—InputImplementation notes, scope clarifications, partner-discussion summary.—
Justification (from Existing) [bridged only]Read-only textarea—AutofillShown only on bridged blocks. Live-synced from the source Existing block's Justification field; not editable here.— (read-only)

Implementation Status (terracotta panel)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
StatusSelectPending (default) / In Progress / Completed / On Hold / Not FeasibleInputDrives the header chip colour and the Summary Implementation-Status aggregates. (The underlying doctype currently exposes In-Progress / Completed / Dropped; the mockup uses the richer five-state set.)—
NotesTextarea—InputImplementation notes, blockers, next steps.—
Tab 4: Resources — Reference Materials ▼

A reference-materials data table for SOP templates and regulatory / certification references. + Add Resource appends a row; the ✕ button deletes a row (at least one row must remain).

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Resource TitleText—InputName of the reference material.—
CategorySelectSOP Template / FSSAI · Regulatory Guideline / HACCP Reference / GMP Standard / ISO · BRC Standard / GI Tag Compliance Document / Partner · Consultant Contact / Training Material / Organic Certification Reference / OtherInputClassifies the resource.—
TypeSelectTemplate / Guideline / Report · Study / Contact / Specification / Photo · EvidenceInputFormat of the resource.—
Description / NotesText—InputFree notes about the resource.—
FileUpload + View buttons—InputAttach & preview the resource file.—
Delete (✕)Action button—InputRemoves the row.Blocked on the last row: “At least one row is required.”
Tab 5: Task Log — Standardisation Activities ▼

A chronological activity log for standardisation work. + Add Entry appends an auto-numbered row; a live counter shows the number of entries. Rows renumber on delete. (Backed by the DF Log Book Child / Direct Log Book Child tables.)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Read-only sequence01, 02, …CalculatedTwo-digit auto index; renumbered when a row is deleted.—
DateDate—InputWhen the activity occurred.—
Task NameSelect01 Awareness on Importance of Product Standardisation / 02 Identify Products Requiring Standardisation / 03 Define Standardisation Type Required / 04 Identify Collaborating Partner · Expert / 05 Get Core Team Approval / 06 Develop Recipe Standardisation / 07 Develop Process SOP / 08 Develop Packaging SOP / 09 Develop Hygiene & Sanitation SOP / 10 Conduct HACCP · GMP Assessment / 11 Review SOP with Entrepreneur / 12 Train Entrepreneur · Workers on SOP / 13 Trial Run — SOP Implementation / 14 Evaluate Trial Outcome / 15 Finalise and Version SOP Document / 16 Upload SOP to CRM / 17 Follow-up — Is SOP Being Followed? / 18 Flag Non-compliance — Intervention Required / 19 Update Status in Module / If other, specify in commentsInputFixed 19-step task picker (plus an “other” option).—
Notes / OutcomeText—InputWhat happened / the outcome of the task.—
AttachmentUpload button—InputOptional supporting file.—
Delete (✕)Action button—InputRemoves the entry and renumbers remaining rows.—
Key validations (whole form): the parent record is keyed to one Entrepreneur (unique, required — auto-fetched into the chip); Module is fixed to Product Development and Applicable-To to Product (both read-only). An Existing block must have a Product or a Standardisation Type to count in Summary roll-ups. The “If no, give reasons” field shows only when Being-Followed = No, and Justification shows only when the Intervention toggle is on. Turning the toggle on creates exactly one bridged Required block (Product / Type locked & copied, Justification live-synced); turning it off — or removing the Required block — clears the flag on both sides. SOP URL “Open” / “Resource Open” buttons enable only for valid http(s):// values. One SOP file per block (re-upload replaces). Resources and Task Log require at least one row to remain. All Indian number formatting; no rejected/pending state — status uses the five-state implementation set.
Section Review:
PR-PD-005

Quality Control

Part of the Product Development module — a maturity-style Quality Control assessment in which the Fessociate rates the entrepreneur’s premises against 23 standard QC practices on a 1–5 star scale (Existing), sets a target rating profile (Optimized / Required), and the system auto-computes a per-practice gap, priority and an aggregate QC score (mean of all rated stars) with a maturity tier and rating distribution.
Key behaviour — star-rated maturity scale with an Existing → Required gap engine. The same fixed list of 23 QC practices appears in both the Existing and Optimized tabs. In Existing each practice is rated 1–5 stars on what is observed at the entrepreneur’s premises; in Optimized the target star level is set. For every practice rated in both tabs, the system calculates Gap = Target − Existing and derives a Priority (Critical ≥ 3, High ≥ 2, Low = 1, At Target = 0). The aggregate QC score per tab is the mean of all rated stars, rounded to a maturity tier. A single “Intervention Need” toggle (with justification) on the Existing tab flags the whole assessment for improvement; the Optimized tab then carries one Implementation Status.

On-Screen UI Structure

Top bar: teal header “Quality Control” with crumb “PRIME Rural · Quality & Compliance”, and an entrepreneur chip (avatar + name) auto-fetched from the profile.
Tab rail: horizontal pill bar with five tabs — Summary · Existing · Optimized · Resources · Task Log; the active tab renders teal-on-white (Summary opens by default).
Body layout: each tab is a panel. Summary is read-only: a Practice-wise Overview table (one row per practice, Existing vs Target with gap + priority), a 2×2 grid of four Aggregate cards (Existing Assessment / Required Target / Interventions / Rating Distribution) and a 4-cell Intervention-flag strip. Existing and Optimized each show a 5-tier star Legend strip followed by a 23-row Assessment table and an aggregate Average strip.
Special widgets: (1) Star-rating control — each of the 23 assessment rows carries a 5-star clickable rating (hover preview, click to set, × clear button) plus a free-text Notes field. (2) Legend strip — a 5-cell key mapping 1–5 stars to maturity labels (Not aware / Not practiced → Fully implemented). (3) Average strip — a teal gradient panel showing Practices Rated (n / 23), the numeric Average Rating and an average-star rendering. (4) Intervention Need toggle (Existing) — an on/off switch “Need improvement in Quality Control?” that reveals a conditional Justification textarea. (5) Implementation Status selector + Notes (Optimized). (6) Distribution bars — a 5-tier bar chart of how many practices fall in each star band.
Per Rule 1, every calculated / autofilled field (all Summary aggregates and overview cells, both Average strips, the per-practice Gap and Priority, the maturity tier, the distribution counts) renders italic on a cream-deep background and is non-editable, with a tooltip describing its source / formula.
Summary
→
Existing
→
Optimized
→
Resources
→
Task Log

User Journey

Open
Diagnostics → Product Development → Quality Control (entrepreneur auto-fetched)
→
Rate Existing
Existing tab → star-rate all 23 QC practices 1–5 against what is observed; add per-practice notes
→
Flag Need
Toggle “Need improvement?” with justification if quality control gaps warrant intervention
→
Set Target
Optimized tab → set the target star level per practice; system computes gap & priority
→
Track
Set Implementation Status + notes; attach Resources; review Summary roll-up & QC score
→
Log & Save
Capture visits / trainings in Task Log; save
Tab 1 — Summary (read-only roll-up; QC score & gap engine)

Entirely auto-populated from the Existing and Optimized tabs — no data entry on this tab. It contains three blocks: (1) a Practice-wise Overview table, (2) a 2×2 grid of four Aggregate cards, (3) an Intervention-flag strip. Rows with a gap ≥ 2 render on an amber background (row-gap2); gap ≥ 3 render orange (row-gap3).

1.1 Practice-wise Overview table (one row per practice; auto-built; 23 rows)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
#Read-only index01–23CalculatedZero-padded practice number matching the fixed practice list.—
PracticeRead-only text—AutofillThe QC practice text from the fixed list (e.g. “Raw material inspection before use”).—
Existing RatingRead-only stars + value— / 1–5 starsAutofillStar rating set for this practice in the Existing tab; renders “Not rated” if unset. Cell on a peach background.—
Required TargetRead-only stars + value— / 1–5 starsAutofillTarget star rating set in the Optimized tab; renders “Not set” if unset. Cell on a green background.—
GapRead-only signed number— / 0 / +1…+4Calculated= Required Target − Existing Rating, shown only when both are set; colour-coded (red ≥ 3, amber ≥ 2, green = 0). “—” if either rating is missing.—
PriorityRead-only badgeCritical / High / Low / At Target / —CalculatedDerived from Gap: gap ≥ 3 → Critical (red); gap ≥ 2 → High (amber); gap = 1 → Low (cream); gap = 0 → At Target (green); “—” when gap cannot be computed.—

1.2 Aggregate cards — 2×2 grid (four cards, all read-only / calculated)

Card A — Existing Assessment

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Practices RatedRead-only countn / 23CalculatedCount of practices given an Existing star rating, out of 23.—
Average RatingRead-only decimal— / 1.00–5.00CalculatedQC score = mean of all rated Existing stars (sum ÷ rated count), to 2 decimals; “—” when nothing is rated.—
Maturity TierRead-only labelNot aware / Not practiced · Aware but not implemented · Partially implemented · Mostly implemented · Fully implementedCalculatedTier label keyed by round(Average Rating); coloured green (tier ≥ 4) or red (tier ≤ 2).—
Fully Implemented (★★★★★)Read-only count (green)—CalculatedCount of Existing practices rated exactly 5 stars.—
Not Aware / Not Practiced (★)Read-only count (red)—CalculatedCount of Existing practices rated exactly 1 star.—
Intervention FlaggedRead-only Yes / No (amber)Yes / NoCalculated“Yes” when the Existing “Need improvement?” toggle is on; else “No”.—

Card B — Required (Target Profile)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Practices SetRead-only countn / 23CalculatedCount of practices given a Target star rating in the Optimized tab, out of 23.—
Target AverageRead-only decimal— / 1.00–5.00CalculatedMean of all set Target stars, to 2 decimals; “—” when none set.—
Target TierRead-only label(same 5 maturity labels)CalculatedTier label keyed by round(Target Average).—
Implementation StatusRead-only labelPending / In Progress / Completed / On Hold / Not FeasibleAutofillMirrors the Implementation Status selector on the Optimized tab.—

Card C — Interventions (gap analytics)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
High Priority Gaps (gap ≥ 2)Read-only count (red)—CalculatedCount of practices whose Gap (Target − Existing) is ≥ 2.—
Critical Gaps (gap ≥ 3)Read-only count (red)—CalculatedCount of practices whose Gap is ≥ 3.—
Already at Target (gap = 0)Read-only count (green)—CalculatedCount of practices rated in both tabs with Gap = 0.—
Average Gap (across rated)Read-only decimal— / 0.0–4.0CalculatedMean of positive gaps across practices rated in both tabs (negative gaps floored at 0), to 1 decimal; “—” if none comparable.—

Card D — Rating Distribution (Existing)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
1★ … 5★ barsRead-only bar chart—CalculatedFive tier rows (1★–5★), each a coloured bar (tier-1 poor → tier-5 good) sized to count ÷ max-bucket, with the count to the right. Empty state: “Rate practices in the Existing tab to see distribution.”—

1.3 Intervention Flag strip (four count cards)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Practices RatedRead-only count card—Calculated= Existing · Practices Rated (count only).—
Intervention FlaggedRead-only Yes / No cardYes / NoCalculated= Existing “Need improvement?” toggle state.—
High Priority GapsRead-only count card—Calculated= count of gaps ≥ 2.—
At TargetRead-only count card—Calculated= count of practices with gap = 0.—
Tab 2 — Existing (rate the 23 QC practices 1–5)

Caption: “Rate each practice on a 1–5 scale based on what you observe at the entrepreneur’s premises.” The tab shows the 5-tier Legend strip, then the Assessment table of all 23 practices, then the Average strip, then the terracotta Intervention Need panel.

2.1 Legend strip (rating key, read-only)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Star Scale KeyRead-only legend (5 cells)1★ Not aware / Not practiced · 2★ Aware but not implemented · 3★ Partially implemented · 4★ Mostly implemented · 5★ Fully implementedStaticFixed maturity key shown above the assessment for rater reference; identical key appears on the Optimized tab.—

2.2 Assessment table — per-practice row fields

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Row No.Read-only index01–23CalculatedFixed zero-padded practice number.—
Practice (Question)Read-only text—StaticThe QC practice from the fixed list (see 2.3 below).—
RatingStar rating (1–5)1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5 starsInputClick a star to set the rating (hover previews); value feeds Existing average, distribution, tier and the per-practice gap.—
Clear (×)Action button—InputClears the rating for the row, returning it to “Not rated” and removing it from all aggregates.—
NotesTextFree textInputOptional per-practice observation note.—

2.3 The 23 fixed QC practices (each rated 1–5 stars; identical list in Existing & Optimized)

#Practice (Question)Rating TypeScoring
01Is Entrepreneur aware of Quality and Control practices?1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
02Raw material inspection before use1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
03Batch labeling done1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
04Expiry date tracking1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
05Rejection of defective products1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
06Standard measurement tools used1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
07Written SOP available1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
08Clean Production Workspace1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
09Personal protective gear (gloves, cap, apron)1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
10Separate raw and finished goods area1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
11Equipment cleaning schedule1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
12Pest control measures1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
13Proper ventilation1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
14Handwashing facility available1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
15Use of clean water1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
16Covered waste bins1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
17Nails trimmed / no jewellery1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
18Regular cleaning of utensils1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
19Raw materials off the floor1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
20Use of pallets or racks1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
21FIFO (First In First Out) followed1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
22Storage away from moisture1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
23Labelled storage containers1–5 starsStar value feeds mean QC score
Aggregate QC score (per tab). Average Rating = (sum of all rated stars) ÷ (number of practices rated). Only rated practices are included — unrated practices do not drag the mean down. The result is shown to 2 decimals and mapped to a Maturity Tier via round(): 1 = Not aware / Not practiced, 2 = Aware but not implemented, 3 = Partially implemented, 4 = Mostly implemented, 5 = Fully implemented. The 5-star “Average” rendering fills round(Average) stars.

2.4 Average strip (teal gradient summary, read-only)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Practices RatedRead-only countn / 23CalculatedLive count of practices given a star rating in this tab.—
Average RatingRead-only decimal— / 1.00–5.00CalculatedMean of rated stars (the QC score), 2 decimals; “—” when nothing rated.—
Average (stars)Read-only star render★…☆ (0–5 filled)Calculatedround(Average Rating) filled stars over a 5-star track.—

2.5 Intervention Need (terracotta panel) — whole-assessment flag

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Need improvement in Quality Control?Toggle (on/off)Off (default) / OnInputWhen On: the note reads “Flagged → Set target profile in Optimized tab”, the Justification field is revealed, and Summary “Intervention Flagged” = Yes. When Off, note reads “Not flagged”.—
JustificationTextarea (conditional)Free text (e.g. “gaps observed, regulatory drivers, scale-up readiness”)InputShown only when the toggle is on; captures what practices need improvement and why.—
Tab 3 — Optimized (set target rating profile + Implementation Status)

Caption: “Set the target rating profile — what level the entrepreneur should reach for each practice.” Mirrors the Existing tab structure (Legend → Target Assessment table over the same 23 practices → Target Average strip), then adds a terracotta Implementation Status panel. Setting a Target star for a practice rated in Existing drives the per-practice Gap and Priority in Summary.

3.1 Target Assessment table — per-practice row fields (same 23 practices as 2.3)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Row No.Read-only index01–23CalculatedFixed zero-padded practice number.—
Practice (Question)Read-only text—StaticSame fixed QC practice list as the Existing tab.—
Target RatingStar rating (1–5)1 / 2 / 3 / 4 / 5 starsInputTarget maturity level for the practice; feeds Target average, tier and Gap = Target − Existing.—
Clear (×)Action button—InputClears the target for the row (“Not set”), removing it from Target aggregates and gap.—
Gap & Priority engine. For each practice set in both tabs: Gap = Target Rating − Existing Rating (negative gaps treated as 0 in averages). Priority: gap ≥ 3 → Critical; gap ≥ 2 → High; gap = 1 → Low; gap = 0 → At Target. These feed the Summary overview rows (amber at gap ≥ 2, orange at gap ≥ 3) and the Interventions card counts.

3.2 Target Average strip (teal gradient summary, read-only)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Practices SetRead-only countn / 23CalculatedCount of practices given a Target star rating.—
Target AverageRead-only decimal— / 1.00–5.00CalculatedMean of set Target stars, 2 decimals.—
Target (stars)Read-only star render★…☆ (0–5 filled)Calculatedround(Target Average) filled stars.—

3.3 Implementation Status (terracotta panel)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
StatusSelectPending (default) / In Progress / Completed / On Hold / Not FeasibleInputTracks progress against the target profile; mirrored to Summary Card B “Implementation Status”.—
NotesTextareaFree text (“Implementation notes, blockers, next steps…”)InputFree-form progress notes for the QC improvement plan.—
Tab 4 — Resources (reference materials)

Caption: “Reference materials and external links useful to support Quality Control standardisation.” A grid of resource tiles, each with a title, a short description and an open / download link. The mockup ships six standing tiles:

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Resource TitleRead-only textFSSAI Schedule 4 (Hygiene & Sanitation) / HACCP Plan Template / 5S Workplace Checklist / FIFO & Storage Best Practices / Production SOP Template (Editable) / Personal Protective Gear ChecklistStaticTile heading for each reference material.—
DescriptionRead-only textFree text per tile (e.g. “Mandatory hygiene requirements for food businesses…”)StaticOne-line summary of what the resource is for.—
Link / DownloadHyperlinkExternal URL (e.g. FSSAI portal) / template / checklist / .docx downloadStaticOpens the reference (new tab) or downloads the file; FSSAI links to the live portal.—
Tab 5 — Task Log (visits, trainings & interventions child table)

Caption: “Log every visit, training, and intervention conducted to improve quality practices.” Repeating child table added via + Add Log Entry; row No. auto-renumbers (01, 02, …) on add / delete. New rows default Date to today. Per row:

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Read-only auto-number—CalculatedSequential zero-padded index; re-numbers on row delete.—
DateDateCalendar date (defaults to today)InputDate the activity occurred.—
Action TakenTextFree text (e.g. “Site visit, Hygiene training”)InputWhat was done.—
Done ByTextFree text (Field officer name)InputPerson who conducted the activity.—
Outcome / NotesTextareaFree text (“What was done, what changed…”)InputOutcome / remarks for the activity.—
Delete (×)Action button—InputRemoves the row and re-numbers the table.—
Key validations (whole form): The aggregate QC score on each tab is the mean of rated stars only (sum ÷ rated count) — unrated practices are excluded, not counted as zero. Existing and Optimized share the same fixed 23-practice list; a per-practice Gap (Target − Existing) and Priority are computed only when a practice is rated in both tabs, otherwise Gap / Priority show “—”. Average Gap floors negative gaps at 0. Maturity Tier is keyed by round(average) onto the five fixed labels. The Existing “Need improvement?” toggle, when on, reveals (and expects) a Justification and sets Summary “Intervention Flagged” = Yes. Task Log rows auto-renumber on add / delete and seed Date to today.
Section Review:
PR-PD-006

Product Packaging

Product Development framework (DocType DF Packaging) that records, per product variant, every existing packaging item — type, material, source, dimensions, capacity, price, print-capability and sealing — together with a months-as-rows monthly order/quantity grid that aggregates spend across the cohort year. Each variant’s annual packaging cost feeds the Packaging line of Unit Pricing, and an intervention bridge carries flagged items into a Required tab for replacement / new packaging.
Golden Thread. Variants are selected from a dropdown sourced from the entrepreneur’s Product Profile — they are not auto-prepopulated (the framework records only the variants the entrepreneur actually packages). The shared Monthly Quantity Ordered grid spans cohort years (Rule 11): months are rows, each variant’s packaging type is a column, and Annual Cost = Σ(month qty × price/unit) auto-fetches to Existing Unit Pricing → Packaging. Toggling Packaging Intervention Needed? in the Existing tab auto-creates a linked row in the Required tab (intervention bridge, Rule 3); removing the Required row clears the flag, and vice-versa.

On-Screen UI Structure

A teal title bar reads Packaging Module with crumb PRIME Rural · DF Packaging. Below it sits a horizontal tab rail — Summary · Existing · Required · Requirement Tracker · Resources · Task Log · Log Book — the active tab highlighted teal. An entrepreneur chip (“Auto-fetched from profile”) sits at the top of every panel; Entrepreneur, District, Block, Module (Product Development) and Applicable To (Product) all autofill from the Enterprenur Profile (Rule 2).

Summary is read-only: a product-wise overview table (one row per packaging type per variant, the two right-most columns shaded green for New Price & Saving), four aggregate cards (Existing / Cost / Interventions / Implementation Status) and a four-tile Intervention Flags strip.

Existing renders each variant as a collapsible block (sea-green header carrying an inline variant-selector dropdown + SKU). Each block holds Section I — a repeating packaging detail table (one row per packaging item: type, material, source, L×B×H dimensions with unit, capacity + unit, price/unit, supports-printing, picture, and a multi-select sealing pill group) — and Section III, a terracotta Intervention Need banner with packaging + sealing toggle/justify pairs per type. Below all blocks sits the shared Section II Monthly Quantity Ordered grid (months ↓ rows, variant packaging types → columns) and a teal total bar.

Required has two numbered sections: Section I Packaging Interventions (bridge-populated rows showing the originating Product / SKU / Current Type / Material / Price / Annual Qty as read-only, then an editable Required Packaging detail row + sealing + Implementation Status) and Section II New Packaging (standalone items added freely). Requirement Tracker mirrors Section II for the required side (Monthly Quantity Required grid). Resources, Task Log (14 seeded activities) and Log Book close the rail.

Calculated fields (Month Total ₹, Annual qty per type, Annual Cost, Avg Monthly Qty, Saving / Unit) render italic on cream-deep background, are never editable, and each carries a formula tooltip (Rule 1).
Summary
→
Existing
→
Required
→
Requirement Tracker
→
Resources
→
Task Log
→
Log Book

User Journey

Open Packaging
EP dashboard · Diagnostics → Product Development
→
Add block & pick variant
Existing — variant dropdown from Product Profile
→
Record packaging detail rows
type, material, source, dims, capacity, price, printing, sealing
→
Fill Monthly Quantity grid
Section II — per month × type; Annual Cost auto-calcs
→
Flag intervention & act
Section III toggle → Required row; add New Packaging
→
Log activity & Save
Task Log / Log Book; Summary & Unit Pricing update
Tab 1: Summary ▼

Entirely read-only. Auto-populated from the Existing and Required tabs — no direct data entry. Recalculates on every switch back to Summary and on any change in Existing / Required.

Product-wise Packaging Overview Table

One row per packaging type per variant. Existing-side cells terracotta-tinted; Required-side (New Price / Saving) green-tinted. New-packaging rows are highlighted (cream) and carry no Existing price / saving.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ProductRead-only text (bold)—AutofillThe variant name from the Existing block selector, or for New packaging the Product / Purpose field.— (display only)
Packing TypeRead-only textPouch / Bottle / Box / Jar / TinAutofillFrom Type of Packaging on the packaging detail row.—
MaterialRead-only text—AutofillFrom the row’s Material field.—
Price / Unit (₹)Read-only currency—AutofillExisting row’s Price Per Unit; shows “—” if 0. Indian formatting (e.g. ₹ 1,250.00).—
Avg Monthly QtyRead-only number—Calculated= Annual Qty for this variant+type ÷ 12 (one decimal); “—” when annual qty is 0.—
Intervention TypeRead-only badge↻ Replacement / + New Packaging / —AutofillShows “↻ Replacement” when the type’s packaging-intervention toggle is ON; “+ New Packaging” (blue) for Section II rows; else “—”.—
Implementation StatusRead-only badgePending / In Progress / Completed / Replace Existing / Not Feasible (or On Hold for New)AutofillMirrors the status of the linked Required row. Completed → green, In Progress / Replace Existing → amber, Not Feasible → red, else neutral.—
New Price / Unit (₹) [shaded]Read-only currency—AutofillRequired intervention row’s Price Per Unit (for New-packaging rows, the new item’s own price); “—” until set.—
Saving / Unit (₹) [shaded]Read-only—Calculated= Existing Price/Unit − New Price/Unit. Down-arrow & green when positive, up-arrow & red when negative; “—” for New rows.—
Empty state shows “Auto-populated from Existing and Required tabs”. The Cost card’s Total Annual Cost is the value that auto-fetches to Existing Unit Pricing → Packaging.

Aggregate Cards (4)

Card / MetricField TypeInput / AutofillLogic
Existing → Products with PackagingRead-only numberCalculatedCount of Existing variant blocks carrying at least one packaging row.
Existing → Total Packaging ItemsRead-only numberCalculatedTotal packaging detail rows across all variants (plus New-packaging rows).
Existing → Annual Qty OrderedRead-only numberCalculatedΣ of every cell in the Monthly Quantity Ordered grid. Indian formatting.
Existing → Annual Cost (₹)Read-only currency (green)Calculated= Σ(month qty × type price/unit) across the grid.
Existing → Print-Ready ItemsRead-only number (green)CalculatedCount of rows where Supports Printing? = Yes.
Existing → No-Print ItemsRead-only number (amber)CalculatedCount where Supports Printing? = No.
Cost → Total Products / Items / Annual CostRead-onlyCalculatedMirror of the counts above; footer note: “Auto-fetches to Existing Unit Pricing → Packaging.”
Interventions → Items FlaggedRead-only number (amber)CalculatedCount of packaging rows whose type has the packaging-intervention toggle ON.
Interventions → Bridged to RequiredRead-only numberCalculatedCount of Required Section I intervention rows.
Interventions → New Packaging AddedRead-only numberCalculatedCount of Required Section II New-packaging rows.
Interventions → Replace ExistingRead-only number (amber)CalculatedCount of Required rows with status = Replace Existing.
Implementation Status → Completed / In Progress / Pending / Not FeasibleRead-only numbersCalculatedStatus counts across all Required rows (intervention + new). Completed green, In Progress amber, Not Feasible red.

Intervention Flags Strip (4 tiles)

TileField TypeInput / AutofillLogic
↻ Intervention NeededRead-only count (amber icon)Calculated= Items Flagged count.
⇄ Replacement RequiredRead-only count (blue icon)Calculated= Required rows with status = Replace Existing.
! No-Print PackagingRead-only count (red icon)Calculated= No-Print Items count.
✓ Print-Ready PackagingRead-only count (green icon)Calculated= Print-Ready Items count.
Tab 2: Existing ▼

One collapsible block per product variant. “+ Add Product” appends a block; the header carries a variant-selector dropdown and an SKU field. Each block holds Section I (packaging detail rows) and Section III (intervention need). The shared Section II monthly grid and total bar sit below all blocks. (Stored in child tables Packaging Info Existing + Monthly Ordered Quantity Existing.)

Block Header — Variant Selection

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Product Variant *Select / Link dropdownVariants from the entrepreneur’s Product ProfileInput (chosen from dropdown)Selected from a dropdown sourced from Product Profile — NOT auto-prepopulated. Only variants the entrepreneur actually packages are added. The chosen variant labels this block and its columns in the shared monthly grid.“Select a product variant.” A variant cannot be selected twice across blocks.
SKUTextFree textInputOptional reference; surfaces in Summary and on the linked Required row.—

Section I — Packaging Details (repeating row per packaging item)

“+ Add Row” appends a packaging item; at least one row is required per variant.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Type of Packaging *SelectPouch / Bottle / Box / Jar / TinInputDrives the column shown for this variant in the shared monthly grid and the row created in Section III. Same type added twice for a variant is de-duplicated in Section III.“Type of Packaging is required.”
MaterialSelectBOPP / Kraft Paper / PET / Glass / HDPE / Aluminium FoilInputSurfaces in Summary and on the bridged Required row (Current Material).—
Name of Purchase SourceTextFree text (supplier name)Input——
Dimensions (L × B × H)Three numeric inputs + unit SelectL, B, H numbers; unit mm / cm / m / inInputCaptured as a trio with a shared unit. Used as a physical spec reference.Values must be non-negative.
CapacityNumber (2-decimal)≥ 0InputPairs with the Unit field below to describe pack size.Must be ≥ 0.
UnitSelectgm / ml / kg / L / pcsInputUnit of the Capacity value.—
Price Per Unit (₹)Currency (2-decimal)≥ 0InputMultiplied by each month’s ordered qty in the shared grid to compute Month Total & Annual Cost. Feeds Unit Pricing.Must be ≥ 0. Indian formatting.
Supports Printing?SelectYes / NoInputDrives Print-Ready vs No-Print aggregate counts and flag tiles.—
PictureAttach (Upload button)Image fileInputReference photo of the packaging item.—
Type of Sealing ExistingMulti-select pillsInduction / Band / Bottleneck / ROPP / Bottle CapInput (toggle pills)One or more sealing methods used on this packaging item; rendered as toggle pills below the row.—

Section II — Monthly Quantity Ordered (shared, all variants)

Transposed grid: months are rows, each variant’s packaging type is a column (columns auto-populate from every block’s Section I). Spans cohort years (Rule 11). Stored as child table Monthly Ordered Quantity Existing → DF Packaging Monthly Order.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Order MonthRow labelApr … Mar (financial-year order)SystemFixed 12-month axis; each Existing entry maps to order_month in DF Packaging Monthly Order.—
Order YearIntCohort year(s)InputRecords which cohort year the quantity belongs to — the grid aggregates across cohort years (Rule 11).“Order Year is required.”
Quantity Ordered (per month × type)Number≥ 0InputOne editable cell per month per variant-type column. Re-renders preserve entries keyed by month + type.Must be ≥ 0 integer.
Price Per UnitCurrency—AutofillCarried from the matching Section I row (price at order time); shown in each column header.—
Month Total (₹)Read-only currency—Calculated= Σ over types (month qty × type price/unit) for that row.—
Annual Total per Type (units)Read-only number—Calculated= Σ of the 12 month cells for that variant-type column; feeds Avg Monthly Qty and the bridged row’s Annual Qty.—
Annual Grand Total (₹)Read-only currency—Calculated= Σ of all Month Totals. This is the Annual Cost that auto-fetches to Existing Unit Pricing → Packaging.—

Section III — Intervention Need (per variant, per type)

Auto-lists one group per distinct packaging type in the variant’s Section I, each with two toggle/justify pairs.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Packaging Intervention Needed?ToggleOn / OffInputON auto-creates a linked row in Required Section I (bridge, Rule 3); OFF removes it. Carries Product / SKU / type / material / price / annual qty across.—
Packaging — JustifyTextFree textInputTwo-way synced with the linked Required row’s Justification field.Recommended when toggle is ON.
Sealing Intervention Needed?ToggleOn / OffInputFlags that the sealing method needs to change (does not itself create a bridge row; recorded on the same type group).—
Sealing — JustifyTextFree textInputReason for the sealing intervention.—
Total bar (read-only): Products · Packaging Items · Annual Qty · Annual Cost · “Auto-fetches to Existing Unit Pricing → Packaging”.
Tab 3: Required ▼

Two sections. Section I rows are bridge-populated from Existing intervention toggles; Section II rows are added freely. (Stored in Packaging Info + Required Sealing + Monthly Ordered Quantity child tables.)

Section I — Packaging Interventions (bridge-populated)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Product / SKU / Current TypeRead-only text—AutofillMirrored from the originating Existing block + packaging row (italic on cream-deep).—
Current Material / PriceRead-only text—AutofillFrom the matching Existing Section I row (Material · ₹ price/unit).—
Annual Qty (Existing)Read-only number—CalculatedAnnual Total for this variant-type pulled from the Existing monthly grid.—
JustificationTextareaFree textInputTwo-way synced with the Existing Section III packaging Justify.—
Required Packaging Details (Type *, Material, Source, Dimensions, Capacity, Unit, Price/Unit, Supports Printing?, Picture)Same field set as Existing Section IAs per Existing rowInputCaptures the new / replacement packaging spec; Price/Unit drives Saving and the Requirement Tracker grid.“Type of Packaging is required.”
Type of Sealing RequiredMulti-select pillsInduction / Band / Bottleneck / ROPP / Bottle CapInputSealing method(s) for the required packaging (child table Required Sealing).—
Implementation StatusSelectPending / In Progress / Completed / Replace Existing / Not FeasibleInputDrives Summary status badge and aggregate counts.—
Implementation NotesTextareaFree textInput——
Removing a Section I row toggles OFF the corresponding Existing packaging-intervention flag (bridge stays in sync both ways).

Section II — New Packaging (standalone)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Product / PurposeTextFree textInputLabel for a new packaging item not linked to any existing variant.—
SKU / ReferenceTextFree textInput——
Packaging Details + SealingSame field set as Section IAs aboveInputFull spec; its Price/Unit shows directly in Summary New Price.“Type of Packaging is required.”
Implementation StatusSelectPending / In Progress / Completed / On HoldInputNote the New-packaging status set differs from Section I (On Hold instead of Replace Existing / Not Feasible).—
Implementation NotesTextareaFree textInput——
Status Overview total bar: Total Interventions · New Packaging Items · Pending · In Progress · Completed.
Tab 4: Requirement Tracker ▼

Mirrors the Existing monthly grid for the required side — Monthly Quantity Required (months ↓ rows, required packaging types → columns). Columns auto-populate from Required Section I interventions and Section II new packaging.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Order MonthRow labelApr … MarSystemFixed 12-month axis (spans cohort years, Rule 11).—
Quantity Required (per month × required type)Number≥ 0InputOne editable cell per month per required-type column; entries preserved on re-render. Maps to Monthly Ordered Quantity with procurement status = Requirement.Must be ≥ 0.
Month Total (₹)Read-only currency—Calculated= Σ (month qty × required type price/unit).—
Annual Total per Type / Grand TotalRead-only—CalculatedColumn-wise units sum and overall required spend.—
Monthly Overview total bar: Total Interventions · New Packaging Items · “Auto-fetches from Required tab Sections I & II”.
Tab 5: Resources ▼

Reference materials table (Drive Resources HTML / child rows). “+ Add Resource” appends a row.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Resource TitleTextFree textInput——
CategorySelectSupplier Directory / Material Sample / Print Vendor / Quotation / Specification Sheet / Sustainability ReferenceInput——
TypeSelectContact / Reference / Quotation / Specification / Photo · SampleInput——
Description / NotesTextFree textInput——
FileAttach (Upload / View)Any fileInputDrive-linked resource.—
Tab 6: Task Log ▼

14 seeded packaging activities grouped by phase (Existing / Intervention / Required) drive the read-only Progress field. Each row carries an editable Status, Owner, Due Date and Notes.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Read-only number01 … 14SystemSequence of seeded tasks.—
Task NameRead-only text14 seeded (e.g. Existing Packaging Inventory Recorded, Monthly Order Volumes Logged, Packaging Intervention Need Identified, Cost Comparison Completed, Procurement Switched to New Packaging)SystemFixed list.—
PhaseRead-only badgeExisting / Intervention / RequiredSystemColour-coded badge.—
StatusSelectPending / In Progress / Completed / BlockedInputAggregates into the read-only Progress (%) field on the DocType.—
Owner / Due Date / NotesText / Date / TextFreeInput——
Tab 7: Log Book ▼

Free-form activity diary (child table DF Log Book Child). “+ Add Entry” appends a row.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
DateDate—Input——
Product / ActivityTextProduct / supplierInput——
Activity TypeSelectSite Visit / Supplier Meeting / Sample Review / Print Trial / Procurement / Quality CheckInput——
Notes / ObservationsTextFree textInput——
Logged ByTextNameInput——
EvidenceAttach (Upload)Any fileInput——
Key validations (whole form): Entrepreneur is required and unique (one DF Packaging per entrepreneur). Each Existing block must have a Product Variant selected from the dropdown (Product Profile sourced, not auto-prepopulated) and the same variant cannot be picked twice. At least one packaging detail row per variant; Type of Packaging is mandatory on every row (Existing & Required). Quantity Ordered, Capacity and Price Per Unit must be ≥ 0; Order Year is required on monthly entries (the grid aggregates across cohort years, Rule 11). Calculated fields (Month Total, Annual qty/type, Annual Cost, Avg Monthly Qty, Saving) are read-only with formula tooltips (Rule 1). The Annual Cost auto-fetches to Existing Unit Pricing → Packaging — do not edit it there.
Section Review:
PR-SK-001

Digital Literacy

Part of the Skilling (Upskilling) module. It captures the entrepreneur's current digital footprint — device/internet access plus a star-rated proficiency assessment across nine platform skills — flags where training is needed, then carries those flags into a Required tab where target skill levels and implementation status are tracked through delivery, with the Summary rolling up the existing-vs-target skill change and intervention progress.
Entrepreneur-scoped, one record per entrepreneur. The header chip is auto-fetched from the Enterprenur Profile (Entrepreneur is a unique Link; District & Block are hidden fetched fields). Module is fixed to Upskilling and Applicable To is fixed to Enterpreneur (both read-only). The Existing and Required skill grids are backed by the Digital Literacy Entry child table (phase = Existing / Required); flagging a row for intervention in the Existing tab auto-bridges a matching row into the Required tab.

On-Screen UI Structure

A teal title bar reads Digital Literacy with crumb PRIME Rural · Capacity Building. Below it sits a horizontal tab rail — Summary · Existing · Required · Resources · Task Log · Log Book — the active tab highlighted teal. An entrepreneur chip (avatar + name, auto-fetched from profile) sits above every tab body.

The Existing tab has two Roman-numbered sections: I Access & Devices (a checkbox group — Smartphone, Laptop/Computer, Internet) and II Skill Assessment, a special skills grid with one row per platform skill. Each grid row carries a 5-star proficiency rating widget (click to set, × to clear), an Intervention Required? toggle, and a Justification text input. A 5-cell legend above the grid maps stars to proficiency labels. Below the grid an average strip (teal gradient) shows Skills Rated, Average Skill Level and an average-stars readout; a teal total bar repeats the totals and notes the auto-fetch to the Required tab.

The Required tab mirrors this: I Access & Device Needs (three device cards — Smartphone, Laptop/Computer, Internet — each with a Needed checkbox + a status dropdown) and II Target Skill Assessment, the same skills grid but with a Target Skill Level star rating, an Implementation Status dropdown and a Learnings/Notes input. Rows bridged from Existing arrive pre-populated with a ↻ Bridged pill and a platform locked read-only. A status-overview total bar tallies Pending / In Progress / Completed.

The Summary tab is fully read-only: a skill-wise overview table (Existing Level · Intervention Flagged · Target Level · Skill Change · Implementation Status), four aggregate cards (Existing / Required / Skill Change / Implementation Status) and a four-tile intervention-flags strip. Resources is a tile list of training guides; Task Log is a fixed activity checklist; Log Book is an add-row table of training/visit entries.

Calculated fields (Skills Rated, Average / Target Average Skill Level, average-stars, all Summary counts & Skill Change) render italic on cream-deep background, are never editable, and carry a formula tooltip (Rule 1). The entrepreneur chip, District and Block are fetched and read-only with a source note (Rule 2).
Summary
→
Existing
→
Required
→

→

User Journey

Open Digital Literacy
entrepreneur auto-fetched from profile
→
Tick current access
Existing · smartphone / laptop / internet
→
Rate each platform skill
5-star proficiency per row; flag gaps
→
Flagged rows bridge to Required
set target level + needed devices
→
Deliver training & update status
Pending → In Progress → Completed; Log Book
→
Review Summary
skill change & intervention roll-up
Tab 1: Summary ▼

Entirely read-only. Auto-populated from the Existing and Required tabs and recalculated on every switch back to Summary and on any change in those tabs.

Skill-wise Overview Table

One row per platform skill. Existing columns from the Existing grid; Target / Skill Change columns (sea-shaded); Implementation Status (green-shaded).

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Platform / SkillRead-only textE-mail / WhatsApp / Instagram / MS-Office / Calls / UPI Account / UPI QR Code Creation / Printing UPI QR Code / Operating Mobile CameraAutofillOne row per rated skill carried from the Existing grid (and any matched Required row).— (display only)
Existing LevelRead-only stars / label—CalculatedThe Existing-tab star rating for that skill (1–5 → No knowledge … Proficient); “—” if unrated.—
Intervention FlaggedRead-only badgeYes / NoCalculatedReflects the Existing-tab Intervention Required? toggle for that skill.—
Target Level [shaded]Read-only stars / label—CalculatedThe Required-tab target star rating for the matched skill; “—” if not set.—
Skill Change [shaded]Read-only (green / red)Improved / Decreased / No Change / —Calculated= Target Level − Existing Level. > 0 → ▲ Improved (green); < 0 → ▼ Decreased (red); 0 → No Change; “—” if either level missing.—
Implementation Status [shaded]Read-only badgePending / In Progress / Completed / On Hold / Not FeasibleCalculatedMirrors the matched Required row's Implementation Status dropdown.—

Aggregate Cards (4)

Card / MetricField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Existing → Devices AvailableRead-only fractionn / 2CalculatedCount of ticked Existing access items (Smartphone, Laptop, Internet) — shown as “n / 2” per mockup.—
Existing → Skills RatedRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Existing grid rows with a star rating > 0.—
Existing → Avg Skill LevelRead-only number—Calculated= ∑ star values ÷ Skills Rated (2 decimals); “—” if none rated.—
Existing → Interventions FlaggedRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Existing rows with the Intervention toggle on.—
Required → Devices NeededRead-only fractionn / 2CalculatedCount of ticked Needed device cards.—
Required → Target Skills SetRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Required rows with a target star rating > 0.—
Required → Target Avg LevelRead-only number—Calculated= ∑ target stars ÷ Target Skills Set; “—” if none.—
Required → Bridged from ExistingRead-only number—CalculatedCount of Required rows that originated from an Existing intervention flag (Bridged pill).—
Skill Change → Improved / Decreased / No ChangeRead-only numbers—CalculatedPer-skill counts where Target − Existing is > 0 / < 0 / = 0.—
Skill Change → Device GapRead-only number (amber)—CalculatedCount of devices marked Needed in Required but not available in Existing.—
Implementation Status → Completed / In Progress / Pending / Not FeasibleRead-only numbers—CalculatedCount of Required rows by Implementation Status value.—
Footer: a four-tile Intervention Flags strip — Intervention Required (flag count), Trainings Completed (rows with status Completed), Skills Improved (positive Skill Change), and Device Gap (needed-but-unavailable devices).
Tab 2: Existing — Access & Skill Assessment ▼

The working baseline tab. Captures what the entrepreneur currently has and rates their current proficiency on each platform skill. Flagging a row auto-creates a matching entry in the Required tab.

Section I — Access & Devices

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Which access does the entrepreneur currently have?Checkbox group (multi-select)Smartphone / Laptop · Computer / InternetInputTick all that apply. Backed by Has Smartphone and Has Internet one-time access checks; drives the Summary “Devices Available” aggregate and the Device Gap flag.— (optional; none ticked = 0 available)

Skill Proficiency Legend (5 levels)

The star scale shown above both skill grids. Each star step maps to a proficiency label.

StarsProficiency LabelNumeric Value
★☆☆☆☆No existing knowledge1
★★☆☆☆Beginner2
★★★☆☆Basic3
★★★★☆Intermediate4
★★★★★Proficient5
The underlying Digital Literacy Entry doctype stores the level as a label list — Existing Skill Level: Beginner / Intermediate / Savy / No existing knowledge; the UI presents it as the 5-star scale above. Required (after-intervention) level options are Beginner / Intermediate / Savy / No Progress.

Section II — Skill Assessment (skills grid — one row per platform skill)

Nine platform skills available: E-mail, WhatsApp, Instagram, MS-Office, Calls, UPI Account, UPI QR Code Creation, Printing UPI QR Code, Operating Mobile Camera. Add a row per skill assessed.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
#Read-only sequence—CalculatedAuto row index; renumbered on delete.—
Platform / SkillSelectE-mail / WhatsApp / Instagram / MS-Office / Calls / UPI Account / UPI QR Code Creation / Printing UPI QR Code / Operating Mobile CameraInputSingle platform per row; drawn from the 9-item platform master. A row counts only once a platform is chosen.Avoid duplicate platforms across rows; unselected rows are skipped in roll-ups.
Existing Skill Level5-star rating widget1–5 stars (see legend)InputClick a star to set proficiency (1 = No existing knowledge … 5 = Proficient); × clears to 0. Feeds Skills Rated & Average.0 (unrated) excluded from the average.
Intervention Required?Toggle (on/off)On / OffInputTurning it on shows “↑ Flagged → Required Tab” and auto-creates a matching (bridged) row in the Required tab pre-filled with this Platform & Justification. Turning it off removes that bridged row.—
JustificationText—InputReason / context (e.g. “Training need, gap noted, equipment unavailable”). Live-syncs into the bridged Required row's Learnings/Notes.— (recommended when flagged)
Delete rowAction button—InputRemoves the row; also removes its bridged Required row if one exists; renumbers and recalculates.—
Average strip (calculated, read-only): Skills Rated = count of rows with stars > 0; Average Skill Level = ∑ stars ÷ Skills Rated (2 dp); Average stars = the rounded average rendered as filled/empty stars. The teal total bar repeats Skills Rated, Average Level and Interventions Flagged, and notes “Auto-fetches to: Required Tab → Target Profile”.
Tab 3: Required — Target Skills & Implementation ▼

Sets target skill levels and tracks implementation. Rows flagged in Existing arrive pre-populated as ↻ Bridged rows (platform locked); new target rows can also be added manually.

Section I — Access & Device Needs (three device cards)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Smartphone — NeededCheckboxOn / OffInputTick if a smartphone must be provided/arranged. Compared against Existing access for the Device Gap flag.—
Smartphone — StatusSelectPending / In Progress / Completed / Not FeasibleInputProvisioning status for this device.—
Laptop / Computer — NeededCheckboxOn / OffInputAs above for laptop/computer.—
Laptop / Computer — StatusSelectPending / In Progress / Completed / Not FeasibleInputProvisioning status.—
Internet — NeededCheckboxOn / OffInputAs above for internet connectivity.—
Internet — StatusSelectPending / In Progress / Completed / Not FeasibleInputProvisioning status.—

Section II — Target Skill Assessment (skills grid)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
#Read-only sequence—CalculatedAuto row index; renumbered on delete.—
Platform / SkillSelect (locked when bridged)Same 9-item platform masterInput / AutofillFor bridged rows the platform is sourced from the Existing row and shown read-only with a ↻ Bridged pill; manual rows let the user pick.Required for the row to count.
Target Skill Level5-star rating widget1–5 stars (see legend)InputDesired proficiency after intervention. Feeds Target Skills Set, Target Average and the Summary Skill Change.0 (unset) excluded from the target average.
Implementation StatusSelectPending / In Progress / Completed / On Hold / Not FeasibleInputDelivery state of the training/intervention for this skill; drives the status totals and Summary roll-up.Defaults to Pending.
Learnings / NotesText—InputKey takeaways / training notes. For bridged rows it pre-fills from the Existing Justification and stays in sync.—
Delete rowAction button—InputRemoves the row; if it was bridged, also clears the originating Existing intervention toggle; renumbers and recalculates.—
Average / status strip (calculated, read-only): Skills Set = rows with target stars > 0; Target Average Level = ∑ target stars ÷ Skills Set (2 dp); average rendered as stars. The status-overview total bar tallies Skills Set, Target Average, and Pending / In Progress / Completed counts.
Bridge behaviour. An Existing row's Intervention toggle and its bridged Required row are two-way linked: removing the Required row clears the Existing flag, and changing the Existing platform/justification flows into the bridged row. The bridged platform select is disabled (“Locked — sourced from Existing”).

Reference materials & training guides — a tile list of step-by-step how-to guides. Backed by a Drive resources HTML widget (link name + drive link).

Tab 5: Task Log ▼

A fixed activity checklist for Digital Literacy delivery, phased Existing / Required. Backed by the DF Log Book Child tasks table and a hidden Progress float.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Read-only sequence—CalculatedFixed task ordering.—
Task NameRead-only label10 fixed tasks (see below)AutofillSeeded task list, not free-text.—

Required: Target Skill Levels Set; WhatsApp / Social Media Training Delivered; UPI / Digital Payment Setup Done; Smartphone Photography Training Done; Email & Basic Office Skills Training Done; Follow-up Assessment Completed; Skills Verified & Signed Off.—
StatusSelectPending / In Progress / CompletedInputPer-task status; rolls into the hidden Progress %.—
OwnerText / Link—InputField officer responsible.—
Due DateDate—InputTarget completion date.—
NotesText—InputFree-text remarks.—
Tab 6: Log Book ▼

An add-row table to log every training session, hand-holding visit, or device set-up conducted. Backed by the Direct Log Book Child table.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
DateDate—InputDate of the activity.—
Platform / SkillTexte.g. WhatsApp, UPIInputWhich skill the session addressed.—
Activity TypeSelectTraining Session / Device Setup / Hand-holding Visit / Assessment / Follow-up / DemoInputNature of the engagement.— (select required)
Outcome / NotesText—InputWhat was done, what changed.—
Done ByTextField officer nameInputWho conducted the activity.—
EvidenceAttach / Upload—InputOptional supporting file (photo, attendance).—
Delete rowAction button—InputRemoves the entry.—
Key validations (whole form): Entrepreneur is a unique Link — one Digital Literacy record per entrepreneur; District & Block are fetched read-only; Module (Upskilling) and Applicable To (Enterpreneur) are read-only. In the skill grids a row counts only once a Platform / Skill is selected; a star rating of 0 (unrated) is excluded from Skills Rated and the average. Turning on an Existing row's Intervention toggle auto-bridges a Required row (platform locked, justification synced); deleting either side keeps the two in sync. Devices marked Needed in Required but absent in Existing surface as a Device Gap. Required Implementation Status defaults to Pending and drives the status totals. All Summary figures are read-only and recalculated on every change; numeric roll-ups use Indian number formatting.
Section Review:
PR-SK-002

Capacity Building

Skilling module of the Diagnostic Framework: records every Past Training the entrepreneur has attended, evaluates its retention / application, and — where a skill has not stuck — flags it for re-training; standalone new skill gaps are captured as New Required Trainings. Soft vs Technical skill areas are tracked separately, each intervention carries a Gap Severity and a six-dimension star-rated Post-Training Assessment scorecard, and a read-only Summary rolls up trainings, gaps and intervention flags.
Key behaviour — the Past → Required re-training bridge. A training is recorded ONCE in the Past Trainings tab. Toggling its “Re-training needed for this skill?” flag captures no cost — it only raises a flag and writes a justification. The flagged training then auto-appears in Required Trainings → Section I (Re-training Interventions), pre-filled (read-only) with the Past Training name, Skill Area and the justification text, where the Fessociate adds Gap Severity, current/required levels, intervention type and an implementation plan. Standalone skill gaps not linked to any past record are added separately in Section II (New Required Trainings). Untoggling the flag (or removing the bridged row) removes it from Section I. Both intervention types share an identical six-dimension Post-Training Assessment scorecard.

On-Screen UI Structure

Top bar: teal header “Capacity Building Module” with crumb “PRIME Rural · DF Capacity Building”, plus an entrepreneur chip (avatar + name) auto-fetched from the profile.
Tab rail: horizontal pill bar with six tabs — Summary · Past Trainings · Required Trainings · Tasks · Resources · Log Book; active tab renders teal-on-white.
Body layout: each tab is a panel. Summary is read-only: a Training-wise Overview table (one row per training), four Aggregate cards (Past Trainings / Required Trainings / Capacity Picture / Status) and a four-cell Intervention Flag strip. Past Trainings renders each training as a collapsible “training block” (sea-blue header with name input + skill-type badge + Remove) holding three sub-sections (Training Details, Skill Retention & Application, Remarks) and a terracotta Intervention Need flag panel. Required Trainings has two sections of repeating rows — Section I Re-training (auto-populated from flagged Past Trainings) and Section II New Required Trainings — each row carrying Skill Type / Skill Area / Gap Severity / levels / intervention + an Implementation Status block + the Post-Training scorecard.
Special widgets: (1) Skill-area multi-select — a grouped listbox split into “— Soft Skills —” and “— Technical Skills —” optgroups that filters to show only the chosen Skill Type’s options. (2) Re-training flag toggle with a live note (“Not flagged” → “↻ Flagged → Details in Required Tab”) and a justification box that bridges to Section I. (3) Six-dimension Post-Training Assessment scorecard — six 1–5 star ratings (Skill / Confidence / Production Improvement, Working Independently, Ability to Train Others, Further Support Needed) over a 1–5 legend (None → Excellent), plus six narrative text fields. (4) Total bars at the foot of Past and Required summarising counts and the Auto-fetch target.
Per Rule 1, every calculated / rolled-up field (all Summary table cells, the four Aggregate cards, the Intervention Flag counts, and both Total bars) renders italic on a cream-deep background and is non-editable, with a tooltip stating its source.
