User Journey

Open
Diagnostics → Skilling → Capacity Building (entrepreneur auto-fetched)
→
Record Past
Add each training attended; skill type/area, completion, retention & application, remarks
→
Flag Re-training
Toggle “Re-training needed?” with a justification on trainings that did not stick
→
Detail Gaps
Required tab → Section I auto-fills flagged trainings; Section II add new gaps; set severity, levels, intervention
→
Assess Outcome
After delivery, rate the six-dimension scorecard and capture learning outcomes & feedback
→
Log & Save
Record activities in Tasks & Log Book; review Summary roll-up; save
Tab 1 — Summary (read-only roll-up)

Entirely auto-populated from the Past Trainings and Required Trainings tabs — no data entry on this tab. Four blocks: (1.1) a Training-wise Overview table (one row per training; re-training rows highlighted, new-training rows tinted green), (1.2) four Aggregate cards, (1.3) a four-cell Intervention Flag strip.

1.1 Training-wise Overview table (one row per training, auto-built)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Training NameRead-only text—AutofillBold training name from each Past block and each Required row (re-training name bridges from its Past Training). Empty state: “Auto-populated from Past Trainings and Required Trainings tabs”.—
Skill TypeRead-only badgeSoft Skill / Technical SkillAutofillFrom the block’s Skill Type. Badge: Soft = purple, Technical = blue; re-training & new rows show “← from Past Trainings” / “New Required Training” in this column span.—
Skill AreaRead-only text—AutofillComma-joined selected skill areas of the Past block.—
Intervention TypeRead-only badge↻ Re-training / + New TrainingAutofillRe-training rows show a terracotta “↻ Re-training” badge; new rows a blue “+ New Training” badge; Past rows show “—”.—
Gap SeverityRead-only badgeCritical / High / Medium / LowAutofillFrom the Required row’s Gap Severity. Badge: Critical = red, High = amber, Medium = highlight, Low = neutral; “—” if unset or for Past rows.—
Implementation StatusRead-only badgeIdentified / Planned / Scheduled / Ongoing / Completed / Follow-up PendingAutofillFrom the Required row’s Implementation Status. Badge: Completed = green, Planned/Scheduled = blue, else neutral; “—” if unset.—

1.2 Aggregate cards (four, all read-only / calculated)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Past Trainings · Total RecordedRead-only count—CalculatedCount of Past training blocks.—
Past Trainings · Soft SkillRead-only count—CalculatedPast blocks with Skill Type = Soft Skill.—
Past Trainings · Technical SkillRead-only count—CalculatedPast blocks with Skill Type = Technical Skill.—
Past Trainings · Fully CompletedRead-only count (green)—CalculatedPast blocks with Completion Status = Completed.—
Past Trainings · Re-training FlaggedRead-only count (amber)—CalculatedPast blocks whose Re-training toggle is On.—
Required Trainings · Total IdentifiedRead-only count—CalculatedSection I re-training rows + Section II new rows.—
Required Trainings · Re-trainingRead-only count—CalculatedCount of Section I rows.—
Required Trainings · New TrainingsRead-only count—CalculatedCount of Section II rows.—
Required Trainings · Critical / High GapRead-only count (red)—CalculatedRequired rows with Gap Severity = Critical or High.—
Required Trainings · CompletedRead-only count (green)—CalculatedRequired rows with Implementation Status = Completed.—
Capacity Picture · Total TrainingsRead-only count—CalculatedPast total + Required total.—
Capacity Picture · Skill Areas CoveredRead-only count—CalculatedDistinct skill areas across Past + Required rows.—
Capacity Picture · Gaps RemainingRead-only count (amber)—Calculatedmax(0, Required total − Required Completed).—
Capacity Picture · Interventions CompletedRead-only count (green)—Calculated= Required rows with status Completed.—
Status · CompletedRead-only count (green)—CalculatedRequired rows with status = Completed.—
Status · Planned / ScheduledRead-only count—CalculatedRequired rows with status = Planned or Scheduled.—
Status · IdentifiedRead-only count—CalculatedRequired rows with status = Identified.—
Status · PendingRead-only count (amber)—CalculatedRequired rows with status unset (“— Select —”).—

1.3 Intervention Flag strip (four count cards)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Re-training NeededRead-only count card (amber ↻)—Calculated= Past blocks with Re-training toggle On.—
Critical GapRead-only count card (red !)—Calculated= Required rows with Gap Severity Critical or High.—
New Training AddedRead-only count card (blue +)—Calculated= count of Section II new training rows.—
Interventions CompletedRead-only count card (green ✓)—Calculated= Required rows with status = Completed.—
Tab 2 — Past Trainings (trainings already attended; child table of training blocks)

Repeating child table: each row is a collapsible “training block” added via + Add Past Training. Block header carries the training name (synced with the name field below), a skill-type badge and a Remove button (confirm prompt). Each block has three sub-sections plus a terracotta Intervention Need panel, with a foot Total bar across all blocks. Below are all fields of one training row.

2.1 Past Trainings — I. Training Details

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Training NameTextFree textInputSyncs both ways with the block header name input. Source for the re-training bridge name in Required → Section I.—
Skill Type *Select— Select — / Soft Skill / Technical SkillInputMandatory. Drives the header badge (Soft = purple, Technical = blue) and filters the Skill Area listbox to the matching optgroup. Feeds Summary Soft / Technical counts.Required: “Select the skill type.”
Skill Area *Multi-select listbox (grouped)Soft: Communication / Leadership / Customer Interaction / Confidence Building / Financial Discipline / Team Coordination / Entrepreneurial Mindset / Decision Making  ·  Technical: Product Making / Recipe Standardisation / Product Consistency / Food Preparation / Craft Technique / Finishing Technique / Production Workflow / Product Innovation / Material Handling / Quality ManagementInputMandatory. Hold Ctrl/Cmd to select multiple. Options shown depend on Skill Type (soft optgroup for Soft, technical optgroup for Technical, both if none chosen). Comma-joined selection surfaces in the Summary Skill Area column.Required: “Select at least one skill area.”
Provider / TrainerTextFree textInputProvider or trainer name who delivered the training.—
Training FormatSelect— Select — / Classroom / Workshop / On-site Demonstration / Exposure Visit / One-on-one Mentoring / Group Training / Online / Self-pacedInputMode in which the training was delivered.—
LocationTextFree textInputVenue or location of the training.—
Start DateDateCalendar dateInputWhen the training began.—
End DateDateCalendar dateInputWhen the training ended.End Date should be ≥ Start Date: “End date cannot be before start date.”
Completion Status *Select— Select — / Completed / Partial / Ongoing / DroppedInputMandatory. “Completed” counts toward Summary & Total bar “Fully Completed”.Required: “Select the completion status.”

2.2 Past Trainings — II. Skill Retention & Application (the “did it stick?” assessment)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Skill RetentionSelect— Select — / Cannot Apply / Applies with Support / Independent / Can Train OthersInputHow well the entrepreneur retains the skill — the primary signal for whether re-training is needed (“Cannot Apply” / “Applies with Support” typically warrant the flag).—
Applied in Enterprise?Select— Select — / Yes / Partial / NoInputWhether the skill is actually being used in the enterprise.—
Behaviour Change Observed?Select— Select — / Yes — Significant / Yes — Minor / No Change / Too Early to TellInputObserved behavioural change since the training.—
Improvement SeenSelect— Select — / Confidence Improved / Product Consistency Improved / Production Speed Improved / Customer Interaction Improved / Entrepreneurial Behaviour Improved / Multiple ImprovementsInputNature of the improvement attributable to the training.—
Time Since TrainingSelect— Select — / Less than 1 month / 1–3 months / 3–6 months / More than 6 monthsInputElapsed time since the training, contextualising retention.—

2.3 Past Trainings — III. Remarks

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Entrepreneur FeedbackTextareaFree textInputEntrepreneur’s own feedback on this past training.—
Trainer FeedbackTextareaFree textInputTrainer’s remarks.—
Field Team ObservationTextareaFree textInputWhat the field team observed.—

2.4 Skill Gaps — Intervention Need (terracotta flag panel; raises a flag, captures no cost)

Re-training conditional logic. The Re-training toggle is the single trigger for the Past → Required bridge. When turned On, the note flips from “Not flagged” to “↻ Flagged → Details in Required Tab”, and a bridged row is created in Required Trainings → Section I pre-filled (read-only) with this training’s Name, Skill Area and the Justification text typed below; the Justification box syncs live into that row. Turning the toggle Off (or removing the bridged Section I row) clears the flag and removes the row. The companion cell carries a static note explaining this routing.
Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Re-training needed for this skill?Toggle (on/off)Off (default) / OnInputWhen On, flags the training for re-training and creates the bridged Section I row; note becomes “↻ Flagged → Details in Required Tab”. Counts toward Summary & Total bar “Re-training Flagged”.—
Justification (Why is re-training needed?)TextareaFree textInputReason for re-training; syncs live into the bridged Section I row’s “Justification (from Past Trainings)” field.Recommended when the toggle is On: “Add a justification for re-training.”
Routing noteRead-only label (static)—StaticStates “When flagged, this training appears in Required Trainings → Section I pre-filled with skill area and justification.”—

2.5 Past Trainings — Total bar (foot, read-only)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
TotalRead-only count—CalculatedNumber of Past training blocks.—
Soft SkillRead-only count—CalculatedPast blocks with Skill Type = Soft Skill.—
Product TechnicalRead-only count—CalculatedPast blocks with Skill Type = Technical Skill.—
Fully CompletedRead-only count—CalculatedPast blocks with Completion Status = Completed.—
Intervention FlaggedRead-only count (amber)—CalculatedPast blocks with the Re-training toggle On.—
Tab 3 — Required Trainings (Section I: Re-training Interventions · Section II: New Required Trainings)
Bridge from Past Trainings (Rule 3). Section I rows are not entered from scratch — they are auto-populated when a Past Training’s Re-training flag is toggled On, arriving pre-filled (read-only) with the Past Training name, Skill Area and Justification. Empty state: “No re-training interventions flagged yet — toggle the flag in Past Trainings to populate this section.” A manual + Add Re-training Row is also available. Section II New Required Trainings are standalone skill gaps not linked to any past record, added via + Add New Required Training. Both row types carry an Implementation Status block and the identical six-dimension Post-Training Assessment scorecard.

3.1 Section I — Re-training Interventions: bridged read-only header

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Past TrainingRead-only text (autofill, cream-deep)—AutofillBridged from the flagged Past Training’s name; “—” if not yet named.—
Skill AreaRead-only text (autofill, cream-deep)—AutofillBridged from the flagged Past Training’s Skill Area.—
Justification (from Past Trainings)TextareaFree textAutofill (editable)Pre-filled from the Past Training justification; stays in sync with the source field.—
Intervention TypeRead-only badge↻ Re-trainingAutofillFixed terracotta “↻ Re-training” badge marking the row origin.—

3.2 Section I & II — Intervention Details (identical body for both row types)

Section II rows add a free-text Training Name and a Skill Type selector in the header (a blue “+ New Training” badge) instead of the bridged read-only header above; the detail grid below is identical for both.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Training Name (Section II only)TextFree textInputName of the new required training; surfaces in the Summary overview row.—
Skill TypeSelect— Select — / Soft Skill / Technical SkillInputFilters the Skill Area listbox to the matching optgroup (soft vs technical skill gap).—
Skill AreaMulti-select listbox (grouped)Soft & Technical skill areas (same lists as Past Trainings 2.1)InputHold Ctrl/Cmd to select multiple; shown options follow the chosen Skill Type. Contributes to the distinct Skill Areas Covered count.—
Gap SeveritySelect— Select — / Critical / High / Medium / LowInputSeverity of the skill gap. Critical/High feed the Summary “Critical / High Gap” count and the red “Critical Gap” flag card.—
Current LevelSelect— Select — / No Exposure / Beginner / Intermediate / AdvancedInputEntrepreneur’s present skill level.—
Required LevelSelect— Select — / No Exposure / Beginner / Intermediate / AdvancedInputTarget skill level after the intervention.Required Level should be ≥ Current Level: “Required level should not be below current level.”
Problem ObservedTextareaFree textInputSpecific problem — e.g. inconsistent output, difficulty repeating recipe, low confidence while selling.—
Intervention TypeSelect— Select — / Training / Demonstration / Mentoring / Exposure Visit / Apprenticeship / RefresherInputHow the gap will be addressed.—
Suggested Provider / ResourceTextFree textInputWho can deliver this training.—
Assigned ToSelect— Select — / Fellow / DC / Core Team / External TrainerInputResponsible party for delivering the intervention.—
Expected OutcomeTextareaFree textInputTarget result — e.g. entrepreneur independently prepares product, improved consistency / communication confidence.—

3.3 Implementation Status (terracotta block; per Required row)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
StatusSelect— Select — / Identified / Planned / Scheduled / Ongoing / Completed / Follow-up PendingInputDrives the Summary Status card breakdown, the “Completed” aggregate and the “Interventions Completed” flag card; unset rows count as Pending.—
Scheduled DateDateCalendar dateInputWhen the intervention is scheduled.—
Implementation NotesTextareaFree textInputNotes on scheduling, logistics or dependencies.—

3.4 Learning Outcomes — Post-Training Assessment scorecard (six dimensions; per Required row, shared by both row types)

A six-dimension star scorecard rated after the intervention, over a 1–5 legend: 1 None · 2 Minimal · 3 Moderate · 4 Good · 5 Excellent. Each dimension is a 1–5 star control, followed by six narrative text fields.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Skill ImprovementStar rating1–5 stars (None → Excellent)InputDimension 1 — improvement in the targeted skill after the intervention.—
Confidence ImprovementStar rating1–5 starsInputDimension 2 — change in the entrepreneur’s confidence.—
Production ImprovementStar rating1–5 starsInputDimension 3 — effect on production / output.—
Working IndependentlyStar rating1–5 starsInputDimension 4 — ability to apply the skill without support.—
Ability to Train OthersStar rating1–5 starsInputDimension 5 — capacity to pass the skill on (e.g. to SHG members).—
Further Support NeededStar rating1–5 starsInputDimension 6 — extent of additional support still required.—
Entrepreneur FeedbackTextareaFree textInputEntrepreneur’s own feedback after the training.—
Trainer / Facilitator FeedbackTextareaFree textInputTrainer’s observations and remarks.—
Field Team ObservationTextareaFree textInputWhat the field team observed post-training.—
Learning OutcomeTextareaFree textInputKey skills or knowledge actually gained.—
Follow-up ActionsTextareaFree textInputNext steps or follow-up required.—
Assessment DateDateCalendar dateInputDate the post-training assessment was conducted.—

3.5 Required Trainings — Total bar (foot, read-only)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Re-trainingRead-only count—CalculatedCount of Section I rows.—
New TrainingsRead-only count—CalculatedCount of Section II rows.—
Critical / High GapRead-only count—CalculatedSection I + II rows with Gap Severity = Critical or High.—
CompletedRead-only count—CalculatedRequired rows with Implementation Status = Completed.—
Auto-fetches toRead-only label (static)—StaticStates “Details Scorecard” — the Post-Training Assessment scorecard for each Required row.—
Tab 4 — Tasks (capacity-building activity log child table)

Repeating child table added via + Add Row; six seed tasks load by default (Awareness & orientation on capacity building → Applied for scheme/training programme → Co-ordination with training provider → Received Registration/Confirmation → Conducted training/attended programme → Follow up on learning outcomes). Row No. auto-numbers (01, 02, …). Per row:

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Read-only auto-number—CalculatedSequential index, zero-padded.—
Task NameTextFree text (seeded with the six standard tasks)InputThe capacity-building activity.—
Actual StartDateCalendar dateInputWhen the task started.—
Completion DateDateCalendar dateInputWhen the task completed.Completion Date should be ≥ Actual Start.
StatusSelectPending / In Progress / Completed / BlockedInputCurrent state of the task.—
DescriptionTextFree textInputTask description.—
CommentTextFree textInputFree remark.—
Tab 5 — Resources (reference materials child table)

Repeating child table added via + Add Resource; at least one row must remain (“At least one row is required.”). Per row:

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Resource TitleTextFree textInputName of the reference material.—
CategorySelect— Select — / Training Material / Curriculum / Assessment Tool / Certificate / Reference GuideInputClassifies the resource.—
TypeSelect— Select — / Document / Video / Link / ContactInputResource format / nature.—
NotesTextFree textInputNotes about the resource.—
FileAttach (Upload)FileInputUpload a supporting file.—
Tab 6 — Log Book (field-visit task logs child table)

Repeating child table added via + Add Row; one seed row on load (“Add your task logs here”). Row No. auto-numbers (01, 02, …). Per row:

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Read-only auto-number—CalculatedSequential index, zero-padded.—
FE AssociateTextFree textInputField associate who made the log entry.—
Log DateDateCalendar dateInputDate of the log entry.—
Entrepreneur NameTextFree text (defaults from profile)Input / AutofillEntrepreneur the log relates to.—
Diagnostic FrameworkTextFree text (e.g. “DF Capacity Building”)InputFramework context for the log.—
Task NameTextFree textInputTask referenced in the log.—
PhotoAttach (Upload)File / imageInputOptional field photo.—
Key validations (whole form): On every Past Training block, Skill Type, Skill Area and Completion Status are mandatory (Skill Area requires at least one selection); End Date must not precede Start Date. The Skill Area listbox must match the chosen Skill Type — selecting Soft Skill clears any technical selections and vice-versa. Toggling “Re-training needed?” On should be accompanied by a Justification and creates exactly one bridged Section I row; turning it Off (or removing that row) clears the flag — the two stay consistent. In Required rows, Required Level should not fall below Current Level. Status / Gap Severity drive every Summary count (Pending = unset status; Critical + High = the red Critical-Gap flag; Completed = the green Interventions-Completed flag). Resources require at least one row. All Summary cells, the four Aggregate cards, the Intervention Flag strip and both Total bars are read-only roll-ups — never edited directly.
Section Review:
PR-SK-003

Business Plan Canvas

Entrepreneur-level framework. Structured business planning tool based on the Business Model Canvas methodology.
Pending: HTML mockup to be added by user. Form spec will be updated once the HTML is available. Current spec is derived from the repo doctype definition (DF Business Plan Canvas).
Expected Structure (from repo doctype) ▼

Based on the DF Business Plan Canvas doctype in the repo. Full field spec to be updated when HTML is provided.

The Business Model Canvas typically covers 9 building blocks: Key Partners, Key Activities, Key Resources, Value Propositions, Customer Relationships, Channels, Customer Segments, Cost Structure, Revenue Streams. The form will follow the standard BMC layout with Existing/Required tabs.
Section Review:
PR-LG-001

Logistic Packing

Product-level framework. Track outer/transit packaging for shipping products. Distinct from Product Packaging (which covers primary product packaging).

Tab Structure: Summary | Existing | Required | Resources | Log Book

Existing Tab — Per-Product Variant Packing ▼

ProductSelectProduct MasterCard header filterRequired
Outer Box TypeSelectCorrugated Box, Kraft Box, Thermocol Box, Wooden Crate, Jute Bag, OtherInput--
Cushioning MaterialSelectBubble Wrap, Foam, Paper Shred, Thermocol, Air Pillows, NoneInput--

Dimensions (L x B x H)3x Float + unitmm/cmInput--
Cost per Shipment (Rs.)CurrencyNumericInput≥ 0
SupplierDataFree textInput--
Intervention NeedToggle + TextareaOn/Off + JustificationBridges to Required tabJustification if ON

Packing material child table: Material Type, Quantity, Cost (Rs.), Supplier per material row.

Required Tab ▼

Target packaging specifications with same field structure + Implementation Status (Pending/In Progress/Completed/Not Feasible) + Pre/Post Intervention Comments.

Section Review:
PR-LG-002

Logistic Service

Entrepreneur-level framework. Track shipping/logistics service providers and delivery capability.

Tab Structure: Summary | Existing | Required | Resources | Log Book

Existing Tab — Connectivity + Route-Based Tracking ▼

Section I: Connectivity Assessment

FieldTypeValues
Road ConnectivitySelectPucca Road (Tarred), Kutcha Road (Gravel/Dirt), Mixed, No Road Access
Nearest Highway / Main RoadDataFree text
Nearest Rail StationDataFree text
Nearest Courier Hub / Post OfficeDataFree text
Last Mile AccessSelectAny vehicle, Small vehicle only, Two-wheeler/foot only, Seasonal only
Main Logistics IssueTextareaFree text
Seasonal DisruptionTextareaFree text

Section II: Available Services (Checkbox Groups)

4 groups: Courier/Post (India Post, Blue Dart, DTDC, Delhivery, Shiprocket), Surface Transport (Own Vehicle, Hired Cab, Auto/Tempo, Mini Truck, Bus Parcel, Train Parcel), E-commerce Fulfillment (Amazon, Flipkart, Meesho, Ecomm Express, Xpressbees), Community/Govt (FPO Van, SHG Network, Cold Chain, NGO Partner, Govt Scheme).

Section III: Route Blocks (Repeater)

FieldTypeValues / Logic
Route NameDataCard header. Required.
Route TypeSelectDirect, Via Hub/Market, Multi-stop
DirectionSelectInbound, Outbound, Both

Leg Table (child table within each route): From, To, Mode of Transport (16 options), Time of Travel, Cost (Rs.), Contact.

CalculatedFormula
Trip CostSum of all leg costs
FrequencySelect: Daily (x30), Twice/week (x12), Weekly (x4), Twice/month, Monthly, Every 2 months
Monthly Cost= Trip Cost x Frequency multiplier
Delay FrequencySelect: Never, Sometimes, Often, Always

Intervention toggle per route: "Needs Service Upgrade?" + Justification. Bridges to Required tab.

Section Review:
PR-INF-001

Infrastructure

A single Infrastructure framework with three intervention tabs — Built Environment (BE), Energy Efficient Machinery (EE) and Clean Energy (CE) — plus Summary, Financial Model, Tracker, Task Log and Resources. The screen opens on a dashboard-style Summary, then drills into the per-tab forms. The SELCO Executive works inside the same framework but edits only SELCO-relevant fields (pricing, equipment status, disbursement); funding is split across SELCO / MBMA / LIFCOM / USER with a LIFCOM EMI option.
Key behaviour: Infrastructure starts from a dashboard overview, then drills into forms. SELCO Executive sees all tabs but edits only SELCO-relevant fields (pricing, equipment status, disbursement). Required machines arrive from the Machinery framework via dropdown selection.

On-Screen UI Structure

Top bar: forest-green header reading “Infrastructure” with a breadcrumb “PRIME Rural · Project Setup”, and an entrepreneur chip (avatar + name) auto-fetched from the EP profile.
Tab rail: a horizontal pill rail — Summary · Built Environment · Energy Efficient Machinery (Machines) · Clean Energy (Solar) · Financial Model · Tracker · Task Log · Resources. Only one panel is visible at a time; switching to Summary / Financial Model / Tracker triggers a live recompute.
Body layout: each tab is a stack of numbered Roman-numeral section blocks (I, II, III…) containing cream sub-sections of label + control field rows. Implementation Status blocks are terracotta-themed with a single status banner + a child “log” table.
Special widgets: Summary uses a component-wise overview table (one row per BE/EE/CE), a 2×2 aggregates grid (Infrastructure Costs, Funding Split, Interventions, Disbursement & Tracker) and an intervention-flag strip. The Financial Model has a tier/slab A/B/C funding-split reference (SELCO/MBMA/LIFCOM/USER %), four left-border funder cards that switch between static % (PRIME) and editable % inputs (Innovation/Scale), a % validation strip, and a LIFCOM EMI calculator (5-year / 3-year cards). The Tracker shows allocated-vs-disbursed tranche cards (cascading MBMA → USER → SELCO → LIFCOM), a vendor-payment disbursement table and a Disbursed − Paid balance card. EE machines render as collapsible cards with an auto-fetched detail panel.
Calculated fields (Plinth Rate, Rent/Unit, Electricity/Unit, BE Estimated Cost, EE/CE totals, all funder amounts, EMI, tranche allocations, balance) render italic on a cream-deep / info background with a formula tooltip per Rule 1.
Summary
→
Built Environment
→
Energy Efficient Machinery
→
Clean Energy
→
Financial Model
→
Tracker
→
Log Book
→
Resources

User Journey

Open dashboard (Summary)
Fessociate reviews costs & flags
→
Site assessment (BE/EE/CE)
Water, rent, land, electricity, solar
→
Flag interventions & estimate
Toggle need, MSOR cost, pull Required machines
→
SELCO Executive: pricing & status
SELCO price, quotation/BOM, equipment status
→
Financial Model & EMI
Slab/tier split, funder %, LIFCOM EMI
→
SELCO Executive: disbursement
Tranche allocation, vendor payments, balance
Tab: Summary (dashboard overview — opens first) ▼
Read-only dashboard. Nothing is entered here — every value is computed live from BE / EE / CE and the Financial Model. Two editable selectors govern what the dashboard shows: Type of Calculation (Estimation / Final) and the Financial Model Project.

Section I: Component-wise Overview (one row per component)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
ComponentRead-only labelBuilt Environment (BE) / Energy-Efficient Machines (EE) / Clean Energy · Solar (CE)AutofillThree fixed rows, colour-coded left border (BE teal, EE brown, CE green)--
DetailRead-onlyBE: intervention type; EE: list of machines; CE: list of solarised machinesAutofill from each tabShows “No intervention” / “flagged — type not selected” states--
CostRead-only (INR)--AutofillReflects active Type of Calculation — Estimation or Final per component--
Tranche AllocationRead-only breakdownMBMA / User / SELCO / LIFCOM amounts per componentAutofill from TrackerShows “Set project in Financial Model” when none allocated--
Intervention TypeRead-only badgeBE type / N machines / CE typeAutofillRequired column highlight (cream)--
Implementation StatusRead-only badgePer-component status (see each tab)AutofillColour-coded badge (pending / revision / confirmed / delivered)--

Section II: Aggregates (2×2 card grid)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Infrastructure Costs — BE / EE / CE / TotalRead-only (INR)--AutofillTotal = BE + EE + CE--
Funding Split — Active Slab/ModelRead-onlySlab A/B/C name or “Innovation/Scale (custom)”Autofill from Financial Model----
Funding Split — SELCO / MBMA / LIFCOM / UserRead-only (INR)--Autofillamount = Total × funder %--
EMI (5-year LIFCOM) / EMI (3-year LIFCOM)Read-only (INR/mo)--Autofill5yr = LIFCOM ÷ 60; 3yr = LIFCOM ÷ 36. Shows “N/A” for non-PRIME projects--
Interventions — BE flag / EE count / CE flagRead-onlyFlagged / Not flagged; integer countAutofill----
Rent / Unit · Electricity / UnitRead-only (INR)--Autofill from BE & CE----
Disbursement — Total Disbursed, Paid (Machines&Solar), Paid (BE), Total Paid, Balance with Entrepreneur, Vendor Payments Logged, Tasks LoggedRead-only (INR / count)--Autofill from Tracker & Task LogBalance = Disbursed − Paid--

Section III: Intervention Flags (4-tile strip)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
BE InterventionRead-only flag tileYes / NoAutofillMirrors BE “Need BE Intervention?” toggle--
EE MachinesRead-only count tileintegerAutofillCount of Required machine cards in EE tab--
CE InterventionRead-only flag tileYes / NoAutofillMirrors CE “Need CE Intervention?” toggle--
Total CostRead-only tile (INR)--AutofillShown in Lakh (e.g. ₹5.3L) when ≥ ₹1,00,000--
Tab: Built Environment (BE) ▼
Assess workspace, land and construction needs. BE cost is auto-calculated from the MSOR 2016 plinth-rate matrix (Hill × Typology) for RCC typologies, or entered manually for non-MSOR typologies (e.g. Kiosks). The SELCO Executive sets the Final DPR BE Cost in Implementation Status.

Section I: Need Assessment — Water

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Water SupplySelectOwn source, Locality source, PHE, Buys waterInput----
Monthly expenses on water (₹)Currency≥ 0Input--Numeric ≥ 0
Practice Rainwater Harvesting?Yes/No segmentedYes, NoInput----
Willing to practice Rainwater Harvesting?Yes/No segmentedYes, NoInput----

Section I: Need Assessment — Rent

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Where does the entrepreneur work?SelectHome, Separate WorkspaceInput----
Where is this space situated?SelectIn the house, Outside the house within premises, Within town / village, In the farm, Commercial complex, Market, OthersInput----
Condition of the WorkspaceSelectDilapidated, Poor, Average, Good, Very GoodInput----
Rent per Month (₹)Currency≥ 0InputFeeds Rent per UnitNumeric ≥ 0
Units Produced per MonthInt≥ 0Input (or autofill from Product Profile → Production Capacity)Shared value — mirrored read-only into CE tab--
Rent per UnitCurrency (calculated)--Autofill (read-only)Rent per Unit = Rent per Month ÷ Units Produced per Month (0 if units = 0). Feeds Unit Pricing → Workspace Cost--

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages

Land tenureship of proposed workspaceSelect(same list as above)InputConditional: reveals “who owns property?” when value is Assigned Community Land / Own Land purchased / Own Land inheritance--
If assigned/owned: who owns property?Select (conditional)Self, Family, Relatives, OthersInputHidden unless proposed tenureship is owned/assigned--
Type of soil conditionSelectFilled land, Rocky, OthersInput----
Upload Land DocumentAttach (.pdf/.jpg/.jpeg/.png)FileInputUpload + View buttons--
Upload Rough Sketch of Site Layout with DimensionAttach (.pdf/.jpg/.jpeg/.png)FileInputUpload + View buttons--

Section II: Intervention Need

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Need BE Intervention?ToggleOn / OffInputWhen On, reveals Type + Justify. Drives Summary BE flag--
Type of InterventionSelect (conditional)Renovation, Extension, NewInputVisible only when toggle On--
JustifyTextarea (conditional)Free textInputVisible only when toggle OnRequired * when BE intervention is flagged

Section III: Estimation (MSOR cost model)

MSOR 2016 plinth-rate matrix (₹ / SQM). Rate auto-fetches from Hill × Typology. SQM ↔ SQF conversion factor = 10.7639. Non-MSOR typologies (Kiosks) hide the MSOR fields and use manual cost entry.
Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Upload Rough Sketch of RequirementAttach (.pdf/.jpg/.jpeg/.png)FileInputUpload + View--
ElaborateTextareaFree textInputConstruction requirement detail--
Hill NameSelectKhasi Hills, Jaintia Hills, Garo HillsInputRow index into MSOR matrix--
Building TypologySelectRCC frame: PPGI sheets and steel truss; RCC frame: Ground Floor Institutional/Public; RCC frame: First Floor Institutional/Public; KiosksInputFirst three = MSOR (auto-calc); Kiosks = non-MSOR (manual)--
Plinth Rate / SQM — MSOR 2016 (₹)Currency (calculated)--Autofill (read-only)Rate = MSOR[Hill][Typology]. Matrix — Khasi: PPGI 25,701 / GF 26,165 / FF 18,554; Jaintia: PPGI 26,279 / GF 26,754 / FF 27,146; Garo: PPGI 26,665 / GF 18,971 / FF 19,250--
Possible Plinth Area (SQM)Float≥ 0InputTwo-way synced with SQF (SQF = SQM × 10.7639)Numeric ≥ 0
Possible Plinth Area (SQF)Float≥ 0InputTwo-way synced with SQM (SQM = SQF ÷ 10.7639)Numeric ≥ 0
Estimated Built Environment CostCurrency (calculated, MSOR)--Autofill (read-only)Estimated BE Cost = Plinth Rate × Plinth Area (SQM)--
Estimated Built Environment Cost (₹) — manualCurrency (conditional)≥ 0InputShown only for non-MSOR typology (Kiosks); MSOR rate matrix does not applyNumeric ≥ 0

Section IV: Implementation Status & Design Coordination (SELCO-editable banner)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Implementation StatusSelectPending Design, Design Revision, On hold, Design confirmed, Construction started, Construction completeInput (SELCO-editable)Drives Summary status badge--
Final DPR Built Environment Cost (₹)Currency≥ 0Input (SELCO-editable)Used as BE cost when Financial Model = “Final”Numeric ≥ 0
Design / Drawing Log — DateChild table: DatedateInputDefaults to today--
Design / Drawing Log — Architecture DrawingsChild table: AttachFile (.pdf/.jpg/.png)InputUpload + View per row--
Design / Drawing Log — FeedbackChild table: DataFree textInputNotes / feedback per revision--
NOC from Entrepreneur — Upload NOCAttach (.pdf/.jpg/.jpeg/.png)FileInputUpload + View--
Tab: Energy Efficient Machinery (EE / Machines) ▼
Every machine flagged Required in the Machinery framework auto-populates here as a collapsible card. Required machines arrive from the Machinery framework via dropdown selection — selecting one auto-fetches its read-only details (product, purpose, cost, condition, procurement status, usage, depreciation). The SELCO Executive enters the SELCO price and equipment status per machine.

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
MachineSelect / LinkMachinery framework → Required tab (dropdown). e.g. Turmeric Grinding Machine, Double Barrel Drum Dryer, Solar Dehydrator 50kg/batch, Sealing Machine Impulse 12″, PPGI Rooftop Fan 3-phaseAutofill source (Machinery framework, Required tab); selection by userChoosing a machine fetches its detail panel--
SELCO price (₹)Currency≥ 0Input (SELCO-editable)Confirmed SELCO price — summed into Total Estimated EE CostNumeric ≥ 0
Equipment Status (per machine)SelectPending, ImplementedInput (SELCO-editable)----
Fetched detail panel (read-only)Read-only groupProduct, Purpose, Cost of Machine, Condition, Procurement Status, Days of Usage/Month, Hours of Usage/Day, Current Usage Capacity/hr, Monthly Output, Depreciation Rate, Monthly Depreciation, Depreciation/UnitAutofill from Machinery frameworkRead-only here — edit in the Machinery framework--
Total Estimated EE CostCurrency (calculated)--Autofill (read-only)Total Estimated EE Cost = ∑ SELCO price across all machine cards--

Section II: Implementation Status & Quotation Log (SELCO-editable banner)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Implementation StatusSelectPending, Revision, On hold, Quotation confirmed, Order deliveredInput (SELCO-editable)Drives Summary status badge--
Final EE Quotation Total (₹)Currency≥ 0Input (SELCO-editable)Used as EE cost when Financial Model = “Final”Numeric ≥ 0
Quotation Log — DateChild table: DatedateInputDefaults to today--

Quotation Log — FeedbackChild table: DataFree textInputNotes / feedback per revision--
Tab: Clean Energy (CE / Solar) ▼
Clean-energy assessment — electricity unit cost, solarisation decisions and CE tracking. The solarisation table dropdown lists all machines from the Machinery framework (Existing + Required); the Required/Existing tag auto-fetches. The SELCO Executive approves and prices the solar lines and sets the Final CE quotation total.

Section I: Need Assessment

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Electricity per Month (₹)Currency≥ 0InputFeeds Electricity per UnitNumeric ≥ 0

Voltage & Phase ConfigurationSelectPhase I, Phase IIIInput----

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages

Type of InterventionSelect (conditional)Only lighting/fans/exhaust; Lighting + solar machines; Lighting + manual equipment + solar machines; Lighting + manual equipmentInputVisible only when toggle On--
JustifyTextarea (conditional)Free textInputVisible only when toggle OnRequired * when CE intervention is flagged

Section II: Machines considered for Solarization (child table)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
MachineChild table: Select / LinkAll Machinery framework machines (Existing + Required)Autofill source (Machinery framework, Existing + Required); selection by userSelecting a machine auto-fills the Required/Existing tag--
Required / ExistingChild table: Read-only tagRequired / ExistingAutofillDerived from the selected machine’s source--
Confirmed by Ground TeamChild table: CheckboxTRUE / FALSEInput----
Approved by SELCOChild table: CheckboxTRUE / FALSEInput (SELCO-editable)----
Solar CapacityChild table: Datae.g. “1.5 kWp”Input (SELCO-editable)----

Total Estimated CE CostCurrency (calculated)--Autofill (read-only)Total Estimated CE Cost = ∑ Solar Cost across rows--

Section III: Implementation Status & BOM/Quotation Log (SELCO-editable banner)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Implementation StatusSelectPending, Revision, On hold, Quotation confirmed, Order delivered, Installation started, Installation completeInput (SELCO-editable)Drives Summary status badge--
Final CE Quotation Total (₹)Currency≥ 0Input (SELCO-editable)Used as CE cost when Financial Model = “Final”Numeric ≥ 0
BOM / Quotation Log — DateChild table: DatedateInputDefaults to today--
BOM / Quotation Log — BOMChild table: AttachFileInputUpload BOM + View per row--
BOM / Quotation Log — FeedbackChild table: DataFree textInputNotes / feedback per revision--
Tab: Financial Model (MSOR tier/slab split + LIFCOM EMI) ▼
The funding model depends on the selected Project. PRIME SELCO Infra-Support uses fixed cost-based slabs (A/B/C) with static funder %; Innovation and Scale let the user enter custom funder % that must sum to 100%. Costs feed live from BE/EE/CE per the Type of Calculation (Estimation vs Final). EMI applies to PRIME only and is driven by the LIFCOM amount.

Section I: Controls & Cost Summary

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Project (Financial Model Breakup)SelectPRIME SELCO Infra-Support, Innovation, ScaleInputPRIME → slab + EMI sections shown, funder % static; Innovation/Scale → editable % inputs + % validation strip, slab/EMI hidden--
Type of CalculationSelectEstimation, FinalInputEstimation pulls BE Estimation + EE SELCO prices + CE Solar Costs; Final pulls Final DPR/Quotation totals from each Implementation Status banner--
BE Cost / EE Cost / CE CostCurrency (calculated)--Autofill (read-only)Per active Type of Calculation--
Total Infrastructure CostCurrency (calculated)--Autofill (read-only)Total = BE + EE + CE--

Section II: Funding Slab/Tier Reference (PRIME SELCO only)

Cost-based slab is auto-selected by Total Infrastructure Cost. Funder shares (SELCO / MBMA / LIFCOM / USER) are fixed per slab; USER is always 5%. Beyond ₹25,00,000 the model flags “Out of policy”.
SlabTotal Cost RangeSELCO %MBMA %LIFCOM %USER %
Slab ATotal < ₹5,00,00050%30%15%5%
Slab B₹5,00,000 ≤ Total < ₹10,00,00030%40%25%5%
Slab C₹10,00,000 ≤ Total < ₹25,00,00020%50%25%5%
Out of policyTotal ≥ ₹25,00,000No slab — manual override required

Section III: Funding Allocation (funder cards)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
SELCO Foundation — %Static label (PRIME) / Number input (Innovation/Scale)0–100Autofill (PRIME) / Input (Innovation/Scale) (SELCO-editable in custom modes)PRIME uses slab %In editable modes total must = 100%
MBMA — %Static / Number input0–100Autofill / InputPRIME uses slab %--
LIFCOM (Loan) — %Static / Number input0–100Autofill / InputDrives EMI calculator--
User Contribution — %Static / Number input0–100Autofill / InputPRIME always 5%--
Others — name + %Data + Number inputFree text name; 0–100Input (Innovation/Scale; 0% in PRIME)Optional extra funder--
Funder Amount (each card)Currency (calculated)--Autofill (read-only)Funder Amount = Total Infrastructure Cost × funder %--
% Validation (Innovation/Scale)Status strip--Autofill∑(SELCO+MBMA+LIFCOM+User+Others) must = 100%Warn until total = 100% (“must equal 100% — X% remaining”); OK at exactly 100%

Section IV: LIFCOM Loan EMI Calculator (PRIME SELCO only)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
5-Year LIFCOM EMICurrency / month (calculated)--Autofill (read-only)EMI(5yr) = LIFCOM Amount ÷ (5 × 12) = LIFCOM ÷ 60. Flat division — no interest--
3-Year LIFCOM EMICurrency / month (calculated)--Autofill (read-only)EMI(3yr) = LIFCOM Amount ÷ (3 × 12) = LIFCOM ÷ 36. Flat division — no interest--
EMI is a flat division and excludes interest. For finalised loan agreements, refer to the LIFCOM partner’s formal sanction letter.
Tab: Tracker (Disbursement & Vendor Payments) ▼
Disbursement tracking. Section I auto-computes tranche allocation per cost head using a cascading priority (MBMA → USER → SELCO → LIFCOM); within each tranche cost heads fill in order BE → EE → CE. Section II logs every vendor payment. The SELCO Executive records disbursement and vendor payments.

Section I: Tranche Allocations (auto-computed)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Tranche Total (MBMA / USER / SELCO / LIFCOM)Currency (calculated)--Autofill= that funder’s amount from the Financial Model--
Tranche → BE / EE / CE (12 cells)Currency (calculated)--AutofillCascade order MBMA → USER → SELCO → LIFCOM; within a tranche BE → EE → CE. Each cell = min(remaining tranche funds, remaining unallocated cost). Each later tranche sees costs reduced by what earlier tranches paid.--
Unallocated (per tranche)Currency (calculated)--Autofill= tranche total − (its BE + EE + CE allocations)--
Funding gap warningConditional banner--AutofillShown when any cost head is not fully covered by the four tranchesWarns “Unfunded: BE / EE / CE ₹…”

Section II: Vendor Payment Tracker — Sub-section I: Machines & Solar (SELCO-editable)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
PurposeChild table: SelectMachine, SolarInput (SELCO-editable)----
Paid ToChild table: DataVendor nameInput----
Amount (₹)Child table: Currency≥ 0InputSummed into Machines & Solar sub-totalNumeric ≥ 0
InvoiceChild table: AttachFileInputUpload + View per row--
CommentChild table: DataFree textInput----

Section II: Vendor Payment Tracker — Sub-section II: Built Environment (SELCO-editable)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
PurposeChild table: DataFree textInput (SELCO-editable)----
Paid ToChild table: DataVendor nameInput----
Amount (₹)Child table: Currency≥ 0InputSummed into Built Environment sub-totalNumeric ≥ 0
InvoiceChild table: AttachFileInputUpload + View per row--
CommentChild table: DataFree textInput----

Section II: Balance Card (calculated)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Total DisbursedCurrency (calculated)--Autofill (read-only)= sum of all 12 tranche → cost allocations (MBMA+USER+SELCO+LIFCOM)--
Total Paid to VendorsCurrency (calculated)--Autofill (read-only)= Machines & Solar sub-total + Built Environment sub-total--
Balance with EntrepreneurCurrency (calculated)--Autofill (read-only)= Total Disbursed − Total Paid--
Tab: Log Book (Task Log — Infrastructure) ▼
Activity log for the Infrastructure framework. One row per task; the count is shown in the header and mirrored into Summary “Tasks Logged”. One blank row renders on load.
Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
DateChild table: DatedateInputDefaults to today--
Task NameChild table: Select01 Awareness on Importance of Infrastructure; 02 Awareness on PRIME SELCO Infra Support; 03 Eligibility check completed; 04 Site Assessment for BE; 05 Site Assessment for EE; 06 Site Assessment for CE; 07 BE design Coordination; 08 EE design Coordination; 09 CE design Coordination; 10 NOC & compliance documents collected; 11 DPR approval & sign from Entrepreneur; 12 DPR submitted to Office; 13 Vendor coordination; 14 Quality check / site inspection; 15 If others, specifyInput----
Notes / OutcomeChild table: DataFree textInput----
AttachmentChild table: AttachFileInputUpload + View per row--
Tab: Resources ▼
Reference materials for BE / EE / CE assessment and funding. Static tiles, each with an external link.
ResourceDescriptionAction
MSOR 2016 Plinth Rate ScheduleHill-wise plinth rates for BE costingOpen schedule
Site Layout Sketch TemplateStandard sketch template for Extension / New / RenovationDownload
SELCO Solar Sizing CalculatorSolar capacity (kWp) sizing tool based on connected loadsOpen calculator
RWH GuidelinesMeghalaya-specific rainwater harvesting design guidelinesOpen
LIFCOM Loan ApplicationStandard LIFCOM loan application template with checklistDownload
EE Equipment ListEmpanelled BEE-rated energy-efficient machinery suppliersOpen list
Key validations (whole form): (1) BE/CE intervention “Justify” is required whenever the corresponding intervention toggle is On. (2) MSOR typologies auto-calc BE cost (Plinth Rate × SQM) and lock the cost field; non-MSOR typologies (Kiosks) require a manual cost entry. (3) SQM and SQF must stay in sync via factor 10.7639. (4) Innovation/Scale funder percentages must sum to exactly 100% (SELCO + MBMA + LIFCOM + USER + Others). (5) PRIME slab is cost-driven; Total ≥ ₹25,00,000 is “Out of policy” and needs manual override. (6) EMI (PRIME only) = LIFCOM amount ÷ 60 (5yr) or ÷ 36 (3yr) — flat, no interest. (7) Tranche disbursement must not over-allocate a cost head; uncovered amounts raise a funding-gap warning. (8) Balance with Entrepreneur = Total Disbursed − Total Paid. (9) SELCO Executive may edit only SELCO-relevant fields (SELCO price, equipment status, SELCO approval, solar capacity/cost, implementation status banners, Final cost totals, disbursement & vendor payments) — all other fields are read-only to that role. (10) Required machines must be selected from the Machinery framework dropdown; their detail panel is read-only here.
Section Review:
PR-FN-001

Pitch Deck

Entrepreneur-level framework. Guide entrepreneurs through creating an 11-slide pitch deck with existing assessment, content creation, and implementation tracking.

Tab Structure: Summary | Existing | Required | Resources | Log Book

Progress
Slides filled X/11, percentage bar
Slide Status
Per-slide filled/empty + impl status + last updated
Existing Assets
Deck uploaded?, Video link?, Intervention needed?

Slide Snapshots showing first 180 chars of each slide content.

FieldTypeLogic
Upload existing Pitch DeckAttach (.pdf, .ppt, .pptx)Input
Pitch Video LinkURL + "Open" buttonInput
Last UpdatedDateInput

#Slide TitleContent Field

2Problem StatementTextarea

Status (Pending/In Progress/Implemented/Not Feasible), Upload Pitch Deck, Pitch Video Link, Last Updated.

Resources: 5 tiles (Pitch Deck Skeleton, Sequoia Framework, Sample Decks, Financial Projection Worksheet, 5-Minute Pitch video).
Task Log: 18 pre-seeded tasks across 3 phases (Existing/Assessment/Implementation).
Log Book: Date, Activity, Visit Type (Site Visit/Phone Call/Pitch Rehearsal/Investor Meeting/Mentoring/Workshop/Other), Discussion, Person Met, Evidence upload.
