Section Review:
PR-AC-001

Financials

The Financials framework is the Accounts module of the Diagnostic Framework. It captures the entrepreneur's baseline financial picture (investment sources, loans, revenue & expenditure), bookkeeping practice pre/post intervention, monthly performance over time, and a Business Model Calculator that derives monthly break-even. Golden-Thread ties: the Calculator auto-fetches Existing loans from Financial Assessment, Existing revenue/expense from the Revenue & Expenditure card, and total monthly production quantity from the Production Capacity framework; its Projected break-even feeds the Summary tab.
Key behaviour: data flows across tabs — Financial Assessment (Loans) → Calculator (Existing Loan 1 & 2); Financial Assessment (Revenue & Expenditure) → Calculator (Existing column, editable override); Growth (monthly table) → Summary KPIs & trend chart; Calculator (Projected break-even) → Summary Monthly Break-even card. Bookkeeping Q1–Q3 captured Pre (Financial Assessment) and Post (Growth) drive the Summary Growth Trajectory.

On-Screen UI Structure

A teal hero bar reads "Financials" with the subtitle "Accounts · Financial Bookkeeping · Applicable to Entrepreneur" and Back / Save buttons. Below sits a horizontal tab rail of five tabs: Summary, Financial Assessment, Growth, Business Model Calculator, and Task Log.

Summary (virtual / read-only, rendered by DfFinancialsSummary.vue) leads with a 4-tile KPI grid (Total Investment, Avg Monthly Revenue, Avg Net Profit/Month, Profit Margin), then a two-column split of an "Investment & Loans" card (investment breakdown bar list + loans table) and a "Revenue & Income" averages card. Below is an SVG "Monthly Revenue & Profit Trend" bar chart (teal=revenue, terracotta=profit, redraws live from Growth edits), a "Bookkeeping Growth Trajectory" card of three Pre→Post comparison tiles (Practices Bookkeeping, Bookkeeping Level, Maintenance Frequency), and a "Monthly Break-even" card of two teal/terracotta hero tiles fetched from the Calculator's Projected scenario.

Financial Assessment opens with a Details card, then Section I — Assessment (Investment Sources child table, Loan Details child table, Revenue & Expenditure field grid with calculated profit/overall/margin), then Section II — Bookkeeping Practice (three baseline questions Q1–Q3) followed by a terracotta Intervention Need block with a toggle + justification textarea.

Growth has Section I — Monthly Tracker (a monthly performance child table with totals + three teal average stat tiles) and Section II — Implementation Status (terracotta), the post-intervention bookkeeping reassessment Q1–Q3.

Business Model Calculator is a collapsible four-section calculator (I. Loan Terms grid with auto-fetched/manual rows + add-to-total checkboxes; II. Revenue & Expense with Existing/Projected columns; III. Monthly Break-even output table; IV. Capital Costs card), ending in a teal result hero, a status banner (good/warn/bad), and a help card. A legend distinguishes auto-fetched (read-only), input, and auto-calculated cells.

Task Log is a single child table of bookkeeping intervention tasks with date, task dropdown, notes, and an upload/view attachment cell.

Calculated fields render italic on a cream-deep background with a formula tooltip (General Rule 1); auto-fetched fields are read-only on cream-deep (Rule 2).
Summary
→
Financial Assessment
→
Growth
→
Business Model Calculator
→
Task Log

User Journey

Open Financials
Fessociate via Diagnostics → Accounts → Financials, or EP dashboard framework card
→
Capture baseline
Financial Assessment: investment sources, loans, revenue/expense, bookkeeping Q1–Q3
→
Flag intervention
toggle Intervention Need + justify if bookkeeping gaps found
→
Log monthly data
Growth tab: add reporting months + revenue/expense/evidence
→
Run Calculator
enter Projected targets & new loan; break-even auto-computes
→
Save
Summary KPIs, trend, trajectory & break-even regenerate
Tab 1: Summary ▼

Virtual, read-only dashboard (DfFinancialsSummary.vue). No editable fields — every value is calculated or fetched from the other tabs. Listed below grouped by widget.

KPI Tiles (4)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Total InvestmentCurrencye.g. ₹2,00,000CalculatedSum of all Investment Sources rows (amt). Sub-label "Across N sources".Read-only; ≥ 0.
Avg Monthly RevenueCurrencye.g. ₹37,500CalculatedMean of Growth monthly Revenue across all months on file.Read-only; "—" when no months.
Avg Net Profit / MonthCurrencye.g. ₹13,800CalculatedAvg Revenue − Avg Expenditure (after expenses & EMI). Mean of (Revenue−Expense) over Growth months.Read-only.
Profit MarginPercente.g. 36.8%Calculated(Avg Net Profit ÷ Avg Revenue) × 100, to 1 decimal. "—" if Avg Revenue ≤ 0.Read-only.

Investment & Loans card

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Investment breakdown (bar list)DisplaySource name + amount + barAutofill from Investment SourcesOne bar per source; bar width = source amount ÷ total investment × 100%.Read-only.
Loans summary tableDisplaySource, Sanctioned, Outstanding, EMIAutofill from Loan DetailsLists each loan row. Header shows "N sources · M active loan(s)".Read-only.

Revenue & Income card (per-month averages)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Avg Monthly Business RevenueCurrencye.g. ₹35,000Autofill from Financial AssessmentRead-only.Read-only.
Avg Monthly ExpenditureCurrencye.g. ₹22,000AutofillRead-only.Read-only.
Average Additional IncomeCurrencye.g. ₹2,500AutofillRead-only.Read-only.
Avg Overall Monthly RevenueCurrencye.g. ₹37,500CalculatedAvg Monthly Business Revenue + Average Additional Income.Read-only.
Avg Net Profit / MonthCurrencye.g. ₹13,800CalculatedAvg Monthly Business Revenue − Avg Monthly Expenditure.Read-only.
Business StatusPillOperational / …AutofillStatus pill carried from business profile.Read-only.

Trend chart, Trajectory & Break-even

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Monthly Revenue & Profit TrendSVG bar chartteal=Revenue, terracotta=ProfitCalculatedOne bar group per Growth month; Profit = Revenue − Expense. Y-axis auto-scales (nice ceiling). Empty state: "Add months in the Growth tab to plot the trend".Read-only; redraws live on Growth edits.
Trajectory — Practices BookkeepingPre→Post tileYes / NoAutofillPre = Financial Assessment Q1; Post = Growth Q1. Delta: No→Yes "Started practicing"; Yes→No "Stopped practicing"; Yes→Yes "Continues to practice".Read-only; "Set values…" if either unset.
Trajectory — Bookkeeping LevelPre→Post tileLevel 1–5AutofillPre = FA Q2; Post = Growth Q2. Levels mapped 1–5 (None/Informal/Basic/Structured/Digital). Delta = Post − Pre levels (e.g. "+2 levels").Read-only.
Trajectory — Maintenance FrequencyPre→Post tileYearly…DailyAutofillPre = FA Q3; Post = Growth Q3. Ordered Yearly<Occasionally<Monthly<Weekly<Daily. Delta: "More frequent" / "Less frequent" / "Same frequency".Read-only.
Monthly Break-even RevenueCurrencye.g. ₹1,50,000 or "Not achievable"Autofill from Calculator (Projected)Mirrors Calculator Revenue Break-even/Month (Projected scenario).Read-only.
Monthly Break-even UnitsInte.g. 820 units or "Not achievable"Autofill from Calculator (Projected)Units needed to cover Expense + EMI; mirrors Calculator Unit Break-even/Month.Read-only.
All Summary widgets are read-only mirrors. KPIs & trend recompute from Growth; Revenue & Income averages and Trajectory Pre from Financial Assessment; Break-even from the Calculator's Projected column.
Tab 2: Financial Assessment ▼

Details (header card)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Entrepreneur NameLink (Entrepreneur)e.g. Banshan MarakAutofillFrom the EP context the framework is opened in.Read-only.
ModuleDataAccountsAutofillFixed = "Accounts".Read-only.
Applicable ToDataEntrepreneurAutofillFixed = "Entrepreneur".Read-only.
LifecyclePillActive / …AutofillEP lifecycle status.Read-only.

Section I — Assessment · Investment Sources (child table)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Investment SourceSelectSelf-funded, Family/Friends, Friends, Bank Loan, Government Scheme, NGO, OtherInputDrives "Other Source Name" enablement.Required per row.
Other Source NameDataFree textInput (conditional)Enabled only when Investment Source = "Other"; otherwise disabled/greyed (opacity 0.5, no pointer).Required if Investment Source = "Other" — "Specify the other investment source".
Source NameDatae.g. Personal savings, Mother — handloom unitInputDescriptive label for the source.Recommended.
AmountCurrencye.g. 1,50,000InputFeeds Total Investment Sources Amount + Summary breakdown bars.≥ 0.
Total Investment Sources AmountCurrencye.g. ₹2,00,000Calculated (footer)Sum of all rows' Amount; also shown in card header.Read-only.
"+ Add Investment Source" appends a row; "✕" removes. Total recomputes on every amount edit.

Section I — Assessment · Loan Details (child table)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Loan AmountCurrencye.g. 2,00,000InputFirst two rows' amount sync to Calculator Existing Loan 1 & 2.≥ 0.
Interest %Floate.g. 12 (% p.a.)InputStep 0.01; first two rows sync to Calculator loan rate.≥ 0; typically ≤ 100.
OutstandingCurrencye.g. 1,80,000InputCurrent outstanding principal; shown in Summary loans table.≥ 0; ≤ Loan Amount — "Outstanding cannot exceed Loan Amount".
Term (mo)Inte.g. 60InputNo. of instalments (months); first two rows sync to Calculator instalments.≥ 0 (integer).
StartDatee.g. 2026-04-01InputLoan start date.End must be ≥ Start — "Loan end date must be on or after start date".
EndDatee.g. 2031-04-01InputLoan end date.≥ Start.
EMICurrencye.g. 4,500InputMonthly repayment recorded; shown in Summary loans table.≥ 0.
Loan %Floate.g. 100InputLoan contribution as % of total.0 – 100.
Loan/SchemeDatae.g. PMEGP — SBIInputScheme name; first two rows' name sync to Calculator loan row labels.Recommended.
"+ Add Loan" appends a row. Header shows active loan count. The first two loan rows auto-populate the Calculator's Existing Loan 1 & 2 (amount, rate, instalments, scheme name).

Section I — Assessment · Revenue & Expenditure

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Avg Monthly Business RevenueCurrencye.g. 35,000InputSource of truth for Calculator Existing Revenue (unless overridden in Calculator).≥ 0.
Avg Monthly ExpenditureCurrencye.g. 22,000InputSource of truth for Calculator Existing Expense (unless overridden).≥ 0.
Avg Additional IncomeCurrencye.g. 2,500InputFeeds Avg Overall Monthly Revenue.≥ 0.
Avg Net Profit Per MonthCurrencye.g. ₹13,000CalculatedAvg Monthly Business Revenue − Avg Monthly Expenditure.Read-only.
Additional Income SourceDatae.g. Family handloom dividendsInputDescribes the additional income.Recommended if Avg Additional Income > 0.
Avg Overall Monthly RevenueCurrencye.g. ₹37,500CalculatedAvg Monthly Business Revenue + Avg Additional Income.Read-only.
Profit MarginPercente.g. 34.7%Calculated(Avg Net Profit ÷ Avg Overall Monthly Revenue) × 100, 1 decimal. "—" if Overall ≤ 0.Read-only.
Revenue EvidenceAttache.g. revenue-apr-may-2026.pdfInputSupporting document for stated revenue.Recommended.
Business StatusPillOperational / …AutofillFrom business profile.Read-only.
Calculation: Avg Net Profit = Revenue − Expenditure; Avg Overall Revenue = Revenue + Additional Income; Margin = Net Profit ÷ Overall Revenue. These values push live into the Calculator's Existing column.

Section II — Bookkeeping Practice (Pre baseline)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Q1: Does the Entrepreneur practice Bookkeeping?SelectYes, NoInputFeeds Summary Trajectory Pre (Practices Bookkeeping).Required to populate trajectory.
Q2: Level of Entrepreneur's Bookkeeping practiceSelectLevel 1 — None (no records kept); Level 2 — Informal (mental notes, scattered slips); Level 3 — Basic (handwritten cash book, uncategorised); Level 4 — Structured (categorised ledger, monthly totals); Level 5 — Digital (digital tool, categorised, regular)InputMapped to 1–5; feeds Trajectory Pre (Bookkeeping Level).Required to populate trajectory.
Q3: How often does he maintain his Bookkeeping?SelectDaily, Weekly, Monthly, Yearly, OccasionallyInputOrdered Yearly<Occasionally<Monthly<Weekly<Daily; feeds Trajectory Pre (Frequency).Required to populate trajectory.

Section II — Intervention Need (terracotta)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Needs Intervention in bookkeeping?Check (toggle)On / OffInputWhen ON, reveals the Justify textarea.—
JustifyTextareaFree textInput (conditional)Shown only when toggle is ON. "Why is the intervention needed? What gaps did the assessment surface?"Required when toggle ON — "Justify why bookkeeping intervention is needed".
Conditional: the Justify field is hidden until the Intervention toggle is switched on; a justification is mandatory once flagged.
Tab 3: Growth ▼

Section I — Monthly Tracker · Monthly Performance (child table)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Reporting MonthDatae.g. Jun 2026InputOne row per reporting month; spans the cohort start→end years (General Rule 11). Short month label feeds the Summary trend chart X-axis.Required per row.
Revenue (₹)Currencye.g. 44,000InputFeeds totals, averages, KPIs and trend chart.≥ 0.
Expenses (₹)Currencye.g. 25,000InputFeeds totals, averages and trend chart.≥ 0.
Profit (₹)Currencye.g. ₹19,000CalculatedRevenue − Expenses (per row).Read-only.
EvidenceAttache.g. jun-2026.pdfInputSupporting document for the month's figures.Recommended.
Totals (footer)CurrencyRev / Exp / Profit sumsCalculatedColumn sums across all rows.Read-only.
Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Average RevenueCurrencye.g. ₹36,500Calculated (stat tile)Mean Revenue across N reporting months on file.Read-only.
Average ExpenditureCurrencye.g. ₹23,000Calculated (stat tile)Avg Revenue − Avg Profit (= mean Expense).Read-only.
Average ProfitCurrencye.g. ₹13,500Calculated (stat tile)Mean Profit (Avg Revenue − Avg Expenditure) across N months.Read-only.
The monthly tracker spans the cohort start→end years (General Rule 11). Averages are computed over all months on file (whatever the count). These averages drive the Summary KPI tiles and trend chart.

Section II — Implementation Status · Bookkeeping Practice (Post intervention)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Q1: Has the entrepreneur improved bookkeeping practice?SelectYes, NoInputFeeds Summary Trajectory Post (Practices Bookkeeping), compared against FA Pre Q1.Required to complete trajectory.
Q2: Level of Entrepreneur's Bookkeeping practiceSelectLevel 1 — None; Level 2 — Informal; Level 3 — Basic; Level 4 — Structured; Level 5 — DigitalInputFeeds Trajectory Post (Bookkeeping Level); delta = Post − Pre.Required to complete trajectory.
Q3: How often does he maintain his Bookkeeping?SelectDaily, Weekly, Monthly, Yearly, OccasionallyInputFeeds Trajectory Post (Frequency); delta vs FA Pre Q3.Required to complete trajectory.
Post-intervention reassessment: these three answers are the Post values for the Summary's Bookkeeping Growth Trajectory, compared against the Pre values captured in Financial Assessment Section II.
Tab 4: Business Model Calculator ▼

Four collapsible sections. A legend distinguishes auto-fetched (read-only), input, and auto-calculated cells. Existing column figures auto-fetch from Financial Assessment; Projected column is the entrepreneur's post-intervention target.

Section I — Loan Terms (grid)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Existing Loan 1 — Loan/SchemeDatae.g. PMEGP — SBIAutofill from Financial Assessment (Loan 1)Row label; falls back to "Existing Loan 1" if no scheme name.Read-only.
Existing Loan 1 — Amount / Interest Rate (% p.a.) / No. of InstalmentsCurrency / Float / Inte.g. 4,00,000 / 6 / 60Autofill from FA Loan 1Cream-deep, italic, read-only (Rule 2).Read-only.
Existing Loan 2 — Loan/Scheme + Amount / Rate / InstalmentsData / Currency / Float / IntFrom FA Loan 2 (0 if none)Autofill from FA Loan 2Read-only.Read-only.
New Loan Amount — AmountCurrencyManual entryInputManual new-loan principal for the projected scenario.≥ 0.
New Loan Amount — Interest Rate (% p.a.)FloatManual entry (step 0.01)InputUsed in EMI amortisation.≥ 0.
New Loan Amount — No. of Instalments (months)IntManual entryInputUsed in EMI amortisation.≥ 0.
Loan Contribution (% of Total)Floatper loanInputInformational contribution share.0 – 100.
Add to Total (per loan)CheckOn / OffInputOnly checked loans (with amount > 0) are summed into Total Loan Amount and the EMI calc.—
Total Loan Amount (BE, EE, CE)Currencye.g. ₹4,00,000CalculatedSum of all loan amounts whose "Add to Total" checkbox is on.Read-only.
Existing Loan 1 & 2 are auto-fetched from Financial Assessment → Loan Details (first two loans). New Loan is manual. Total Loan Amount = sum of all checked loans.

Section II — Revenue & Expense (Existing vs Projected)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Total Quantity (All Products — Monthly) — ExistingInte.g. 400Autofill from Production CapacityFetched from Production Capacity → "Average Production unit per month". Read-only.Read-only.
Total Quantity (All Products — Monthly) — ProjectedInte.g. 1,500InputEntrepreneur's monthly quantity target.≥ 0.
Weighted Avg Selling Price / Unit (₹) — ExistingFloatCalculatedCalculatedAverage Revenue per month ÷ Total Quantity (Existing).Read-only (calc-cell).
Weighted Avg Selling Price / Unit (₹) — ProjectedFloatCalculatedCalculatedAverage Revenue per month ÷ Total Quantity (Projected).Read-only.
Average Revenue per month (₹) — ExistingCurrencye.g. 36,500Autofill (editable override)Auto-fetched from FA → Revenue & Expenditure; editing sets "Overridden" flag and stops re-sync.≥ 0.
Average Revenue per month (₹) — ProjectedCurrencye.g. 2,80,000InputPost-intervention target revenue.≥ 0.
Average Expense per month (₹) — ExistingCurrencye.g. 23,000Autofill (editable override)Auto-fetched from FA; editing sets "Overridden" flag.≥ 0.
Average Expense per month (₹) — ProjectedCurrencye.g. 50,000InputPost-intervention target expense.≥ 0.
Note: Existing Revenue/Expense are auto-fetched from Financial Assessment but remain editable — an override here displays a "· Overridden" badge and prevents the FA values from re-clobbering the cell. "Reset all fields" clears the Projected column, checkboxes, and new-loan inputs.

Section III — Monthly Break-even (output, auto-calculated)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Operating Profit / (Loss) — Before EMI (Existing & Projected)CurrencycalcCalculatedRevenue − Expense (per column). Negative renders in danger colour.Read-only.
EMI (Existing & Projected)CurrencycalcCalculatedSum of per-loan EMI (amortisation, see Section IV); same value shown both columns.Read-only.
Net Profit / (Loss)CurrencycalcCalculatedOperating Profit − EMI (per column).Read-only.
Variable cost per unitFloatcalcCalculatedAverage Expense per month ÷ Total Quantity (per column).Read-only; "—" if quantity 0.
Contribution per unitFloatcalcCalculatedWeighted Avg Selling Price − Variable cost per unit (per column).Read-only.
Monthly fixed costs incl. EMICurrencycalcCalculatedAverage Expense per month + EMI (per column).Read-only.
Revenue Break-even / Month (₹)Currencycalc or "Not achievable"CalculatedUnit Break-even × Weighted Avg Selling Price. "Not achievable" if Contribution ≤ 0.Read-only.
Unit Break-even / Month (units)Intcalc or "Not achievable"Calculated⌈ (Monthly fixed costs incl. EMI) ÷ Contribution per unit ⌉. "Not achievable" if Contribution ≤ 0.Read-only.

Section IV — Capital Costs

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Total Capital CostCurrencye.g. ₹4,00,000CalculatedEquals Total Loan Amount (checked loans).Read-only.
Loan Amount TakenCurrencye.g. ₹4,00,000CalculatedTotal Loan Amount.Read-only.
Interest Rate (blended)Floate.g. 6.00 %CalculatedAmount-weighted average rate across included loans (informational).Read-only.
No. of Instalments (blended)Inte.g. 60 monthsCalculatedAmount-weighted average tenure, rounded (informational).Read-only.
EMI per monthCurrencye.g. ₹7,733CalculatedComputed per-loan via amortisation and summed.Read-only.
Total Loan Repayment incl. interestCurrencye.g. ₹4,63,980CalculatedTotal EMI × blended instalments.Read-only.
EMI amortisation: EMI = P × r × (1+r)n ÷ ((1+r)n − 1), where r = annual rate ÷ 1200 and n = instalments (if r = 0, EMI = P ÷ n). With multiple checked loans a blended rate/tenure is shown for information; EMI is computed per-loan and summed.

Result hero & Status banner

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
Monthly Break-even Revenue (hero)CurrencyProjected RBE or "Not achievable"CalculatedMirrors Section III Projected Revenue Break-even; also mirrored to Summary.Read-only.
Monthly Break-even Units (hero)IntProjected UBE or "Not achievable"CalculatedMirrors Section III Projected Unit Break-even; also mirrored to Summary.Read-only.
Status BannerDisplaygood / warn / badCalculatedIf Projected Contribution ≤ 0 → bad "Not achievable"; if Projected Quantity ≥ Unit Break-even → good "At or above break-even"; else warn "Below break-even" with units-needed gap.Read-only.
Help-card logic: Contribution/unit = Selling price/unit − Variable cost/unit (Variable cost/unit = Total Expense ÷ Quantity); Monthly obligation = Total Expense + EMI; Break-even units = ⌈ obligation ÷ contribution/unit ⌉; Break-even revenue = units × selling price. Contribution ≤ 0 → "Not achievable".
Tab 5: Task Log ▼

Task Log — Financials (child table)

Field NameField TypeInput ValuesInput / AutofillLogicValidation & Error Messages
No.Intauto 01, 02…CalculatedZero-padded row index.Read-only.
DateDatee.g. 2026-03-10InputDate the task was performed.Required per row.
Task NameSelect01 — Initial Financial Assessment; 02 — Awareness on Importance of Bookkeeping; 03 — Financial Data Recorded in CRM; 04 — Bank Passbook Detail Collection; 05 — Loan Details Documented; 06 — Bookkeeping Training Delivered; 07 — Business & Home Money Separation Training; 08 — Cash Book Follow Up; 09 — Monthly Performance Updated; 10 — Monthly P&L Review; 11 — Assessed the Monthly Break-even through BMC; 12 — Follow Up on Bookkeeping Practices; 13 — Awareness on Digital Bookkeeping; 14 — Digital Bookkeeping Training Delivered; 15 — Investment Planning Session; 16 — If others, specifyInputStandard bookkeeping intervention task list.Required per row.
Notes / OutcomeDataFree textInputOutcome or follow-up note.Optional.
AttachmentAttachUpload · View · filenameInputUpload button stores filename; View enabled only when an attachment exists.Optional.
"+ Add Entry" appends a task row; header shows the entry count. Task "16 — If others, specify" is the free-text catch-all.
Key validations (whole form): Investment Source "Other Source Name" is required when source = "Other". Loan Outstanding cannot exceed Loan Amount; Loan End date must be on or after Start. All currency/quantity amounts must be ≥ 0; Interest % and Loan % in range 0–100. Bookkeeping Q1–Q3 must be answered (Pre in Financial Assessment, Post in Growth) to populate the Summary Growth Trajectory. When Intervention Need is toggled ON, the Justify reason is mandatory. Calculator: when Contribution per unit ≤ 0, break-even is reported as "Not achievable" (selling more units cannot cover the monthly obligation). Each Task Log row requires Date and Task Name.
Section Review:
PR-LC-001

Product Compliance (Legal Compliance module)

New framework introduced via the updated Module Master Google Sheet. Tabs follow the standard pattern: Summary · Existing · Required · Log Book · Resources. Tracks product-level certifications and compliance (FSSAI, BIS, AGMARK, etc.) separately from business registrations. Detailed field-level spec to be drafted in a follow-up pass.
PR-DB-001

Entrepreneurial Landscape Map

Landing page for Fessociates and Core Team. An interactive map showing all entrepreneurs in the portal. Pins are plotted from entrepreneur profile geo-locations and village survey geo-tags.
Data sources: Entrepreneur Profile (location, sector, business basket, cohort, status), Village Survey (geo-tagged locations for village pins), Product (product names linked to entrepreneurs).

Filters

FilterTypeBehaviourDefault
DistrictMulti-selectCascading — filters Block optionsAll
BlockMulti-selectFiltered by District. Cascading — filters entrepreneur listAll
CohortSelectFilters by cohort batchAll
SectorSelectFood Processing, Agriculture, Handicrafts etc.All
Business BasketSelectPickles, Teas, Spices, Earrings etc.All
Product NameSearch/SelectAutocomplete from Product MasterNone

Map Behaviour

Pins
Each entrepreneur = one pin. Default Meghalaya view. Geo-coordinates from EP Profile location field. Villages from Village Survey geo-tags also shown.
Pin Click
Shows summary popup: Entrepreneur Name, Block, District, Sector, Status (MET/Incubated/Supported), Product count. Click "View Profile" to navigate to EP Profile.
Clustering
At zoom-out levels, pins cluster into count bubbles. Click to zoom into cluster.
Filter interaction
Pins dynamically show/hide as filters change. Count updates in real-time.
Section Review:
PR-DB-002

Intervention Dashboard

Aggregated view of all intervention progress across entrepreneurs and frameworks. Shows KPIs, charts, and drill-downs into module-level data.

KPI Cards

Total Entrepreneurs
Count of all EP Profiles
Interventions Active
Count of "In Progress" across all frameworks
Interventions Completed
Count of "Completed" across all frameworks
Frameworks Covered
Count of unique frameworks with at least 1 entry

Filters

FilterTypeBehaviour
DistrictMulti-selectCascading with Block
BlockMulti-selectFiltered by District
ModuleSelectFilters framework dropdown + charts
FrameworkSelectFiltered by Module
StatusMulti-selectPending, In Progress, Completed, Not Feasible
CohortSelectBatch filter

Visualisations

Sankey Flow
MET → Incubated/Supported → Sectors → Business Baskets. Shows entrepreneur flow through the programme lifecycle.
Donut Charts
Intervention status distribution (Pending/In Progress/Completed/Not Feasible). Per-module breakdown.
Horizontal Bar
Framework-wise intervention count. Stacked by status. Sorted by total descending.
Drill-down
Click any chart segment to filter the entrepreneur list below. Click entrepreneur name to navigate to profile.
Intervention Dashboard KPI Form: Pending from client. The dashboard will also have a form component for entering/editing KPI targets. Spec to be updated when received.
Section Review:
PR-DB-003

Milestone Tracker

Programme-level milestone and activity tracking dashboard. Built on Milestone Master + Milestone Activity doctypes only (NOT Calendar Task or DF Master). Workspace: Progress Matrix.

Tabs

Overview
→
Activities
→
Timeline
→
RACI Matrix

Overview Tab KPIs

Total Milestones
Count of Milestone Master records (26)
Total Activities
Count of Milestone Activity records (85)
Completed
Activities with status = Completed
In Progress
Activities with status = In Progress

Charts

Lollipop: Activities by Milestone
3 dot series: Completed (green), In Progress (amber), Total (grey). Per milestone horizontal axis.
Gantt: Timeline
Dual bars per activity: Planned dates (teal) vs Executed dates (terracotta). Shows delays visually.
Activities Table
Sortable, filterable table of all activities with milestone, status, quarter, dates, amounts, impact.
RACI Tab
Milestone activities with open links. Shows Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed per activity.
Two-layer data strategy: Embedded data (always works even without session) + background live refresh from API. Live banner shows data freshness. Brand: forest green #1a6b3c for this dashboard (legacy design system, not Monsoon Court).
Section Review:
PR-DB-004

EP Status Monitor

Entrepreneur status overview for monitoring activity levels, geographic distribution, and selection status across the programme.

Filters

FilterTypeBehaviour
DistrictSelectPre-fills based on user's assigned district (for Fessociate)
BlockSelectFiltered by district
CohortSelectFrom Cohort master — e.g. Cohort 1 (Apr 2026 – Mar 2029). Independent filter.
SectorSelectFrom Sector master. Independent filter — can be applied alone.
Business BasketSelectFrom Business Basket master. Independent filter — can be applied alone. Cascading behaviour: if a Sector is also selected, the Business Basket dropdown is filtered to baskets within that sector. If Sector is cleared, all baskets become available again.
Time RangeTabs: All / Today / This Week / This Month / This YearFilters by visit date or creation date
Activity StatusSelectActive, Inactive
Selection StatusSelectMET, Incubated, Supported
Filter combination logic: All filters are AND-combined for the result set. Sector and Business Basket each work independently — either can be applied alone — but when both are selected, Business Basket is constrained to the chosen Sector.

Dashboard Content

KPI Cards
Total Entrepreneurs, Active count, Inactive count, MET/Incubated/Supported split, new this period.
Geographic Breakdown
District-wise and block-wise entrepreneur count. Bar chart or table.
Status Distribution
Donut: MET vs Incubated vs Supported. Activity status breakdown.
Entrepreneur List
Filtered list with name, block, status, last visit date, product count. Click to open profile.
Section Review:
PR-DB-005

Entrepreneur Progress Report

Per-entrepreneur progress overview showing framework completion, intervention status, and activity log summary.
Pending from client: Full spec for this dashboard. Current understanding derived from the journey doc and existing Profile Dashboard behaviour.

Expected Content (from Journey Doc + Profile Dashboard)

Product Photo Cards + Add New
All products for the entrepreneur show as clickable photo cards. Clicking a card opens that product's Product Profile form. After the last card, an additional "+ Add New Product" tile is rendered — clicking it opens a fresh Product doctype form, pre-filled with the entrepreneur reference, ready for a new product to be added.
Framework Completion
Per framework task: shows "Not Started" by default. "Completed" when first activity log exists. "Completed x2" etc. for repetitions. Summary popup shows % completion.
Framework Priority Order
Cards ordered by priority number from Prioritisation tab. Priority 0 = default. Lower number = higher priority. Reorders dynamically on change.
Intervention Logs (Read-only)
All activities from Fellow Activity Logger for this entrepreneur. Framework + non-framework activities. Read-only display.
Section Review:
PR-XC-001

User Management

Accessible to Core Team IT (full CRUD) and Core Team Program (create + edit, no delete). All other roles have no access.

Add User Flow

Open User Management
Left menu
→
Click "Add User"
Top right
→
Fill User Form
See fields below
→
Assign Role
From 6 roles
→
Assign Block
For Fessociates
→
Save
Triggers welcome email
User Form Fields ▼
FieldTypeValuesLogicValidation
Full NameDataFree text--Required
EmailEmail--Used as username for login. If Gmail → Google SSO enabled.Required. Valid email format. Must be unique.
PhonePhoneDefault +91---Valid phone format
RoleSelectCore Team IT, Core Team Program, Fessociate, Designer, SELCO Executive, District CoordinatorDetermines sidebar menu visibility + permissionsRequired. Exactly one role.
Assigned BlockLink (Block master)From Block masterRequired for Fessociate role. Auto-fills District/Block on forms created by this user.Required if role = Fessociate
Assigned DistrictLink (District master)From District masterAuto-derived from Block or manually set--
Login & Welcome Email ▼
Welcome Email
On user creation, a custom-template email fires automatically containing: set-password link, role-based quick pointers (expected actions for that role), and a welcome message.
Login Methods
Method 1: Email (as username) + password set via welcome email link.
Method 2: If email is a Gmail address, user can login via Google SSO.
Password Reset
Standard Frappe "Forgot Password" flow. Reset link sent to registered email.
Session Management
Standard Frappe session management. Auto-logout on inactivity. Single active session per user (configurable).
Section Review:
PR-XC-002

Fellow Activity Logger

Replaces the Tasks workspace. This is how Fessociates log all field activities. Two views: Calendar (default) and Board. Activities sync to framework Log Books and EP Profile Intervention Logs.
Key data flows: Entrepreneur + framework type activities → sync to respective framework Log Book tabs → reflect as read-only Intervention Logs on EP Profile. Also drives framework completion tracking on EP Profile Dashboard (Not Started → Completed → Completed xN).

Views

Calendar View (Default)
All activities the fellow has logged render as calendar tiles on the date they were logged for. Tile preview shows date, type, entrepreneur name, framework. Multi-activity days show stacked chips. Click a tile to expand details inline. This is the landing view on workspace open.
Board View (Block-wise Cards)
Toggled via the top-right view switch. Activities are grouped block-wise into card columns per the HTML wireframe. Within each block, cards are sorted in descending order of logging date — latest activities at the top. Each card shows entrepreneur, framework / non-framework chip, task, comments preview, and photo thumbnail.

Filters

FilterTypeBehaviour
DistrictMulti-selectCascading — filters Block options
BlockMulti-selectFiltered by selected District(s)
EntrepreneurSingle selectShows entrepreneurs in selected blocks
ModuleSingle selectFilters Framework dropdown
FrameworkSingle selectFiltered by selected Module
Framework TasksSingle selectFiltered by selected Framework (from task master)

Add Activity Form — Branching Logic

Activity Form (Full Branching Spec) ▼
StepFieldTypeValuesLogicValidation
1DateDateDefault = todayEditableRequired. ≤ today (no future dates)
2Activity TypeSelectEntrepreneur / Non-EntrepreneurBranching point: determines which fields show nextRequired

Branch A: Entrepreneur Activity

StepFieldTypeValuesLogicValidation
3aSub-typeSelectFramework Activity / Non-Framework ActivityIf Framework: show steps 4a-6a. If Non-Framework: skip to Comments.Required
4aEntrepreneurSingle selectFrom EP Profile masterFiltered by user's blockRequired
5aFrameworkSingle selectFrom Diagnostic Framework master--Required
6aFramework TaskSingle selectFrom Task Master, filtered by selected FrameworkCascading filterRequired
7aCommentsTextareaFree text----
8aPhoto UploadAttach + ViewImage fileUpload and view--

Branch B: Non-Entrepreneur Activity

StepFieldTypeValuesValidation
3bActivity DetailsTextareaFree textRequired
4bCommentsTextareaFree text--
5bPhoto UploadAttach + ViewImage file--

On Submit Behaviour

Activity TypeWhat Happens on Submit
Entrepreneur + Framework1. Activity appears as calendar tile
2. Syncs to framework's Log Book tab for that entrepreneur (Fessociate autofill, Entrepreneur autofill, Task from selection, Upload, Comments)
3. Syncs to EP Profile → Intervention Logs (read-only)
4. Updates framework completion on EP Dashboard: Not Started → Completed. If repeated: shows "Completed xN"
Entrepreneur + Non-Framework1. Calendar tile
2. Syncs to EP Profile Intervention Logs (read-only)
3. Does NOT appear in any framework Log Book
Non-Entrepreneur1. Calendar tile only
2. No sync to any EP Profile or framework form
Section Review:
PR-XC-002b

Framework Task Master

Single master that feeds the Task dropdown wherever a fellow logs an activity — both inside any framework form's Log Book tab and inside the Fellow Activity Logger workspace. The source-of-truth data lives in from-client/Final PRIME Rural Task Dropdowns Master.xlsx — 27 framework sheets, 351+ tasks.

DocType: Framework Task Master

Field Type Values / Source Visibility Logic Validation
ModuleLinkModule master (13 modules)Core Team IT only (master entry)Filters the Framework dropdownRequired
FrameworkLinkDiagnostic Framework master (27 frameworks)Core Team IT only (master entry)Filtered by selected ModuleRequired
Task No.DataTwo-digit string ("01", "02"…)Core Team IT onlyAuto-increments within framework on new entry; editable to allow reorderingRequired, unique within (Module, Framework)
Task NameDataFree textVisible to Fellow — this is the only field shown in the activity-log task dropdown--Required, unique within (Module, Framework)
DescriptionSmall TextFree textCore Team IT enters; shown on hover/expand in the activity log dropdown--Recommended (not strictly required)
PhaseSelectAwareness · Assessment · Training · Implementation · Follow-up · OtherCore Team IT only — not shown to Fellow anywhereInternal taxonomy for core-team reporting; never surfaces in the fellow's activity logRequired

The "Other" Task — One Per Framework

Every framework's task list must contain exactly one row whose Task Name is "Other". Phase for this row is Other. This is the catch-all the fellow selects when their on-ground activity does not match any predefined task.
  • What fellow sees: "Other" appears as the last option in the Task dropdown for that framework.
  • What fellow does: picks "Other", then writes free-text in the existing Comments column of the activity logging table to explain what the activity actually was. No new field is needed — the Comments column already serves this purpose.
  • What core team sees later: in reports / dashboards, "Other" rows are easy to filter; the Comments give the qualitative context.

Master Entry Flow (Core Team IT)

Masters → Framework Task Master
Left menu (CTIT only)
→
Add New Task
Top-right button
→
Select Module → Framework
Cascading dropdowns
→
Type Task Name + Description
Both go into the master
→
Pick Phase
Internal taxonomy — not shown to Fellow
→
Save
Immediately available in fellow dropdowns

Fellow Surfaces (Where the Dropdown Appears)

SurfaceFilter applied to Task dropdownRequired fields on submit
Framework form → Log Book tab → Add Log rowTasks filtered to that framework only; sorted by Task No.Fessociate (autofill), Entrepreneur (autofill), Task (this dropdown), Comments (required if Task = "Other"), Photo (optional)
Fellow Activity Logger → Add Activity → Branch A (Entrepreneur + Framework Activity)After fellow picks Module + Framework, Task dropdown filters to that framework's tasks (incl. "Other")Date, Entrepreneur, Framework, Task, Comments (required if Task = "Other"), Photo (optional)
Permissions recap: Only Core Team IT can create / edit Framework Task Master records (see Role Matrix — Masters row). Core Team Program has view-only access. Fellows never see the master directly — they only ever see the filtered Task Name dropdown in the surfaces above.

Initial Seed Data

Initial seed comes from Final PRIME Rural Task Dropdowns Master.xlsx — 351+ tasks across 27 sheets. On data migration, each sheet maps to a framework; rows map to Framework Task Master records. The em-dash (—) phase rows in the source spreadsheet (already present in 26 of 27 sheets) are imported with Phase = "Other" and Task Name = "Other". The missing "Other" row for Logistic Service is added during migration.

Section Review:
PR-XC-005

Notifications

Core Team IT has a dedicated Notification workspace in the left menu for sending targeted notifications to field users. Recipients see push/force notification popups on login and can track all notifications in a notification module. Per User Journey 5 (Prime Rural User Journey Docs).

Notification Workspace (Core Team IT)

Left Menu
Core Team IT sees "Notifications" as a workspace in the left sidebar menu. This is where they manage and send all notifications.
Notification Log
Landing view shows a Notification Log table — history of all notifications sent in the portal with statuses and all relevant tracking fields (date, sender, recipients, message, read status).

Send Notification Form

FieldTypeLogic
DistrictSelect (multi)First filter — narrows blocks
BlockSelect (multi)Cascading from District selection
RoleSelectFilter users by role (Fessociate, Designer, SELCO, DC, etc.)
UsersMulti-selectFiltered by District + Block + Role. Can select individual users.
Notification MessageText areaFree-text message content. Used by Core Team IT to direct field users to complete framework activities by specific dates.
ActionsButtonsSend Notification and Cancel

Recipient Experience

Push Popup
Notifications are push and force notifications. On every login, a popup with a notification bar shows all new unread notifications. This ensures field users see pending tasks immediately.
Tracking
Recipients can access a Notification module where they see all received notifications together in one view — both read and unread, with timestamps and sender info.
Primary Use
Core Team IT sends notifications to Fessociates (field users) primarily — directing them to complete framework activities for specific entrepreneurs by target dates.

Welcome Email (User Management)

Trigger
On every new user creation, a welcome email is automatically sent with: set-password link, expected actions based on role as quick pointers, and a welcoming feel. This is part of User Management (Journey 1), not the Notification workspace.
Section Review:
PR-TC-001

Test Cases: Functional

Core business logic, calculated fields, cross-module data flows, conditional logic, and form operations. Each test follows Given / When / Then format for TDD.
Calculated Fields (28 tests) ▼
IDModuleGivenWhenThen
F-CALC-001Product VariantsVariant has L=10, B=5, H=3 (cm)Dimensions enteredVolume = 150 cm3 auto-calculated
F-CALC-002Product VariantsVariant Name="Pickle", Sub="Standard", Weight=200, Unit=gFields filledSKU = "PICKLE-STANDARD-200-G" auto-generated
F-CALC-003Raw MaterialsPrice/Unit=50, Qty/Batch=10Both fields enteredCost/Batch = 500 auto-calculated
F-CALC-004Raw Materials3 materials with Cost/Batch = 500, 300, 200All rows filledTotal Cost/Batch = 1000
F-CALC-005MachineryDays=25, Hours=8, Current Cap=10 units/hrFields enteredMonthly Output = 2000
F-CALC-006MachineryCurrent Cap=10, Max Cap=20Both enteredCapacity Utilisation = 50%, Capacity Gap = 10 units/hr
F-CALC-007MachineryCost=50000, Condition=Good (10%), Monthly Output=2000Fields filledMonthly Dep = 416.67, Dep/Unit = 0.21
F-CALC-008MachineryCondition changed from Good to PoorDropdown changedDep Rate changes 10%→30%, all downstream recalculates
F-CALC-009Human ResourceWage Type=Per Day, Rate=500, Days=25, Workers=3Fields filledMonthly Cost = 37,500
F-CALC-010Human ResourceWage Type=Per Hour, Rate=60, Days=25, Hours=8Fields filledLabour Rate/hr = 60, Monthly/worker = 12,000
F-CALC-011Human ResourceSkill=Unskilled, Daily equiv=300GoM check runsGoM Compliant = No (min 360). Warning shown.
F-CALC-012Human ResourceSkill=Skilled, Daily equiv=500GoM check runsGoM Compliant = Yes (min 480)
F-CALC-013Unit PricingRM sub=100, HR sub=50, Pkg sub=30, Transport=20, Machine dep=10, Gas=5, Rent/unit=2, Elec/unit=1All sections filled, Units Produced=100Total Input=215, Per-unit overhead=3, Cost/Unit = 215/100 + 3 = 5.15
F-CALC-014Unit PricingCost/Unit=100, Markup=40%, Method=Cost PlusMarkup enteredSelling Price = 140, Retail = 140 x (1+Retailer Margin%)
F-CALC-015Unit PricingCompetitor Avg=120, Positioning=Above Market (+10%)Competitor method selectedSelling Price = 132
F-CALC-016Infrastructure BERent=2000/month, Units=500/monthBoth enteredRent/Unit = 4.00
F-CALC-017Infrastructure CEElectricity=1500/month, Units=500/month (synced from BE)Electricity enteredElectricity/Unit = 3.00
F-CALC-018Infrastructure BEHill=Khasi, Typology=PPGI Sheets, SQM=50Fields selectedPlinth Rate=25,701, Estimated Cost=12,85,050
F-CALC-019Infrastructure BESQM field set to 50SQM enteredSQF auto = 538.2 (50 x 10.7639)
F-CALC-020Infrastructure FMTotal Cost=8,00,000 in PRIME SELCO modeCost calculatedSlab B auto-selected. SELCO=2,40,000 (30%), MBMA=3,20,000 (40%), LIFCOM=2,00,000 (25%), User=40,000 (5%)
F-CALC-021Infrastructure FMLIFCOM Amount=2,00,000EMI section renders5-Year EMI = 3,333/month, 3-Year EMI = 5,556/month
F-CALC-022Market LinkagePrice=100, Avg Units/Month=50Both enteredAvg Revenue/Month = 5,000. Annual Revenue = 60,000
F-CALC-023Market Linkage SalesPrice=100, Jan=10, Feb=20, Mar=30, rest=0Monthly qty enteredTotal Units = 60, Annual Revenue = 6,000
F-CALC-024Quality Control15 of 23 questions rated, sum=55Ratings enteredPractices Rated = 15/23, Average = 3.67, Tier = Established
F-CALC-025PackagingCost/Unit=5, Qty/Batch=200Both enteredTotal Cost = 1,000
F-CALC-026Capacity BuildingBaseline=2, Current=3, Target=5Scorecard filledGap = 2 (Target - Current). Color = amber.
F-CALC-027Logistics ServiceLeg costs: 200+300+150, Frequency=Weekly(x4)Route filledTrip Cost=650, Monthly Cost=2,600
F-CALC-028Schemes & FundingLoan=1,00,000, Term=36 monthsLoan details filledEMI/Month = 2,778
Cross-Module Data Flow (14 tests) ▼
IDFlowGivenWhenThen
F-FLOW-001Product → Raw MaterialsProduct has ingredients: Bamboo, Salt, OilRaw Material form opened for that entrepreneurExisting tab auto-populates 3 rows with material names
F-FLOW-002Product → MachineryProcess Flow has Machine A at step 3Machinery form openedExisting tab shows Machine A card with Process Step = 3
F-FLOW-003Raw Materials → Unit PricingTotal Cost/Batch = 1500 in RM frameworkUnit Pricing form openedSection I auto-fills with RM data, shows "Fetched from Raw Material" hint
F-FLOW-004Machinery → Infrastructure EE2 machines flagged as Required in MachineryInfrastructure EE tab opened2 machine cards auto-populated with name, qty, process step
F-FLOW-005Infrastructure BE → Unit PricingRent/Unit = 4.00 in BE tabUnit Pricing Section VII openedRent per month auto-fetched, Rent/Unit shows 4.00
F-FLOW-006Infrastructure CE → Unit PricingElectricity/Unit = 3.00 in CE tabUnit Pricing Section VII openedElectricity/Unit shows 3.00
F-FLOW-007Market Research → Unit PricingLocal offline avg = 80 in Competitive PricingUnit Pricing Competitor method, Scope=Local, Channel=OfflineCompetitor Avg auto-fills with 80
F-FLOW-008Unit Pricing → Product VariantsSelling Price = 150 calculated in Unit PricingProduct Variants tab viewedRetail Price column shows 150 with "Fetched from Pricing" hint
F-FLOW-009Product Variants → LabelsProduct has 3 variantsLabels Required tab openedVariant Content table has 3 rows pre-populated
F-FLOW-010Product Variants → PackagingProduct has 3 variantsPackaging Existing tab opened3 variant rows in packaging detail table

F-FLOW-012Activity Logger → EP ProfileFramework activity loggedActivity submittedEntry appears in EP Profile Intervention Logs (read-only)
F-FLOW-013Village Survey → Landscape MapVillage survey saved with geo location lat=25.5, lng=91.8Landscape map loadedVillage pin appears at coordinates

Conditional Logic & Branching (18 tests) ▼
IDModuleGivenWhenThen
F-COND-001Product CapacityForm displayedAgri By-Product = YesCrop, Land, Area, Season fields visible
F-COND-002Product CapacityForm displayedAgri By-Product = NoAgri fields hidden/greyed
F-COND-003Product CapacitySeasonal? = NoChanged to YesSeason month grid appears
F-COND-004RegistrationLifetime Validity uncheckedCheckedValid Till field hidden, value cleared
F-COND-005RegistrationIntervention toggle OFFToggled ONType + Justification shown. Bridged entry created in Required.
F-COND-006RegistrationBridged entry exists in RequiredExisting intervention toggled OFFRequired bridged entry removed
F-COND-007Product TestingConsent Letter Signed = checkedUnchecked"Reason for no consent" textarea shown
F-COND-008StandardizationIs standardisation followed = YesChanged to No"Reasons" textarea shown
F-COND-009PrototypingType = PackagingSelectedType Name shows Packing Master options (12 items)
F-COND-010PrototypingType = ProductSelectedType Name shows Business Basket Master options (11 items)
F-COND-011LabelsAlcohol toggle OFFToggled ONWarning banner + ABV field shown
F-COND-012Market ResearchHave you used product = NoChanged to YesPurchase Frequency dropdown shown
F-COND-013Infrastructure BETypology = KiosksSelectedPlinth Rate + SQM fields hidden. Manual cost entry shown.
F-COND-014Infrastructure FMProject = PRIME SELCOSelectedSlab cards + EMI shown. Percentages read-only.
F-COND-015Infrastructure FMProject = InnovationSelectedSlabs + EMI hidden. Editable % inputs + Others field shown.
F-COND-016Activity LoggerType = EntrepreneurSelectedSub-type field shown (Framework / Non-Framework)
F-COND-017Activity LoggerType = Non-EntrepreneurSelectedActivity Details field shown. No entrepreneur/framework fields.
F-COND-018Capacity BuildingSkill Type = Soft SkillSelectedSkill Area shows only 8 soft skill options. Technical hidden.
Intervention Bridging (8 tests) ▼
IDGivenWhenThen
F-BRIDGE-001RM Existing: Bamboo row, intervention OFFToggle ON + justification enteredRequired tab gets new bridged row for Bamboo. Registration locked. Justification synced.
F-BRIDGE-002Bridged row exists in RequiredUser tries to edit registration name on bridged rowSelect is disabled. "Bridged from Existing" marker visible.
F-BRIDGE-003Bridged row exists in RequiredExisting intervention toggled OFFRequired bridged row removed. No orphan data.
F-BRIDGE-004Required bridged row existsRequired row deleted manuallyExisting toggle auto-unchecked. No orphan.
F-BRIDGE-005Existing justification = "Quality issue"Justification edited to "Cost issue"Required row justification syncs live.
F-BRIDGE-006Machinery: Service flag ON + Capacity flag ONBoth toggledTwo separate entries in Required tab with correct flag types.
F-BRIDGE-007Digital Literacy: Skill flagged for interventionToggle ONRequired tab gets bridged row with skill name locked + justification synced.
F-BRIDGE-008Capacity Building: Re-training flaggedToggle ONRequired Section I gets bridged training with read-only header from Past Trainings.
Section Review:
PR-TC-002

Test Cases: Non-Functional

Performance, scalability, accessibility, security, and offline capability.
IDCategoryTestExpected
NF-PERF-001PerformanceLoad Entrepreneurial Landscape Map with 500+ entrepreneur pinsMap renders within 3 seconds. Pin clustering active at zoom-out.
NF-PERF-002PerformanceOpen Unit Pricing form with 4 products, each having 8 cost sectionsForm loads within 2 seconds. Auto-calcs update within 200ms.
NF-PERF-003PerformanceMonthly Production Tracker with 12 months x 5 variantsAll cells editable. Revenue calcs update live per keystroke.
NF-PERF-004PerformanceMarket Research with 10 channel entries + 15 brand entries + 20 customer entriesRepeater sections expand/collapse within 100ms. No jank.
NF-PERF-005PerformanceIntervention Dashboard with 200 entrepreneurs, all frameworksKPIs + charts render within 3 seconds. Filter changes update within 1 second.
NF-SEC-001SecurityFessociate attempts to create entrepreneur in another blockBlocked. District/Block auto-filled from profile. Cannot override.
NF-SEC-002SecurityDesigner attempts to access Entrepreneur workspaceWorkspace not visible in sidebar. Direct URL returns 403.
NF-SEC-003SecurityDistrict Coordinator attempts to edit a formAll edit controls disabled. Save button hidden. Read-only enforced.
NF-SEC-004SecurityUnauthenticated user accesses /app/enterprenur-profileRedirected to /login
NF-SEC-005SecurityAPI call without valid tokenReturns 401 or 403. No data exposed.
NF-ACC-001AccessibilityNavigate entire form using keyboard only (Tab/Enter/Esc)All fields reachable. Focus ring visible (terracotta). Accordions toggle on Enter.
NF-ACC-002AccessibilityScreen reader reads star rating componentAnnounces "Rating: 3 out of 5 stars" for QC/Digital Literacy forms.
NF-SCALE-001ScalabilitySystem has 80 concurrent users (all fellows online)No API timeout. Response times under 3 seconds.
NF-OFFLINE-001Offline (mobile)Flutter app: entrepreneur form filled offlineData saved locally. Syncs on reconnection without data loss.

Load & Performance Testing

Capacity targets: 800+ EP profiles now, 1200+ in 3 months, 2000+ in 1 year. 200 total users, 60 daily concurrent now, scaling to 150 peak concurrent in 1 year.
IDScenarioLoadExpected
NF-LOAD-001Baseline: normal day60 concurrent users, 800 EP profiles in DBAll pages load < 3s. API responses < 1s. No errors.
NF-LOAD-002Peak: all fellows online80 concurrent users, 800 EPsPage load < 4s. No 502/504 errors. DB connections stable.
NF-LOAD-0033-month projection80 concurrent, 1200 EPs, 200 total usersMap renders < 5s with 1200 pins. List views paginate correctly. Filters responsive.
NF-LOAD-0041-year projection150 concurrent, 2000 EPs, 300 total usersSystem stays responsive. No memory leaks. DB query times < 500ms.
NF-LOAD-005Stress test: breaking point200 concurrent users, sustained 30 minIdentify breaking point. Document max concurrent before degradation.
NF-LOAD-006Landscape Map heavy load2000 EP pins + 500 village pins on mapMap usable. Clustering works. Zoom/pan smooth. Filter changes < 2s.
NF-LOAD-007Dashboard data aggregationIntervention Dashboard with 2000 EPs x 27 frameworksKPIs calculate < 3s. Charts render < 2s. No browser freeze.
NF-LOAD-008Concurrent form saves30 users saving different EP profiles simultaneouslyAll saves succeed. No deadlocks. Optimistic locking works.
NF-LOAD-009File upload under load10 simultaneous file uploads (5MB each)All complete. No timeout. Upload progress shown.
NF-LOAD-010Database growth2000 EPs x avg 15 framework entries each = 30,000 recordsIndex analysis shows no full table scans. Query plans optimised.

VAPT (Vulnerability Assessment & Penetration Testing)

Scope: OWASP Top 10 focus. Internal VAPT using ZAP + Burp Suite. Run before go-live + annually during AMC. PII data (Aadhaar, bank details, phone, geo-locations) makes this mandatory even under AMC.
IDOWASP CategoryTestExpected
NF-VAPT-001A01: Broken AccessFessociate manipulates request to access another block's EP dataServer rejects. 403 returned. Permission check server-side, not just UI.
NF-VAPT-002A01: Broken AccessDesigner accesses non-Marketing Tools framework via direct API callServer rejects. Role-based access enforced at API level.
NF-VAPT-003A01: Broken AccessDistrict Coordinator attempts write operation via APIServer rejects. Read-only enforced server-side.
NF-VAPT-004A02: Crypto FailuresCheck passwords stored in DBHashed (bcrypt/pbkdf2). No plain text. No reversible encryption.
NF-VAPT-005A02: Crypto FailuresCheck HTTPS enforcementHTTP redirects to HTTPS. HSTS header present. No mixed content.
NF-VAPT-006A03: InjectionSQL injection in all search/filter fieldsFrappe ORM parameterises. No raw SQL exposed. ZAP scan clean.
NF-VAPT-007A03: InjectionXSS in all text fields (stored + reflected)All output escaped. CSP headers set. No script execution.
NF-VAPT-008A04: Insecure DesignEnumerate user emails via login error messages"Invalid credentials" generic message. No "email not found" vs "wrong password" distinction.
NF-VAPT-009A05: Security MisconfigCheck default Frappe admin credentialsDefault admin password changed. No test accounts in production.
NF-VAPT-010A05: Security MisconfigCheck error pages for stack tracesProduction mode: no stack traces. Generic error page shown.
NF-VAPT-011A07: Auth FailuresBrute force login (100 attempts, wrong password)Account locked after 5 failed attempts. CAPTCHA or cooldown activated.
NF-VAPT-012A07: Auth FailuresSession fixation attackSession ID regenerated on login. Old session ID invalid.
NF-VAPT-013A08: Software IntegrityCheck Frappe/Vue dependency versions for known CVEsNo critical CVEs in dependencies. All packages up to date.
NF-VAPT-014A09: LoggingVerify failed login attempts are loggedAll auth failures logged with timestamp, IP, email. Accessible to admin.
NF-VAPT-015A10: SSRFTest URL fields (SOP URL, Video Link) for SSRFNo server-side fetch of user-supplied URLs. Links rendered client-side only.

Role-Wise End-to-End Journey Tests

Each role tested login-to-logout: Every navigation path, every form, every action the role can perform. These are the master E2E tests for TDD.
IDRoleJourney StepActionExpected
Fessociate (Field User) — 25-step E2E Journey
E2E-FES-001Fessociate1. LoginEnter email + password from welcome emailRedirected to Entrepreneurial Landscape Map. Sidebar shows correct menu items.
E2E-FES-002Fessociate2. Landscape MapView map, apply District filterPins filter. Only entrepreneurs in selected district shown.
E2E-FES-003Fessociate3. Add EntrepreneurNavigate to EP List, click Add, fill all required fieldsEP saved with Status=MET. District/Block auto-filled from user profile. Name = EP-.{name}-.#####
E2E-FES-004Fessociate4. Verify block restrictionTry changing Block to a different blockBlocked. Cannot create EP outside assigned block.
E2E-FES-005Fessociate5. Add ProductNavigate to Products, add product for EP. Fill Details, Variants (2), Process Flow (3 steps)Product saved. Photo card appears on EP Profile Dashboard. SKU auto-generated for variants.
E2E-FES-006Fessociate6. Fill Raw MaterialsOpen RM framework for EP. Verify ingredients auto-populated. Add costs.Ingredients from Product appear as rows. Cost/Batch calculates. Total sums correctly.
E2E-FES-007Fessociate7. Flag RM interventionToggle intervention ON for one material, add justificationRequired tab gets bridged row. Justification synced. Banner shows flag.
E2E-FES-008Fessociate8. Fill MachineryOpen Machinery. Verify machines from Process Flow. Add operational data.Monthly Output, Capacity Util, Dep/Unit all auto-calculate.
E2E-FES-009Fessociate9. Fill Unit PricingOpen Unit Pricing. Verify auto-fetched costs. Set markup.RM cost, rent/unit, elec/unit all auto-fetched with "Fetched from" hints. Selling Price calculates.
E2E-FES-010Fessociate10. Verify price feedbackGo back to Product VariantsRetail Price column now shows the selling price from Unit Pricing.
E2E-FES-011Fessociate11. Fill Market ResearchAdd channel research, competitor pricingCompetitor avg auto-calculates. SWOT editable.
E2E-FES-012Fessociate12. Fill RegistrationAdd FSSAI license with Lifetime Validity checkedValid Till field hidden. Status chip shows "Received" in green.
E2E-FES-013Fessociate13. Fill Quality ControlRate 23 practices using starsAverage calculates. Maturity tier shows. Summary tab populates after save.
E2E-FES-014Fessociate14. Fill InfrastructureEnter rent, units, electricity. Flag BE intervention.Rent/unit and Elec/unit auto-calc. Values appear in Unit Pricing on refresh.
E2E-FES-015Fessociate15. Log ActivityOpen Fellow Activity Logger. Add Framework activity for EP + RM framework.Calendar tile appears. Entry syncs to RM Log Book. Entry shows in EP Intervention Logs.
E2E-FES-016Fessociate16. Verify completion trackingCheck EP Profile Dashboard for RM frameworkShows "Completed" (was "Not Started"). If logged again, shows "Completed x2".
E2E-FES-017Fessociate17. Add IntroductionOpen EP Networking tab. Add introduction with mentor.Mentor preview panel shows. Introduction card appears with "Not yet met" status.
E2E-FES-018Fessociate18. Log MeetingLog meeting under the introductionTimeline entry appears. Status changes. Meeting count increments.
E2E-FES-019Fessociate19. Village SurveyFill village survey with geo-locationSurvey saved. Village pin appears on Landscape Map.
E2E-FES-020Fessociate20. Research NotesAdd research note linked to EPNote saved with auto-filled Fessociate, District, Block.
E2E-FES-021Fessociate21. Check Intervention DashboardView dashboard, filter by own blockKPIs reflect data entered. Charts show correct counts.
E2E-FES-022Fessociate22. Check NotificationsClick bell icon in sidebarNotifications for intervention flags, framework saves visible. Click navigates to source.
E2E-FES-023Fessociate23. View other block dataNavigate to EP List, view entrepreneur from another blockCan VIEW (read) but edit controls are disabled for other block's EPs.
E2E-FES-024Fessociate24. Verify hidden menusCheck sidebar for User Management, MastersNot visible in sidebar. Direct URL returns 403.
E2E-FES-025Fessociate25. LogoutLogout from portalSession cleared. Redirected to login page. Back button shows login, not cached page.
Core Team IT — 12-step E2E Journey
E2E-CIT-001Core IT1. LoginLogin with Core IT credentialsFull sidebar visible (all 13 menu items). Lands on Landscape Map.
E2E-CIT-002Core IT2. Create UserUser Management → Add User (Fessociate role, assign block)User created. Welcome email sent. User appears in list.
E2E-CIT-003Core IT3. Manage MastersAdd new Sector, Business Basket, Block entriesMasters saved. Appear in dropdowns across all forms.
E2E-CIT-004Core IT4. Selection & EvaluationOpen EP, Selection tab, rate 10 parameters, set Final Selection = IncubatedAvg rating calculates. Status changes to Incubated. Dashboard updates.
E2E-CIT-005Core IT5. PrioritisationSet framework priorities (e.g. RM=1, Machinery=2)EP Dashboard cards reorder by priority number.
E2E-CIT-006Core IT6. Milestone ManagementCreate Milestone Master + ActivitiesMilestone Tracker dashboard reflects new milestone. Lollipop chart updates.
E2E-CIT-007Core IT7. Approve interventionFill "Approved by Core Team" on Product Testing RequiredCheckbox saved. Notification sent to Fessociate.
E2E-CIT-008Core IT8. View all dashboardsNavigate through all 5 dashboardsAll render correctly with data. Filters work. Drill-downs navigate.
E2E-CIT-009Core IT9. Edit any EP across blocksEdit an EP from a different block than ownFull edit access. No block restriction for Core Team.
E2E-CIT-010Core IT10. Export dataExport entrepreneur list / framework dataCSV/Excel export works. All fields included. Indian number formatting.
E2E-CIT-011Core IT11. Delete userDelete a test user from User ManagementUser removed. Cannot login. Linked data intact (no cascade delete of data).
E2E-CIT-012Core IT12. LogoutLogoutSession cleared. Clean redirect to login.
Designer — 8-step E2E Journey
E2E-DES-001Designer1. LoginLogin with Designer credentialsSidebar shows ONLY: Diagnostics → Marketing Tools. No other workspaces.
E2E-DES-002Designer2. Navigate to LabelsDiagnostics → Marketing Tools → LabelsList view shows forms assigned to this designer.
E2E-DES-003Designer3. Upload Draft 1Open label form, upload Draft 1 for a variantFile uploads. Status = "Pending". Notification sent to Core Team.
E2E-DES-004Designer4. Upload Logo DraftOpen Brand Identity → Logo tab, upload Draft 1File uploads. Dual approval section shows Pending for both Entrepreneur + Core Team.
E2E-DES-005Designer5. Verify restricted accessTry to access Entrepreneur workspace via URL403 or redirect. No data exposed.
E2E-DES-006Designer6. Check notificationsView notifications for approval status changes"Draft Approved" or "Draft Redo" notifications visible. Click navigates to form.
E2E-DES-007Designer7. Upload revised draftUpload Draft 2 after "Redo" feedbackDraft 2 uploaded. Status resets to Pending.
E2E-DES-008Designer8. LogoutLogoutSession cleared.
SELCO Executive — 6-step E2E Journey
E2E-SEL-001SELCO1. LoginLogin with SELCO credentialsSidebar shows ONLY: Infrastructure → EE Machinery + Notifications.
E2E-SEL-002SELCO2. View machinesOpen EE tab, see auto-populated machines from Machinery moduleMachine cards show name, qty, process step (read-only fetched data).
E2E-SEL-003SELCO3. Update pricingEnter SELCO Price for each machinePrice saved. Total EE Cost recalculates. Financial Model updates.
E2E-SEL-004SELCO4. Approve solarisationCheck "Approved by SELCO" on CE tab machinesCheckbox saved. Notification sent to Core Team.
E2E-SEL-005SELCO5. Upload quotationUpload quotation in EE Quotation LogFile uploaded. Log entry created with date.
E2E-SEL-006SELCO6. LogoutLogoutSession cleared.
District Coordinator — 6-step E2E Journey
E2E-DC-001DC1. LoginLogin with DC credentialsSidebar shows view-only workspaces. No User Management, Masters, Products, Activity Logger.
E2E-DC-002DC2. View dashboardsNavigate Landscape Map, Intervention Dashboard, Milestone TrackerAll data visible. Charts render. Filters work.
E2E-DC-003DC3. View EP ProfileOpen an entrepreneur profileAll tabs visible but ALL edit controls disabled. No Save button.
E2E-DC-004DC4. View framework formOpen any diagnostic framework formAll data visible. Star ratings show but not clickable. Toggles disabled.
E2E-DC-005DC5. Attempt edit via APIUse browser devtools to send save requestServer returns 403. No data modified.
E2E-DC-006DC6. LogoutLogoutSession cleared.
Core Team Program — 8-step E2E Journey
E2E-CTP-001Core Prog1. LoginLogin with Core Program credentialsFull sidebar minus delete in User Mgmt, minus create in Masters.
E2E-CTP-002Core Prog2. Review interventionOpen flagged framework, review intervention detailsCan view justification, set "Approved by Core Team" checkbox.
E2E-CTP-003Core Prog3. Review design draftsOpen Label form, review Draft 1, set status to "Approved"Status chip updates. Notification sent to Designer.
E2E-CTP-004Core Prog4. Set EP selectionSelection tab: rate params, set Rural E-Champ score, Final Selection = SupportedAll fields save. EP status changes to Supported.
E2E-CTP-005Core Prog5. Add user (no delete)Create a new Fessociate userUser created successfully. Welcome email sent.
E2E-CTP-006Core Prog6. Attempt delete userTry to delete a userDelete button hidden or disabled. Action blocked.
E2E-CTP-007Core Prog7. Attempt create masterTry to create new Sector in MastersCreate button hidden or disabled. Can only edit existing.
E2E-CTP-008Core Prog8. LogoutLogoutSession cleared.
Section Review:
PR-TC-003

Test Cases: UI & UX

Visual rendering, responsive behaviour, component states, design system compliance.
IDCategoryTestExpected
UI-DS-001Design SystemVerify all card backgroundsCards use cream grades (cream-deep, offwhite), NEVER white. White only for input fields.
UI-DS-002Design SystemVerify all fontsGeorgia serif only. No sans-serif anywhere. Numerals use same Georgia face.
UI-DS-003Design SystemVerify status chip coloursPending=cream, In Progress=terracotta-sft, Completed=success-sft, Not Feasible=danger-sft
UI-DS-004Design SystemVerify no green or yellow usedSuccess = teal (#0E4D4E). Warning = terracotta (#C26849). No #00FF00 or #FFFF00.
UI-CALC-001Calculated FieldsVerify visual distinction of calculated fieldsItalic text + cream-deep background. Formula tooltip visible on hover.
UI-FETCH-001Fetched FieldsVerify "Fetched from [X]" hint on auto-filled fieldsHint text visible. Clickable redirect link to source form.
UI-RESP-001ResponsiveFramework form at 768px widthFields stack vertically. Tables scroll horizontally. No content clipping.
UI-RESP-002ResponsiveDashboard at 1024px widthKPI cards reflow to 2-column. Charts resize.
UI-STATE-001StatesEmpty state: Summary tab before first saveShows "Save the form first to generate summary" message. No broken charts.
UI-STATE-002StatesLoading state: API fetch in progressSkeleton loader shown (per design system). No flash of empty content.
UI-STATE-003StatesError state: API returns error on form saveError message in terracotta callout. Form data preserved. No data loss.
UI-DND-001Drag & DropReorder Process Flow steps by draggingStep numbers re-calculate. Dragging card has reduced opacity. Drop zone highlights.
UI-STAR-001Star RatingClick 3rd star in QC assessmentStars 1-3 filled. Hover on star 4 highlights 1-4 preview. Clear button resets to 0.
UI-CHIP-001Multi-select ChipsSelect 3 values in Customer Analysis chip picker3 chips shown with teal background. Click chip to deselect. Count updates.
UI-ACCORDION-001AccordionsClick collapsible card header in Raw MaterialsSmooth expand/collapse. Chevron rotates. Only one card open at a time (or all, per design).
UI-UPLOAD-001File UploadUpload a .pdf file in Registration Scanned CopyFile pill appears with filename. View button opens in new tab. Remove button clears.
UI-NAV-001NavigationClick product photo card on EP Profile DashboardNavigates to Product Profile form for that product.
UI-NAV-002NavigationClick notification bell, then click a notificationNavigates to the relevant record (EP Profile, Framework form, etc.).
Section Review:
PR-TC-004

Test Cases: API

Endpoint validation, authentication, error handling, data integrity on API calls.
IDEndpoint / PatternTestExpected
API-AUTH-001Any frappe.callCall without session cookieReturns login redirect HTML (not JSON). Two-layer strategy handles this.
API-AUTH-002Any frappe.callCall with expired tokenReturns 401. UI shows "Session expired, please login" message.
API-AUTH-003Any frappe.callCall with valid token but insufficient roleReturns 403. No data leaked.
API-CRUD-001frappe.client.saveSave EP Profile with all required fields filledReturns 200 with saved doc. Name = EP-[Name]-#####.
API-CRUD-002frappe.client.saveSave EP Profile with missing required field (name)Returns 417 with "Entrepreneur Name is required" message.
API-CRUD-003frappe.client.deleteDelete EP Profile that has linked framework entriesReturns error or cascading delete per Frappe config. No orphan framework records.
API-CRUD-004frappe.client.get_listFessociate fetches entrepreneur listReturns all entrepreneurs (read across blocks). Correct fields returned.
API-CRUD-005frappe.client.get_listFessociate fetches with filters: district + blockReturns filtered results. SQL injection in filter values blocked by ORM.
API-PERM-001frappe.client.saveFessociate saves EP in another blockBlocked by permission rule. Returns 403.
API-PERM-002frappe.client.saveDesigner saves a non-Marketing Tools frameworkBlocked. Returns 403.
API-PERM-003frappe.client.saveDistrict Coordinator attempts save on any formBlocked. Returns 403.
API-FILE-001File upload APIUpload .exe file in attachment fieldRejected. Only allowed types (.pdf, .jpg, .png, .doc) accepted.
API-FILE-002File upload APIUpload file > 10MBRejected with size limit error.
API-RATE-001Any endpoint100 rapid-fire requests from same sessionRate limiting kicks in. Returns 429 after threshold.
Section Review:
PR-TC-005

Test Cases: Database

Data integrity, foreign key constraints, cascade deletes, migration safety.
IDCategoryTestExpected
DB-FK-001Foreign KeyCreate RM entry referencing non-existent EP ProfileBlocked by FK constraint. Error returned.
DB-FK-002Foreign KeyCreate Machinery entry referencing non-existent ProductBlocked by FK constraint.
DB-CASCADE-001CascadeDelete a Product that has RM, Machinery, Packaging entriesAll child entries either cascade-deleted or blocked with "has linked records" error.
DB-CASCADE-002CascadeDelete an Entrepreneur Profile with 5 framework entriesConsistent behaviour (cascade or block). No orphaned framework records.
DB-CHILD-001Child TableSave Product Variant child table with 3 rowsAll 3 rows saved with correct parent, parenttype, parentfield, idx.
DB-CHILD-002Child TableDelete middle row of 3-row child table, saveRemaining 2 rows re-indexed (idx 1, 2). No gaps.
DB-UNIQUE-001UniquenessCreate two EP Profiles with same entrepreneur nameNaming rule EP-.{name}-.##### ensures unique. No duplicate names.
DB-UNIQUE-002UniquenessCreate two users with same emailBlocked. "Email already registered" error.
DB-NULL-001Null SafetySave form with required field blankFrappe mandatory check blocks save. Clear error message.
DB-TYPE-001Data TypeEnter text "abc" in a Currency field (e.g. Cost/Unit)Field rejects non-numeric input or saves as 0.
DB-MIGRATE-001MigrationRun bench migrate after adding new field to doctypeNew column added to table. Existing data preserved. No data loss.
DB-CONCUR-001ConcurrencyTwo users edit same EP Profile simultaneouslySecond save shows "document has been modified" conflict. No silent overwrite.
Section Review:
PR-TC-006

Test Cases: Standard Validations

Field-level validations that apply universally. Every input field must enforce these rules. These generate the most test cases in the system.
IDRuleFields AffectedTestExpected Error Message
SV-DATE-001DOB / dates must not be in futureAll date fields: Survey Date, Issued Date, Sample Deposit Date, Training Date, Log Date, Activity DateEnter tomorrow's date"Date cannot be in the future"
SV-DATE-002End date ≥ Start dateValid Till > Issued Date, Test Results ≥ Sample Deposit, Training End ≥ Start, Loan End ≥ StartEnter end date before start date"[End field] must be after [Start field]"
SV-DATE-003Date chain: Office ≤ Lab ≤ ResultsProduct Testing: Sample to Office, to Lab, ResultsEnter Lab date before Office date"Lab deposit date must be after office deposit date"
SV-EMAIL-001Valid email formatUser email, EP email, Contact emailEnter "not-an-email""Please enter a valid email address"
SV-EMAIL-002Unique email for usersUser Management emailEnter existing user's email"Email already registered"
SV-PHONE-001Valid phone formatEP Phone, Mentor Phone, Contact fieldsEnter "123""Please enter a valid phone number"
SV-PHONE-002Default +91- prefixEP Phone NumberNew form loadedPhone field pre-filled with "+91-"
SV-NUM-001Currency fields ≥ 0All Cost, Price, Amount, Wage, Rent, Electricity fieldsEnter -100"Value must be 0 or greater"
SV-NUM-002Quantity fields > 0Qty/Batch, No. of Workers, No. of Machinery, Units ProducedEnter 0"Value must be greater than 0"
SV-NUM-003Percentage 0-100Margin %, CAGR %, Funding split %, Loan Contribution %Enter 150"Percentage must be between 0 and 100"
SV-NUM-004Working days 1-31HR Working Days/Month, Machinery Days/MonthEnter 32"Working days must be between 1 and 31"
SV-NUM-005Hours 1-24HR Hours/Day, Machinery Hours/DayEnter 25"Hours must be between 1 and 24"
SV-SUM-001Funding percentages sum to 100%Infrastructure FM: SELCO + MBMA + LIFCOM + User + OthersPercentages sum to 95%"Funding percentages must sum to 100%" (amber warn strip)
SV-REQ-001Required fields on saveAll fields marked Required in field spec tablesSave with empty required field"[Field Name] is required"
SV-REQ-002Conditional requiredJustification when intervention ON, Block when role=Fessociate, Crop when Agri=YesToggle ON but leave justification empty, then save"Justification is required when intervention is flagged"
SV-URL-001Valid URL formatSOP URL, Pitch Video Link, LinkedInEnter "not-a-url""Please enter a valid URL (starting with http:// or https://)"
SV-FILE-001Accepted file typesAll Attach fieldsUpload .exe file"File type not allowed. Accepted: .pdf, .jpg, .jpeg, .png"
SV-FILE-002File size limitAll Attach fieldsUpload 15MB file"File size exceeds maximum limit of 10MB"
SV-DASH-001Dashboard data reconciliationAll dashboard KPI cardsCompare KPI count with direct DB query (SELECT COUNT)Dashboard value = DB query result. No mismatch.
SV-DASH-002Dashboard filter accuracyAll dashboard filtersApply District=East Khasi, verify all displayed data belongs to that districtZero records from other districts shown.
SV-DASH-003Dashboard chart dataSankey, Donut, Lollipop, Bar chartsCompare chart segment values with underlying data countSum of all segments = total. No double-counting.
SV-XSS-001XSS preventionAll text/textarea fieldsEnter <script>alert('xss')</script> in Product NameScript tags escaped. No alert fires. Value saved as plain text.
SV-SQL-001SQL injection preventionAll Link/select fields, filter inputsEnter ' OR 1=1 -- in search fieldORM escapes input. No injection. Normal error or empty results.
TDD Implementation Note: These standard validations should be implemented as reusable validation helpers (e.g. validateDateNotFuture(), validatePercentRange(), validateRequiredIf()) and tested once in a shared test suite. Every form that uses these fields inherits the validation automatically.
Section Review:
PRIME Rural Living BRD v1.0 · Generated 14 May 2026 · Dhwani Rural Information Services for MBMA Meghalaya
